EXAMPLE: Aspect ratio
--------
<Box style={{
  width: 320,
  aspectRatio: 16 / 9,
  backgroundColor: '#cba6f7',
  borderRadius: 8,
}} />
--------
Platforms: all

---------
CRITICAL RULES
---------

* Root containers MUST use width: '100%', height: '100%' -- NOT flexGrow: 1
* Box nodes have zero intrinsic width and height -- always specify dimensions or use flex sizing
* Text nodes have intrinsic dimensions based on fontSize and content -- but you still MUST set fontSize
* Pre-compute grid dimensions rather than relying on child content to infer container size
* Percentage values are relative to the parent's computed dimension, not the viewport (unless the parent is the root)

---------
SEE ALSO
---------

- flexbox.txt -- Flexbox layout and flexGrow/flexShrink
- spacing.txt -- Padding and margin affect effective size
- critical-rules.txt -- Root sizing and other critical rules


========================================
Padding, Margin, and Gap
========================================

Category: Layout | Difficulty: beginner | Platforms: love2d, web, terminal, cc, nvim, hs, awesome

Spacing properties for controlling element padding, margin, and flex gap.

---------
OVERVIEW
---------

ReactJIT supports padding (inner spacing), margin (outer spacing), and gap (spacing between flex children). These work the same way as in CSS Flexbox: padding creates space inside the element's border, margin creates space outside, and gap creates uniform spacing between sibling children in a flex container.

All spacing values accept numbers (pixels on Love2D/web, character cells on grid targets) or percentage strings. Shorthand properties (padding, margin) apply to all four sides, and per-side properties (paddingLeft, marginTop, etc.) override the shorthand for specific sides.

---------
API / SYNTAX
---------

Padding properties:

  padding: number | string
    Applies uniform padding to all four sides.

  paddingLeft: number | string
  paddingRight: number | string
  paddingTop: number | string
  paddingBottom: number | string
    Per-side padding. Overrides the shorthand `padding` for the specified side.

Margin properties:

  margin: number | string
    Applies uniform margin to all four sides.

  marginLeft: number | string
  marginRight: number | string
  marginTop: number | string
  marginBottom: number | string
    Per-side margin. Overrides the shorthand `margin` for the specified side.

Gap property:

  gap: number | string
    Spacing between children along the main axis of a flex container.
    Applied between each pair of adjacent children.

---------
EXAMPLES
---------

EXAMPLE: Uniform padding
--------
<Box style={{
  width: '100%',
  height: '100%',
  padding: 24,
  backgroundColor: '#1e1e2e',
}}>
  <Text style={{ fontSize: 16, color: '#cdd6f4' }}>
    {`24px of space on all sides`}
  </Text>
</Box>
--------
Platforms: all

EXAMPLE: Per-side padding
--------
<Box style={{
  width: 300,
  height: 100,
  paddingLeft: 20,
  paddingRight: 20,
  paddingTop: 8,
  paddingBottom: 8,
  backgroundColor: '#313244',
}}>
  <Text style={{ fontSize: 14, color: '#cdd6f4' }}>Asymmetric padding</Text>
</Box>
--------
Platforms: all

EXAMPLE: Margin for spacing between siblings
--------
<Box style={{ width: '100%', height: '100%', padding: 16 }}>
  <Box style={{ height: 50, marginBottom: 12, backgroundColor: '#f38ba8' }} />
  <Box style={{ height: 50, marginBottom: 12, backgroundColor: '#a6e3a1' }} />
  <Box style={{ height: 50, backgroundColor: '#89b4fa' }} />
</Box>
--------
Platforms: all

EXAMPLE: Gap for uniform child spacing
--------
<Box style={{ width: '100%', height: '100%', padding: 16, gap: 12 }}>
  <Box style={{ height: 50, backgroundColor: '#f38ba8' }} />
  <Box style={{ height: 50, backgroundColor: '#a6e3a1' }} />
  <Box style={{ height: 50, backgroundColor: '#89b4fa' }} />
</Box>
--------
Platforms: all

EXAMPLE: Row with gap
--------
<Box style={{
  flexDirection: 'row',
  width: '100%',
  gap: 8,
  padding: 16,
}}>
  <Box style={{ width: 80, height: 80, backgroundColor: '#f38ba8' }} />
  <Box style={{ width: 80, height: 80, backgroundColor: '#a6e3a1' }} />
  <Box style={{ width: 80, height: 80, backgroundColor: '#89b4fa' }} />
</Box>
--------
Platforms: all

---------
CRITICAL RULES
---------

* Padding reduces the available content area inside an element
* Margin does not collapse in Flexbox layout (unlike CSS block layout)
* Gap is applied between children only, not before the first or after the last child
* Per-side properties (paddingLeft, marginTop, etc.) override the shorthand for that side
* Percentage spacing values are relative to the parent container's width (matching CSS behavior)

---------
SEE ALSO
---------

- flexbox.txt -- Flexbox layout and gap usage
- sizing.txt -- How sizing interacts with padding
- visual.txt -- Border width also affects content area


========================================
Text-Specific Style Properties
========================================

Category: Layout | Difficulty: beginner | Platforms: love2d, web, terminal, cc, nvim, hs, awesome

Styling properties for the Text primitive, including font, color, alignment, and decoration.

---------
OVERVIEW
---------

The Text primitive has its own set of style properties for controlling typography. The most important rule in ReactJIT is that every Text element MUST have an explicit fontSize in its style. This is not optional -- the layout engine needs fontSize to measure text and compute layout, and the linter will block builds that violate this rule.

Text styling covers font properties (size, family, weight), text layout (alignment, overflow, line height, letter spacing), text decoration (underline, strikethrough), and text shadow. Colors use the same Color type as visual styling (CSS strings or Love2D-style arrays).

---------
API / SYNTAX
---------

Text style properties:

  color: Color
    Text color. Accepts CSS strings ("#cdd6f4") or Love2D arrays ([0.8, 0.84, 0.96]).

  fontSize: number (REQUIRED)
    Font size in pixels (Love2D/web) or character scale (grid targets).
    This property is MANDATORY on every Text element. The linter enforces this with the
    'no-text-without-fontsize' rule.

  fontFamily: string
    Font family name. On Love2D, this must be a font file available in the project.
    On web, it maps to CSS font-family.

  fontWeight: 'normal' | 'bold' | number
    Font weight. 'normal' is typically 400, 'bold' is 700. Numeric values (100-900)
    are supported on web and Love2D (if the font file supports weight variants).

  textAlign: 'left' | 'center' | 'right'
    Horizontal text alignment within the Text element's bounds.
    Default: 'left'

  textOverflow: 'clip' | 'ellipsis'
    How to handle text that overflows its container.
    'clip': text is cut off at the boundary.
    'ellipsis': text is truncated with "..." at the boundary.

  textDecorationLine: 'none' | 'underline' | 'line-through'
    Text decoration. 'underline' draws a line below the text, 'line-through' draws
    a line through the middle.

  lineHeight: number
    Line height in pixels. Controls the vertical spacing between lines of text.
    On web, this is converted to CSS line-height with 'px' units.

  letterSpacing: number
    Additional horizontal space between characters, in pixels.

  textShadowColor: Color
    Color of the text shadow. If not set, no text shadow is rendered.

  textShadowOffsetX: number
    Horizontal offset of the text shadow.

  textShadowOffsetY: number
    Vertical offset of the text shadow.

---------
EXAMPLES
---------

EXAMPLE: Basic text with required fontSize
--------
<Text style={{ fontSize: 16, color: '#cdd6f4' }}>Hello, world</Text>
--------
Platforms: all

EXAMPLE: Styled heading
--------
<Text style={{
  fontSize: 32,
  fontWeight: 'bold',
  color: '#cba6f7',
  textAlign: 'center',
  letterSpacing: 2,
}}>
  Dashboard
</Text>
--------
Platforms: all

EXAMPLE: Text with decoration
--------
<Text style={{
  fontSize: 14,
  color: '#a6adc8',
  textDecorationLine: 'underline',
}}>
  Click here for details
</Text>
--------
Platforms: all

EXAMPLE: Multiline text with lineHeight
--------
<Text style={{
  fontSize: 14,
  color: '#cdd6f4',
  lineHeight: 22,
}}>
  {`This is a longer paragraph of text that demonstrates line height control. Each line has 22 pixels of vertical space.`}
</Text>
--------
Platforms: all

EXAMPLE: Text overflow with ellipsis
--------
<Box style={{ width: 200 }}>
  <Text style={{
    fontSize: 14,
    color: '#cdd6f4',
    textOverflow: 'ellipsis',
  }}>
    {`This is a very long string that will be truncated with an ellipsis`}
  </Text>
</Box>
--------
Platforms: all

EXAMPLE: Text shadow
--------
<Text style={{
  fontSize: 24,
  color: '#cdd6f4',
  textShadowColor: 'rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.5)',
  textShadowOffsetX: 2,
  textShadowOffsetY: 2,
}}>
  Shadow Text
</Text>
--------
Platforms: love2d, web

---------
CRITICAL RULES
---------

* Every Text element MUST have explicit fontSize -- builds will fail without it (linter rule: no-text-without-fontsize)
* Do NOT mix literal text and JSX expressions in Text children -- use template literals instead (linter rule: no-mixed-text-children)
* WRONG: <Text style={{ fontSize: 14 }}>Score: {score}</Text>
* RIGHT: <Text style={{ fontSize: 14 }}>{`Score: ${score}`}</Text>
* Do NOT use the block character (U+2588) in Text elements -- it renders as a font glyph, not a filled pixel (linter rule: no-block-char-in-text)
* Text nodes have intrinsic dimensions from font measurement -- they do not need explicit width/height in most cases

---------
SEE ALSO
---------

- visual.txt -- Background and border styling for Box elements
- critical-rules.txt -- All critical layout rules
- flexbox.txt -- How text nodes participate in flex layout


========================================
Transform System
========================================

Category: Layout | Difficulty: intermediate | Platforms: love2d, web, terminal, cc, nvim, hs, awesome

The transform property for translating, rotating, scaling, and skewing elements.

---------
OVERVIEW
---------

ReactJIT supports a transform object on any element's style for applying geometric transformations. Transforms affect the visual rendering of the element without changing its position in the layout tree. A transformed element still occupies the same layout space it would without the transform -- sibling elements are not affected.

The transform system supports translation, rotation, scaling, and skewing. Transform origin defaults to the center of the element (0.5, 0.5) and can be adjusted with originX and originY values ranging from 0 (top-left) to 1 (bottom-right). Transforms are commonly combined with the animation system for motion effects.

---------
API / SYNTAX
---------

Transform style property:

  transform: {
    translateX?: number     // Horizontal offset in pixels
    translateY?: number     // Vertical offset in pixels
    rotate?: number         // Rotation in degrees (clockwise)
    scaleX?: number         // Horizontal scale factor (1 = no change)
    scaleY?: number         // Vertical scale factor (1 = no change)
    skewX?: number          // Horizontal skew in degrees
    skewY?: number          // Vertical skew in degrees
    originX?: number        // Transform origin X, 0-1 (default: 0.5 = center)
    originY?: number        // Transform origin Y, 0-1 (default: 0.5 = center)
  }

All transform sub-properties are optional. Only the properties you specify are applied.

Transform origin:
  originX and originY are normalized values from 0 to 1, where:
    0, 0 = top-left corner
    0.5, 0.5 = center (default)
    1, 1 = bottom-right corner

---------
EXAMPLES
---------

EXAMPLE: Simple translation
--------
<Box style={{
  width: 100,
  height: 100,
  backgroundColor: '#89b4fa',
  transform: { translateX: 50, translateY: 20 },
}} />
--------
Platforms: all

EXAMPLE: Rotation from center
--------
<Box style={{
  width: 100,
  height: 100,
  backgroundColor: '#f38ba8',
  transform: { rotate: 45 },
}} />
--------
Platforms: all

EXAMPLE: Rotation from top-left corner
--------
<Box style={{
  width: 100,
  height: 100,
  backgroundColor: '#a6e3a1',
  transform: { rotate: 15, originX: 0, originY: 0 },
}} />
--------
Platforms: all

EXAMPLE: Scale
--------
<Box style={{
  width: 100,
  height: 100,
  backgroundColor: '#f9e2af',
  transform: { scaleX: 1.5, scaleY: 0.8 },
}} />
--------
Platforms: all

EXAMPLE: Combined transforms
--------
<Box style={{
  width: 100,
  height: 100,
  backgroundColor: '#cba6f7',
  transform: {
    translateX: 30,
    translateY: 10,
    rotate: 10,
    scaleX: 1.2,
    scaleY: 1.2,
  },
}} />
--------
Platforms: all

EXAMPLE: Animated transform with useSpring
--------
import { Box } from '@reactjit/core';
import { useSpring } from '@reactjit/core';

function PulseBox({ active }: { active: boolean }) {
  const scale = useSpring(active ? 1.1 : 1.0, { stiffness: 200, damping: 15 });
  return (
    <Box style={{
      width: 100,
      height: 100,
      backgroundColor: '#89b4fa',
      transform: { scaleX: scale, scaleY: scale },
    }} />
  );
}
--------
Platforms: all

---------
CRITICAL RULES
---------

* Transforms do NOT affect layout -- the element still occupies its original layout rectangle
* Transform origin defaults to center (0.5, 0.5), not top-left
* Rotation is specified in degrees, not radians
* Scale values of 1.0 mean no change -- 2.0 doubles the size, 0.5 halves it
* Transforms are visual-only and commonly used with the animation system for motion effects
* OriginX/originY are 0-1 normalized, not pixel values

---------
SEE ALSO
---------

- visual.txt -- Other visual styling properties
- ../07-animation/usespring.txt -- Animating transform values
- ../07-animation/useanimation.txt -- Primary animation hook


========================================
Visual Styling
========================================

Category: Layout | Difficulty: beginner | Platforms: love2d, web, terminal, cc, nvim, hs, awesome

Background colors, borders, border radius, shadows, gradients, opacity, and other visual properties.

---------
OVERVIEW
---------

ReactJIT provides a comprehensive set of visual styling properties for Box elements. These include background colors, border styling (width, color, radius with per-corner and per-side control), box shadows, linear gradients, opacity, overflow clipping, z-index stacking, outlines, and visibility toggling.

Colors can be specified as CSS strings ("#ff0000", "rgba(255, 0, 0, 0.5)") or as Love2D-style number arrays [r, g, b, a] where each component is 0-1. The Color type accepts both formats and the renderer converts as needed for each target.

---------
API / SYNTAX
---------

Color type:
  type Color = string | [number, number, number, number?]

  String format: "#ff0000", "rgba(255, 0, 0, 0.5)", "red"
  Array format: [1, 0, 0, 1] (Love2D-style, 0-1 range, alpha optional)

Background:

  backgroundColor: Color
    Fills the element background with a solid color.

  backgroundGradient: {
    direction: 'horizontal' | 'vertical' | 'diagonal',
    colors: [Color, Color]
  }
    Linear gradient background. Takes precedence over backgroundColor when set.
    'horizontal' goes left to right, 'vertical' top to bottom, 'diagonal' top-left to
    bottom-right.

Border:

  borderWidth: number
    Sets border width on all sides. Requires borderColor to be visible.

  borderColor: Color
    Color of the border. Only visible when borderWidth > 0.

  borderRadius: number
    Rounds all four corners uniformly.

  Per-side border width:
    borderTopWidth: number
    borderRightWidth: number
    borderBottomWidth: number
    borderLeftWidth: number

  Per-side border color:
    borderTopColor: Color
    borderRightColor: Color
    borderBottomColor: Color
    borderLeftColor: Color

  Per-corner border radius:
    borderTopLeftRadius: number
    borderTopRightRadius: number
    borderBottomLeftRadius: number
    borderBottomRightRadius: number

Shadow:

  shadowColor: Color
    Shadow color. If not set, no shadow is rendered.

  shadowOffsetX: number
    Horizontal shadow offset (default: 0).

  shadowOffsetY: number
    Vertical shadow offset (default: 0).

  shadowBlur: number
    Blur radius for the shadow (default: 0).

Outline:

  outlineColor: Color
    Color of the outline. Outlines render outside the element border.

  outlineWidth: number
    Width of the outline in pixels (default: 1 when outlineColor is set).

  outlineOffset: number
    Gap between the element border and the outline.

Stacking and visibility:

  opacity: number
    Element transparency, 0 (fully transparent) to 1 (fully opaque).

  overflow: 'visible' | 'hidden' | 'scroll'
    Controls whether child content that exceeds the element bounds is visible,
    clipped, or scrollable.

  zIndex: number
    Stacking order. Higher values render on top of lower values.

  visibility: 'visible' | 'hidden'
    Hides the element visually but it still occupies space in layout.
    Unlike display: 'none', hidden elements are not removed from the layout tree.

  display: 'flex' | 'none'
    Set to 'none' to completely remove from layout (no space occupied).

---------
EXAMPLES
---------

EXAMPLE: Colored box with rounded corners
--------
<Box style={{
  width: 200,
  height: 100,
  backgroundColor: '#89b4fa',
  borderRadius: 12,
}} />
--------
Platforms: all

EXAMPLE: Box with border
--------
<Box style={{
  width: 200,
  height: 100,
  borderWidth: 2,
  borderColor: '#cba6f7',
  borderRadius: 8,
}} />
--------
Platforms: all

EXAMPLE: Per-corner border radius
--------
<Box style={{
  width: 200,
  height: 100,
  backgroundColor: '#f38ba8',
  borderTopLeftRadius: 20,
  borderTopRightRadius: 20,
  borderBottomLeftRadius: 0,
  borderBottomRightRadius: 0,
}} />
--------
Platforms: all

EXAMPLE: Box shadow
--------
<Box style={{
  width: 200,
  height: 100,
  backgroundColor: '#cdd6f4',
  shadowColor: 'rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.3)',
  shadowOffsetX: 4,
  shadowOffsetY: 4,
  shadowBlur: 8,
  borderRadius: 8,
}} />
--------
Platforms: love2d, web

EXAMPLE: Linear gradient
--------
<Box style={{
  width: 300,
  height: 100,
  backgroundGradient: {
    direction: 'horizontal',
    colors: ['#f38ba8', '#89b4fa'],
  },
  borderRadius: 8,
}} />
--------
Platforms: love2d, web

EXAMPLE: Opacity and z-index
--------
<Box style={{ width: 200, height: 200, position: 'relative' }}>
  <Box style={{
    position: 'absolute',
    width: 150,
    height: 150,
    top: 0,
    left: 0,
    backgroundColor: '#f38ba8',
    zIndex: 1,
  }} />
  <Box style={{
    position: 'absolute',
    width: 150,
    height: 150,
    top: 50,
    left: 50,
    backgroundColor: '#89b4fa',
    opacity: 0.7,
    zIndex: 2,
  }} />
</Box>
--------
Platforms: all

---------
CRITICAL RULES
---------

* borderWidth alone does not produce a visible border -- you must also set borderColor
* backgroundGradient takes precedence over backgroundColor
* overflow: 'hidden' clips children that extend beyond the element bounds
* visibility: 'hidden' hides the element but it still occupies space (use display: 'none' to remove from layout)
* Shadow rendering depends on the target platform (not available on all grid targets)
* Colors can use either string format ("#ff0000") or array format ([1, 0, 0, 1])

---------
SEE ALSO
---------

- text-styling.txt -- Text-specific visual properties
- sizing.txt -- Element dimensions
- spacing.txt -- Padding and margin
- transforms.txt -- Transform system


========================================
Components Overview
========================================

Category: Components | Difficulty: beginner | Platforms: love2d, web, terminal, cc, nvim, hs, awesome

Reference index for all ReactJIT primitives and components

---------
OVERVIEW
---------

ReactJIT ships a set of universal primitives and higher-level components that work across all render targets. Every visible element on screen is built from these building blocks. Primitives are the lowest-level elements (Box, Text, Image) that map directly to host elements in each target. Components are composed from primitives and provide richer interaction patterns (Pressable, ScrollView, Modal, Slider, and others).

All primitives and components are exported from `@reactjit/core`. Grid targets (terminal, ComputerCraft, Neovim, Hammerspoon, AwesomeWM) use lowercase JSX intrinsics (`<view>`, `<text>`) and typically define local wrapper components; the `@reactjit/core` exports target Love2D and web renderers.

---------
API / SYNTAX
---------

```tsx
import {
  // Primitives
  Box, Text, Image, Video,

  // Text display
  CodeBlock,

  // Interaction
  Pressable, ScrollView, Modal, Slider,

  // Form primitives
  TextInput, TextEditor, Checkbox, Radio, RadioGroup, Select, Switch,

  // Layout helpers
  FlexRow, FlexColumn, Spacer, Card, Badge, Divider,

  // Navigation
  NavPanel, Tabs, Breadcrumbs, Toolbar,

  // Data visualization
  Table, BarChart, ProgressBar, Sparkline, FlatList,

  // Portal system
  Portal, PortalHost,
} from '@reactjit/core';
```

---------
PLATFORM NOTES
---------

Love2D:
All components render as string-typed host elements ('View', 'Text', 'Image') processed by the Lua layout engine and painter. Flexbox is computed by layout.lua. Events go through bridge_quickjs.lua.

Web:
Primitives render as DOM elements (div, span, img) with CSS flexbox. Interaction events use standard DOM event listeners. Styles are converted from the Style type to React.CSSProperties by styleToCSS().
Terminal / Grid targets:
Use lowercase intrinsics (<view>, <text>) with the grid layout engine. The @reactjit/core components are not used directly; grid targets have their own component wrappers built on @reactjit/grid.

---------
CRITICAL RULES
---------

* Always import from '@reactjit/core', never from internal package paths
* Every <Text> must have an explicit fontSize -- the linter will block your build otherwise
* Root containers need width: '100%' and height: '100%', not flexGrow: 1
* Grid targets use different primitives; do not import @reactjit/core components into grid-target code
* Run `reactjit lint` after any component change to catch layout violations early

---------
SEE ALSO
---------

- Layout System (04-layout-system)
- Hooks (06-hooks)
- Animation (07-animation)
- Style type reference (12-api-reference)


========================================
AmbientLight
========================================

Category: Components | Difficulty: intermediate | Platforms: love2d

Uniform ambient illumination that prevents shadowed areas from going fully black

---------
OVERVIEW
---------

AmbientLight provides a base level of non-directional illumination for all meshes in a Scene. It ensures shadowed surfaces receive some light instead of rendering as pure black. AmbientLight has no position or direction — it contributes equally from all angles. Only one AmbientLight per Scene is supported. It renders nothing on web targets.

---------
API / SYNTAX
---------

```tsx
import { AmbientLight } from '@reactjit/3d';
```

Props:
| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| color | string | '#1a1a2e' | Ambient light color as a hex string |
| intensity | number | 0.15 | Intensity multiplier applied to the color |

---------
EXAMPLES
---------

EXAMPLE: Default ambient light for a dark space scene
--------
```tsx
import { Scene, Camera, Mesh, AmbientLight, DirectionalLight } from '@reactjit/3d';

function SpaceScene() {
  return (
    <Scene style={{ width: 800, height: 600 }} backgroundColor="#0a0a14" stars>
      <Camera position={[0, -5, 2]} lookAt={[0, 0, 0]} />
      <AmbientLight color="#1a1a2e" intensity={0.15} />
      <DirectionalLight direction={[1, -1, 1]} color="#ffffff" intensity={1.0} />
      <Mesh geometry="sphere" texture="planet" seed={4} />
    </Scene>
  );
}
```
--------
Platforms: love2d

EXAMPLE: Warm tinted ambient light for an interior scene
--------
```tsx
import { Scene, Camera, Mesh, AmbientLight, DirectionalLight } from '@reactjit/3d';

function WarmScene() {
  return (
    <Scene style={{ width: 800, height: 600 }} backgroundColor="#1a0e00">
      <Camera position={[3, -3, 2]} lookAt={[0, 0, 0]} />
      <AmbientLight color="#3d1f00" intensity={0.4} />
      <DirectionalLight direction={[0, -1, 1]} color="#ffcc88" intensity={0.8} />
      <Mesh geometry="box" color="#c0a060" edgeColor="#2a1500" edgeWidth={0.02} />
    </Scene>
  );
}
```
--------
Platforms: love2d

EXAMPLE: High ambient for a flat, evenly lit scene
--------
```tsx
import { Scene, Camera, Mesh, AmbientLight } from '@reactjit/3d';

function FlatLitScene() {
  return (
    <Scene style={{ width: 800, height: 600 }}>
      <Camera position={[0, -4, 2]} lookAt={[0, 0, 0]} />
      <AmbientLight color="#ffffff" intensity={0.9} />
      <Mesh geometry="sphere" color="#89b4fa" />
    </Scene>
  );
}
```
--------
Platforms: love2d

---------
CRITICAL RULES
---------

* AmbientLight renders nothing on web targets and returns null — it is Love2D only
* Only one AmbientLight per Scene is supported — placing multiple will produce undefined behavior
* AmbientLight must be a direct child of Scene, not nested inside other elements
* intensity is a multiplier on the color — setting intensity to 0 effectively disables ambient lighting
* AmbientLight alone without a DirectionalLight will cause all surfaces to be shaded with only the flat ambient color, losing all depth cues

---------
SEE ALSO
---------

- Scene — the 3D viewport that AmbientLight must be placed inside
- DirectionalLight — directional sun-like light that works alongside AmbientLight for full shading
- Mesh — the geometry that receives ambient illumination
- Camera — the viewpoint used to render the scene


========================================
AreaChart
========================================

Category: Components | Difficulty: beginner | Platforms: love2d, web, terminal, cc, nvim, hs, awesome

Filled area chart; a LineChart preset with area fill enabled and dots disabled

---------
OVERVIEW
---------

AreaChart is a thin wrapper over LineChart with `showArea` forced to true, `showDots` forced to false, and `areaOpacity` defaulting to 0.4. It renders the same column-based layout as LineChart but emphasizes volume and trend through filled regions rather than point markers. All LineChart props are accepted and forwarded; any prop you pass overrides the preset defaults including re-enabling dots or disabling the area.

---------
API / SYNTAX
---------

```tsx
import { AreaChart } from '@reactjit/core';
```

AreaChart accepts exactly the same props as LineChart:

Data shape:
```tsx
interface LineChartPoint {
  x?:    string;  // Optional x-axis label shown in the hover tooltip
  value: number;  // Y-axis value; min and max across the dataset define the scale
}
```

Props (inherited from LineChart):
| Prop          | Type                                                              | Default (AreaChart) | Description                                                           |
|---------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------|---------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------|
| data          | LineChartPoint[]                                                  | required            | Array of data points to plot                                          |
| width         | number                                                            | 280                 | Total chart width in pixels                                           |
| height        | number                                                            | 120                 | Total chart height in pixels                                          |
| color         | Color                                                             | '#3b82f6'           | Color used for the area fill                                          |
| showDots      | boolean                                                           | false               | Preset to false; pass true to re-enable dots                          |
| showArea      | boolean                                                           | true                | Preset to true; pass false to produce a dotless line chart            |
| areaOpacity   | number                                                            | 0.4                 | Opacity of the area fill (0–1)                                        |
| interactive   | boolean                                                           | false               | Enable hover crosshair and per-point tooltips                         |
| onPointHover  | (index: number \| null, point: LineChartPoint \| null) => void    | undefined           | Called when the pointer enters or leaves a column                     |
| onPointPress  | (index: number, point: LineChartPoint) => void                    | undefined           | Called when a column is clicked (requires interactive: true)          |
| style         | Style                                                             | undefined           | Additional styles applied to the outer container Box                  |

---------
EXAMPLES
---------

EXAMPLE: Traffic volume over a week
--------
```tsx
import { AreaChart } from '@reactjit/core';

const traffic = [
  { x: 'Mon', value: 2400 },
  { x: 'Tue', value: 1800 },
  { x: 'Wed', value: 3200 },
  { x: 'Thu', value: 2900 },
  { x: 'Fri', value: 4100 },
  { x: 'Sat', value: 1200 },
  { x: 'Sun', value: 900  },
];

export default function App() {
  return (
    <Box style={{ width: '100%', height: '100%', padding: 20 }}>
      <AreaChart data={traffic} width={320} height={100} color="#6366f1" />
    </Box>
  );
}
```
--------
Platforms: all

EXAMPLE: Stacked feel with reduced opacity and custom color
--------
```tsx
import { AreaChart } from '@reactjit/core';

const memory = [
  { value: 1.2 }, { value: 1.8 }, { value: 2.1 },
  { value: 1.9 }, { value: 2.4 }, { value: 3.0 },
  { value: 2.7 }, { value: 3.2 }, { value: 2.9 },
];

export default function App() {
  return (
    <Box style={{ width: '100%', height: '100%', padding: 20 }}>
      <AreaChart
        data={memory}
        width={260}
        height={80}
        color="#22c55e"
        areaOpacity={0.6}
      />
    </Box>
  );
}
```
--------
Platforms: all

EXAMPLE: Interactive area chart with dots re-enabled
--------
```tsx
import { AreaChart } from '@reactjit/core';

const latency = [
  { x: '10s', value: 42 }, { x: '20s', value: 38 },
  { x: '30s', value: 55 }, { x: '40s', value: 49 },
  { x: '50s', value: 61 }, { x: '60s', value: 44 },
];

export default function App() {
  return (
    <Box style={{ width: '100%', height: '100%', padding: 20 }}>
      <AreaChart
        data={latency}
        width={300}
        height={100}
        color="#ef4444"
        showDots
        interactive
      />
    </Box>
  );
}
```
--------
Platforms: love2d, web

---------
CRITICAL RULES
---------

* AreaChart is not an independent component — it renders a LineChart internally. Every constraint that applies to LineChart applies here.
* Props you pass to AreaChart override the presets. Passing `showArea={false}` produces a dotless, area-less chart (effectively invisible). Passing `showDots={true}` adds dots back on top of the area fill.
* The vertical scale is relative to the dataset, not zero-based. A dataset with values [100, 110, 105] will show strong variation even though the values are close together.
* `onPointPress` requires `interactive={true}` to fire.
* Do not use Unicode symbols in the `x` label strings of data points.

---------
SEE ALSO
---------

- LineChart — the underlying component; use it directly when you need dots-only or full control
- Sparkline — a simpler no-configuration trend widget for embedding in text-dense layouts
- BarChart — vertical bars for discrete comparison rather than trend display
- ChartTooltip — standalone tooltip primitive used internally by the underlying LineChart


========================================
Badge
========================================

Category: Components | Difficulty: beginner | Platforms: love2d, web, terminal, cc, nvim, hs, awesome

A small colored label/pill component for displaying status, categories, tags, or counts. Supports five semantic variants with pre-defined color schemes.

---------
OVERVIEW
---------

The Badge component renders a compact, pill-shaped label with semantic color variants. It automatically sizes to fit the label text and uses `alignSelf: 'start'` to prevent unwanted stretching in flex layouts. Badges are ideal for status indicators, category tags, notification counts, or any small textual label that needs visual emphasis. The component provides five built-in variants with carefully chosen background and text color pairings optimized for readability.

---------
API / SYNTAX
---------

```tsx
import { Badge } from '@reactjit/core';

<Badge
  label="Active"
  variant="success"
  style={{ marginLeft: 8 }}
/>
```

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| `label` | `string` | (required) | Text content displayed inside the badge |
| `variant` | `BadgeVariant` | `'default'` | Color scheme: `'default'`, `'success'`, `'warning'`, `'error'`, `'info'` |
| `style` | `Style` | `{}` | Overrides for the badge container (margins, explicit dimensions, etc.) |

**Variant Color Schemes:**

| Variant | Background | Text | Use Case |
|---------|-----------|------|----------|
| `default` | `#334155` (slate-700) | `#e2e8f0` (slate-200) | Neutral labels, defaults |
| `success` | `#166534` (green-800) | `#bbf7d0` (green-200) | Active, online, completed, success |
| `warning` | `#854d0e` (yellow-800) | `#fef08a` (yellow-200) | Caution, pending, warnings |
| `error` | `#991b1b` (red-800) | `#fecaca` (red-200) | Error, failed, critical, offline |
| `info` | `#1e40af` (blue-800) | `#bfdbfe` (blue-200) | Information, tips, metadata |

**Default Styling:**
- Border radius: 10px (pill shape)
- Padding: 2px vertical, 8px horizontal
- Font size: 11px
- Font weight: bold
- Alignment: `alignSelf: 'start'` (no stretch)

---------
EXAMPLES
---------

EXAMPLE: Basic Badge
--------
```tsx
<Badge label="New" variant="info" />
```
--------
Platforms: all

EXAMPLE: Status Indicators
--------
```tsx
<Box style={{ gap: 8 }}>
  <Badge label="Online" variant="success" />
  <Badge label="Offline" variant="error" />
  <Badge label="Away" variant="warning" />
  <Badge label="Idle" variant="default" />
</Box>
```
--------
Platforms: all

EXAMPLE: Badge with Text Label (Row Layout)
--------
```tsx
<Box style={{ flexDirection: 'row', gap: 8, alignItems: 'center' }}>
  <Text fontSize={14} style={{ color: '#e2e8f0' }}>Server Status:</Text>
  <Badge label="Running" variant="success" />
</Box>
```
--------
Platforms: all

EXAMPLE: Notification Count
--------
```tsx
<Box style={{ flexDirection: 'row', gap: 8, alignItems: 'center' }}>
  <Text fontSize={14} style={{ color: '#e2e8f0' }}>Messages</Text>
  <Badge label="12" variant="error" />
</Box>
```
--------
Platforms: all

EXAMPLE: Category Tags
--------
```tsx
<Box style={{ flexDirection: 'row', gap: 8, flexWrap: 'wrap' }}>
  <Badge label="React" variant="info" />
  <Badge label="TypeScript" variant="info" />
  <Badge label="Lua" variant="info" />
  <Badge label="Love2D" variant="success" />
</Box>
```
--------
Platforms: all

EXAMPLE: Badges Inside Cards
--------
```tsx
<Card title="System Services">
  <Box style={{ gap: 8 }}>
    <Box style={{ flexDirection: 'row', gap: 8, alignItems: 'center' }}>
      <Text fontSize={12} style={{ color: '#e2e8f0' }}>Web Server</Text>
      <Badge label="Active" variant="success" />
    </Box>
    <Box style={{ flexDirection: 'row', gap: 8, alignItems: 'center' }}>
      <Text fontSize={12} style={{ color: '#e2e8f0' }}>Database</Text>
      <Badge label="Error" variant="error" />
    </Box>
    <Box style={{ flexDirection: 'row', gap: 8, alignItems: 'center' }}>
      <Text fontSize={12} style={{ color: '#e2e8f0' }}>Cache</Text>
      <Badge label="Pending" variant="warning" />
    </Box>
  </Box>
</Card>
```
--------
Platforms: all

EXAMPLE: Custom Styling
--------
```tsx
<Badge
  label="Custom"
  variant="info"
  style={{
    marginLeft: 16,
    borderWidth: 1,
    borderColor: '#60a5fa',
  }}
/>
```
--------
Platforms: all

EXAMPLE: Dynamic Variant Based on State
--------
```tsx
function StatusBadge({ status }: { status: 'online' | 'offline' | 'away' }) {
  const variantMap = {
    online: 'success',
    offline: 'error',
    away: 'warning',
  } as const;

  return <Badge label={status} variant={variantMap[status]} />;
}

// Usage:
<StatusBadge status="online" />
```
--------
Platforms: all

---------
CRITICAL RULES
---------

* Do not use Unicode symbols in the `label` prop (see layout rules)
* Badge auto-sizes to label text width (no explicit width needed)
* Uses `alignSelf: 'start'` by default to prevent stretching in flex containers
* For horizontal alignment with text, wrap in a Box with `flexDirection: 'row'` and `alignItems: 'center'`
* The internal Text component uses `fontSize: 11` and `fontWeight: 'bold'` (not customizable without forking)
* Variant colors are hardcoded; for custom colors, fork Badge or use a styled Box + Text
* Badge has no onClick handler; wrap in Pressable for interactivity

---------
SEE ALSO
---------

- Card component for containing badges in structured layouts
- Pressable for making badges interactive
- Box primitive for custom badge designs
- Text styling rules (fontSize requirement, no Unicode symbols)
- FlexRow helper for horizontal badge + text layouts


========================================
BarChart
========================================

Category: Components | Difficulty: beginner | Platforms: love2d, web, terminal, cc, nvim, hs, awesome

Vertical bar chart that renders data as proportionally sized colored bars with optional labels, values, and interactive hover tooltips

---------
OVERVIEW
---------

BarChart renders a vertical bar chart from an array of data points. Each bar's height is proportional to its value relative to the maximum value in the dataset. Bars can have individual colors or share a default color.

The component renders entirely with Box and Text primitives, so it works on all targets. An optional label row appears below the bars, and values can be shown above each bar. When barWidth is omitted, bars auto-size to fill the parent container evenly using flexGrow. When interactive mode is enabled, hovering a bar dims all other bars and reveals a ChartTooltip showing the label, raw value, and percentage of the maximum.

---------
API / SYNTAX
---------

```tsx
import { BarChart } from '@reactjit/core';
```

Data shape:
```tsx
interface BarChartBar {
  label: string;  // Bar label shown below the chart
  value: number;  // Numeric value driving bar height
  color?: Color;  // Per-bar color override; falls back to the chart-level `color` prop
}
```

Props:
| Prop        | Type                                                        | Default    | Description                                                      |
|-------------|-------------------------------------------------------------|------------|------------------------------------------------------------------|
| data        | BarChartBar[]                                               | required   | Array of bar data points                                         |
| height      | number                                                      | 120        | Chart area height in pixels                                      |
| barWidth    | number                                                      | undefined  | Fixed width per bar in pixels; omit for auto-sizing via flexGrow |
| gap         | number                                                      | 8          | Horizontal gap between bars in pixels                            |
| showLabels  | boolean                                                     | true       | Show label text below each bar                                   |
| showValues  | boolean                                                     | false      | Show numeric value above each bar                                |
| color       | Color                                                       | '#3b82f6'  | Default bar color; overridden per bar via BarChartBar.color      |
| style       | Style                                                       | undefined  | Additional styles applied to the outer container Box             |
| interactive | boolean                                                     | false      | Enable hover highlighting and per-bar ChartTooltip               |
| onBarHover  | (index: number \| null, bar: BarChartBar \| null) => void   | undefined  | Called when the pointer enters or leaves a bar                   |
| onBarPress  | (index: number, bar: BarChartBar) => void                   | undefined  | Called when a bar is clicked (requires interactive: true)        |

---------
EXAMPLES
---------

EXAMPLE: Basic bar chart with auto-sizing
--------
```tsx
import { BarChart } from '@reactjit/core';

export default function App() {
  return (
    <Box style={{ width: '100%', height: '100%', padding: 20 }}>
      <BarChart
        data={[
          { label: 'Q1', value: 40 },
          { label: 'Q2', value: 65 },
          { label: 'Q3', value: 52 },
          { label: 'Q4', value: 78 },
        ]}
      />
    </Box>
  );
}
```
--------
Platforms: all

EXAMPLE: Custom colors per bar with values displayed
--------
```tsx
import { BarChart } from '@reactjit/core';

export default function App() {
  return (
    <Box style={{ width: '100%', height: '100%', padding: 20 }}>
      <BarChart
        data={[
          { label: 'CPU',  value: 72, color: '#ef4444' },
          { label: 'RAM',  value: 45, color: '#3b82f6' },
          { label: 'Disk', value: 89, color: '#f59e0b' },
        ]}
        height={80}
        showValues
      />
    </Box>
  );
}
```
--------
Platforms: all

EXAMPLE: Interactive chart with hover callback
--------
```tsx
import { useState } from 'react';
import { BarChart } from '@reactjit/core';

const weekly = [
  { label: 'Mon', value: 32 },
  { label: 'Tue', value: 58 },
  { label: 'Wed', value: 47 },
  { label: 'Thu', value: 71 },
  { label: 'Fri', value: 55 },
];

export default function App() {
  const [active, setActive] = useState<string | null>(null);

  return (
    <Box style={{ width: '100%', height: '100%', padding: 20, gap: 12 }}>
      <BarChart
        data={weekly}
        height={100}
        interactive
        onBarHover={(_, bar) => setActive(bar ? bar.label : null)}
      />
      {active && (
        <Text style={{ fontSize: 12, color: '#94a3b8' }}>
          {`Hovering: ${active}`}
        </Text>
      )}
    </Box>
  );
}
```
--------
Platforms: love2d, web

---------
CRITICAL RULES
---------

* The `data` prop is required. An empty array renders an empty placeholder Box at the specified height.
* Bar heights are relative to the maximum value in the data. If all values are equal, all bars render at full height.
* If `maxValue` is 0 (all values are 0), bars are normalized against 1, producing minimum-height (1px) bars.
* Per-bar `color` overrides the chart-level `color` prop for that specific bar only.
* BarChart does not handle negative values. All values should be non-negative.
* When `showValues` is true, 14px of vertical space is reserved above bars for value labels — factor this into the `height` prop.
* When `barWidth` is omitted, bars use `flexGrow: 1` and fill the parent container. The parent must have an explicit width for this to work.
* When `barWidth` is set, the chart computes its own total width as `data.length * barWidth + (data.length - 1) * gap`.
* `onBarPress` only fires when `interactive` is also `true`.
* Hover events require pointer support — not available on all targets. Check platform capabilities before enabling `interactive`.

---------
SEE ALSO
---------

- HorizontalBarChart — horizontal orientation for the same kind of comparison
- StackedBarChart — multi-series bars stacked in a single column
- Sparkline — compact inline trend bars with no axes or labels
- ProgressBar — single-value horizontal fill bar
- ChartTooltip — standalone tooltip primitive used internally by this component


========================================
Box
========================================

Category: Components | Difficulty: beginner | Platforms: love2d, web, terminal, cc, nvim, hs, awesome

Fundamental flexbox layout container for all visual structure

---------
OVERVIEW
---------

Box is the fundamental layout primitive in ReactJIT. Every visual structure is built from nested Box elements. It renders as a flexbox container with configurable direction, alignment, spacing, and visual styling. In web mode it outputs a `<div>` with CSS flexbox; in native mode it outputs a `'View'` host element processed by the Lua layout engine.

Box also serves as the primary event target. It accepts handlers for pointer, keyboard, touch, gamepad, and drag interactions. For higher-level interaction patterns (press state tracking, long press, hover callbacks), use Pressable instead, which wraps Box internally.

---------
API / SYNTAX
---------

```tsx
import { Box } from '@reactjit/core';
```

```tsx
<Box
  style={{ width: '100%', height: '100%', flexDirection: 'column', backgroundColor: '#1e1e2e' }}
  hoverStyle={{ backgroundColor: '#313244' }}
  activeStyle={{ backgroundColor: '#45475a' }}
  onClick={(event) => { /* handle click */ }}
>
  {children}
</Box>
```

Props:
| Prop            | Type                            | Default   | Description                          |
|-----------------|---------------------------------|-----------|--------------------------------------|
| style           | Style                           | undefined | Flexbox layout and visual styling    |
| hoverStyle      | Style                           | undefined | Style merged on pointer hover        |
| activeStyle     | Style                           | undefined | Style merged on press/active         |
| onClick         | (event: LoveEvent) => void      | undefined | Click/press handler                  |
| onRelease       | (event: LoveEvent) => void      | undefined | Release handler                      |
| onPointerEnter  | (event: LoveEvent) => void      | undefined | Pointer enters the box               |
| onPointerLeave  | (event: LoveEvent) => void      | undefined | Pointer leaves the box               |
| onKeyDown       | (event: LoveEvent) => void      | undefined | Key pressed while focused            |
| onKeyUp         | (event: LoveEvent) => void      | undefined | Key released while focused           |
| onTextInput     | (event: LoveEvent) => void      | undefined | Text input while focused             |
| onWheel         | (event: LoveEvent) => void      | undefined | Mouse wheel / scroll wheel           |
| onTouchStart    | (event: LoveEvent) => void      | undefined | Touch began                          |
| onTouchEnd      | (event: LoveEvent) => void      | undefined | Touch ended                          |
| onTouchMove     | (event: LoveEvent) => void      | undefined | Touch moved                          |
| onGamepadPress  | (event: LoveEvent) => void      | undefined | Gamepad button pressed               |
| onGamepadRelease| (event: LoveEvent) => void      | undefined | Gamepad button released              |
| onGamepadAxis   | (event: LoveEvent) => void      | undefined | Gamepad axis moved                   |
| onDragStart     | (event: LoveEvent) => void      | undefined | Drag gesture began                   |
| onDrag          | (event: LoveEvent) => void      | undefined | Drag gesture moved                   |
| onDragEnd       | (event: LoveEvent) => void      | undefined | Drag gesture ended                   |
| onFileDrop      | (event: LoveEvent) => void      | undefined | File dropped on the box              |
| onDirectoryDrop | (event: LoveEvent) => void      | undefined | Directory dropped on the box         |
| onFileDragEnter | (event: LoveEvent) => void      | undefined | File drag enters the box             |
| onFileDragLeave | (event: LoveEvent) => void      | undefined | File drag leaves the box             |
| onFocus         | (event: LoveEvent) => void      | undefined | Box gained focus                     |
| onBlur          | (event: LoveEvent) => void      | undefined | Box lost focus                       |
| children        | ReactNode                       | undefined | Child elements                       |

The `style` prop accepts the full Style type, which includes flexbox properties (flexDirection, justifyContent, alignItems, flexGrow, flexShrink, flexBasis, flexWrap, gap), sizing (width, height, minWidth, minHeight, maxWidth, maxHeight, aspectRatio), spacing (padding*, margin*), visual (backgroundColor, borderRadius, borderWidth, borderColor, opacity, overflow, boxShadow), positioning (position, top/right/bottom/left, zIndex), gradients, transforms, transitions, and animations.

Default flexDirection is 'column'. Default display is 'flex'.

---------
EXAMPLES
---------

EXAMPLE: Root container filling the viewport
--------
```tsx
<Box style={{ width: '100%', height: '100%', backgroundColor: '#1e1e2e' }}>
  <Text style={{ fontSize: 24, color: '#cdd6f4' }}>Hello</Text>
</Box>
```
--------
Platforms: all

EXAMPLE: Horizontal row with centered, spaced children
--------
```tsx
<Box style={{
  width: '100%',
  flexDirection: 'row',
  justifyContent: 'space-between',
  alignItems: 'center',
  padding: 16,
}}>
  <Text style={{ fontSize: 14, color: '#cdd6f4' }}>Left</Text>
  <Text style={{ fontSize: 14, color: '#cdd6f4' }}>Right</Text>
</Box>
```
--------
Platforms: all

EXAMPLE: Interactive box with hover and active states
--------
```tsx
<Box
  style={{ padding: 12, backgroundColor: '#313244', borderRadius: 8 }}
  hoverStyle={{ backgroundColor: '#45475a' }}
  activeStyle={{ backgroundColor: '#585b70' }}
  onClick={() => console.log('clicked')}
>
  <Text style={{ fontSize: 14, color: '#cdd6f4' }}>Click me</Text>
</Box>
```
--------
Platforms: love2d, web

EXAMPLE: Drag-enabled box
--------
```tsx
const [pos, setPos] = useState({ x: 100, y: 100 });

<Box
  style={{
    position: 'absolute',
    left: pos.x,
    top: pos.y,
    width: 80,
    height: 80,
    backgroundColor: '#89b4fa',
    borderRadius: 8,
  }}
  onDragStart={() => {}}
  onDrag={(e) => setPos({ x: pos.x + (e.dx ?? 0), y: pos.y + (e.dy ?? 0) })}
  onDragEnd={() => {}}
>
  <Text style={{ fontSize: 12, color: '#1e1e2e' }}>Drag me</Text>
</Box>
```
--------
Platforms: love2d, web

---------
PLATFORM NOTES
---------

Love2D:
Renders as a 'View' host element. Layout computed by layout.lua (yoga-like flexbox). Visual rendering by painter.lua. Supports all style properties including gradients, transforms, box shadows, and per-corner border radii. The Lua painter handles hover/active style merging on the native side for smooth interaction without JS round-trips.

Web:
Renders as a <div> with CSS flexbox. Style is converted via styleToCSS(). The web renderer uses React state for hover/active style merging. Drag handlers use pointer capture for reliable cross-element dragging. Keyboard handlers automatically set tabIndex=0 for focus.

Terminal:
Grid targets use <view> lowercase intrinsic, not the Box component. Layout is handled by @reactjit/grid.

---------
CRITICAL RULES
---------

* Root containers MUST have width: '100%' and height: '100%'. Using flexGrow: 1 on the root will produce a zero-height container because there is no parent with a known size to grow into.
* Row-direction Boxes (flexDirection: 'row') MUST have an explicit width for justifyContent to work. Box nodes have no intrinsic width (only Text nodes do), so without a width the container collapses and justifyContent has nothing to distribute.
* Do not use flexGrow without sibling sizing context. flexGrow only works when the parent has a known size and there are siblings that establish the remaining space.
* Default flexDirection is 'column'. If you expect horizontal layout, set flexDirection: 'row' explicitly.
* Keep flex trees shallow. Prefer a single Box with flexDirection: 'row' over multiple nested wrapper Boxes. Deep hierarchies slow down layout computation.
* Love2D is a fixed canvas, not a scrolling page. Fill the viewport; do not leave empty space expecting it to scroll.

---------
SEE ALSO
---------

- Text
- Image
- Pressable
- FlexRow
- FlexColumn
- Style type reference
- Layout System


========================================
Breadcrumbs
========================================

Category: Components | Difficulty: beginner | Platforms: love2d, web, terminal, cc, nvim, hs, awesome

Navigation breadcrumb trail with clickable path segments and configurable separator.

---------
OVERVIEW
---------

Breadcrumbs renders a horizontal trail of navigation items separated by a configurable separator character. Each item except the last is clickable via Pressable and triggers the `onSelect` callback with the item's id. The last item in the trail is displayed as bold text, indicating the current location.

The component is built with Box, Text, and Pressable primitives, making it work identically across all targets.

---------
API / SYNTAX
---------

Import: import { Breadcrumbs } from '@reactjit/core';

BreadcrumbItem type:
```ts
interface BreadcrumbItem {
  id: string;
  label: string;
}
```

Usage:
```tsx
<Breadcrumbs
  items={[
    { id: 'home', label: 'Home' },
    { id: 'settings', label: 'Settings' },
    { id: 'profile', label: 'Profile' },
  ]}
  onSelect={(id) => navigate(id)}
/>
```

Props:

| Prop      | Type                       | Default | Description                             |
|-----------|----------------------------|---------|-----------------------------------------|
| items     | BreadcrumbItem[]           | (required)| Array of breadcrumb path items        |
| onSelect  | (id: string) => void       | -       | Called when a non-last item is clicked  |
| separator | string                     | '/'     | Separator character between items       |
| style     | Style                      | -       | Container style overrides               |

---------
EXAMPLES
---------

EXAMPLE: Basic breadcrumbs
--------
```tsx
<Breadcrumbs
  items={[
    { id: 'root', label: 'Dashboard' },
    { id: 'users', label: 'Users' },
    { id: 'alice', label: 'Alice' },
  ]}
  onSelect={(id) => setPage(id)}
/>
```
--------
Platforms: all

EXAMPLE: Custom separator
--------
```tsx
<Breadcrumbs
  items={path}
  onSelect={navigateTo}
  separator=">"
/>
```
--------
Platforms: all

EXAMPLE: Single item (current page only)
--------
```tsx
<Breadcrumbs items={[{ id: 'home', label: 'Home' }]} />
```
--------
Platforms: all

---------
CRITICAL RULES
---------

* The `items` prop is required. An empty array renders nothing.
* The last item in the array is always rendered as non-clickable bold text. It does not trigger `onSelect`.
* Only non-last items are wrapped in Pressable. The last item is a plain Text element.
* Breadcrumbs renders in a row layout. Ensure the parent container has enough width for the trail, or items will wrap depending on the parent's flexWrap setting.

---------
SEE ALSO
---------

- Tabs
- NavPanel
- Pressable


========================================
Camera
========================================

Category: Components | Difficulty: intermediate | Platforms: love2d

Defines the viewpoint for a 3D Scene

---------
OVERVIEW
---------

Camera sets the perspective viewpoint used when rendering a Scene. Place exactly one Camera as a direct child of Scene. All props are reactive — changing position, lookAt, or fov causes the camera to update each frame without full reconciliation overhead. If no Camera is present in a Scene, a default camera at position [0, -3, 2] looking at the origin is used automatically.

---------
API / SYNTAX
---------

```tsx
import { Camera } from '@reactjit/3d';
```

Props:
| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| position | [number, number, number] | [0, -3, 2] | Camera position in world space as [x, y, z] |
| lookAt | [number, number, number] | [0, 0, 0] | The point in world space the camera looks at as [x, y, z] |
| fov | number | Math.PI / 3 | Field of view in radians (Math.PI / 3 = 60 degrees) |
| near | number | 0.01 | Near clipping plane distance — geometry closer than this is not rendered |
| far | number | 1000 | Far clipping plane distance — geometry beyond this is not rendered |

---------
EXAMPLES
---------

EXAMPLE: Basic camera looking at the origin
--------
```tsx
import { Scene, Camera, Mesh } from '@reactjit/3d';

function App() {
  return (
    <Scene style={{ width: 800, height: 600 }}>
      <Camera position={[0, -4, 2]} lookAt={[0, 0, 0]} />
      <Mesh geometry="box" color="#89b4fa" />
    </Scene>
  );
}
```
--------
Platforms: love2d

EXAMPLE: Animated camera orbiting the scene
--------
```tsx
import { Scene, Camera, Mesh, AmbientLight, DirectionalLight } from '@reactjit/3d';
import { useAnimationFrame } from '@reactjit/core';

function OrbitingCamera() {
  const [angle, setAngle] = React.useState(0);

  useAnimationFrame((dt) => {
    setAngle((a) => a + dt * 0.5);
  });

  const radius = 5;
  const camX = Math.sin(angle) * radius;
  const camY = -Math.cos(angle) * radius;

  return (
    <Scene style={{ width: 800, height: 600 }}>
      <Camera position={[camX, camY, 2]} lookAt={[0, 0, 0]} fov={Math.PI / 4} />
      <AmbientLight color="#1a1a2e" intensity={0.2} />
      <DirectionalLight direction={[1, -1, 1]} color="#ffffff" intensity={1.0} />
      <Mesh geometry="sphere" color="#cba6f7" />
    </Scene>
  );
}
```
--------
Platforms: love2d

EXAMPLE: Narrow field of view for a telephoto effect
--------
```tsx
import { Scene, Camera, Mesh } from '@reactjit/3d';

function TelephotoView() {
  return (
    <Scene style={{ width: 800, height: 600 }}>
      <Camera
        position={[0, -10, 4]}
        lookAt={[0, 0, 0]}
        fov={Math.PI / 8}
        near={0.1}
        far={100}
      />
      <Mesh geometry="box" color="#a6e3a1" scale={1.5} />
    </Scene>
  );
}
```
--------
Platforms: love2d

---------
CRITICAL RULES
---------

* Place exactly one Camera per Scene — multiple Camera children in the same Scene produce undefined behavior
* Camera renders nothing on web targets and returns null; it is Love2D only
* The position and lookAt props use a right-handed coordinate system where Z is up
* fov is in radians, not degrees — use Math.PI / 3 for 60 degrees, Math.PI / 4 for 45 degrees
* Omitting Camera entirely is valid; the default camera at [0, -3, 2] will be used

---------
SEE ALSO
---------

- Scene — the 3D viewport that Camera must be placed inside
- Mesh — renderable geometry that Camera looks at
- DirectionalLight — sun-like light that interacts with mesh shading
- AmbientLight — base illumination applied across all meshes


========================================
Card
========================================

Category: Components | Difficulty: beginner | Platforms: love2d, web, terminal, cc, nvim, hs, awesome

A styled container component with optional header, title, subtitle, and body sections. Provides a consistent visual structure for grouping related content with automatic borders, padding, and spacing.

---------
OVERVIEW
---------

The Card component is a pre-styled container that provides a visual boundary around related content. It automatically includes dark theme styling (slate colors), rounded corners, borders, and proper padding. Cards can optionally display a header section with a title and subtitle, separated from the body content by a horizontal divider. The component auto-sizes to its content unless explicit dimensions are provided via the `style` prop.

---------
API / SYNTAX
---------

```tsx
import { Card } from '@reactjit/core';

<Card
  title="Optional Title"
  subtitle="Optional Subtitle"
  style={{ width: 300 }}
  headerStyle={{ backgroundColor: '#2d3748' }}
  bodyStyle={{ gap: 8 }}
>
  {children}
</Card>
```

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| `children` | `React.ReactNode` | (required) | Content rendered in the card body section |
| `title` | `string` | `undefined` | Optional title displayed in the header (bold, 14px, light gray) |
| `subtitle` | `string` | `undefined` | Optional subtitle below title (11px, muted gray) |
| `style` | `Style` | `{}` | Overrides for the outer card container (dimensions, margins, etc.) |
| `headerStyle` | `Style` | `{}` | Overrides for the header section (padding, background, borders) |
| `bodyStyle` | `Style` | `{}` | Overrides for the body section (padding, gap, alignment) |

**Default Styling:**
- Background: `#1e293b` (slate-800)
- Border: 1px `#334155` (slate-700)
- Border radius: 8px
- Header padding: 12px
- Body padding: 12px
- Header border-bottom: 1px when title or subtitle present

---------
EXAMPLES
---------

EXAMPLE: Basic Card with Title
--------
```tsx
<Card title="System Status">
  <Text fontSize={14} style={{ color: '#e2e8f0' }}>
    All systems operational
  </Text>
</Card>
```
--------
Platforms: all

EXAMPLE: Card with Title and Subtitle
--------
```tsx
<Card
  title="Player Stats"
  subtitle="Last updated 2 minutes ago"
>
  <Box style={{ gap: 4 }}>
    <Text fontSize={12} style={{ color: '#e2e8f0' }}>Health: 100/100</Text>
    <Text fontSize={12} style={{ color: '#e2e8f0' }}>Mana: 85/100</Text>
  </Box>
</Card>
```
--------
Platforms: all

EXAMPLE: Card with No Header
--------
```tsx
<Card>
  <Text fontSize={14} style={{ color: '#e2e8f0' }}>
    This card has no header section.
    Use it for simple content grouping.
  </Text>
</Card>
```
--------
Platforms: all

EXAMPLE: Custom Dimensions and Styling
--------
```tsx
<Card
  title="Fixed Width Card"
  style={{ width: 400, height: 200 }}
  headerStyle={{ backgroundColor: '#2d3748', padding: 16 }}
  bodyStyle={{ gap: 12, padding: 16 }}
>
  <Text fontSize={14} style={{ color: '#e2e8f0' }}>
    Custom header background and increased padding.
  </Text>
</Card>
```
--------
Platforms: all

EXAMPLE: Dashboard Card Grid
--------
```tsx
<Box style={{ flexDirection: 'row', gap: 16, flexWrap: 'wrap' }}>
  <Card title="CPU Usage" style={{ width: 200 }}>
    <Text fontSize={24} style={{ color: '#22c55e' }}>42%</Text>
  </Card>
  <Card title="Memory" style={{ width: 200 }}>
    <Text fontSize={24} style={{ color: '#eab308' }}>67%</Text>
  </Card>
  <Card title="Disk" style={{ width: 200 }}>
    <Text fontSize={24} style={{ color: '#ef4444' }}>89%</Text>
  </Card>
</Box>
```
--------
Platforms: all

EXAMPLE: Card with Badge
--------
```tsx
<Card
  title="Task Manager"
  subtitle="Active processes"
>
  <Box style={{ gap: 8 }}>
    <Box style={{ flexDirection: 'row', gap: 8, alignItems: 'center' }}>
      <Text fontSize={12} style={{ color: '#e2e8f0' }}>Server</Text>
      <Badge label="Running" variant="success" />
    </Box>
    <Box style={{ flexDirection: 'row', gap: 8, alignItems: 'center' }}>
      <Text fontSize={12} style={{ color: '#e2e8f0' }}>Database</Text>
      <Badge label="Error" variant="error" />
    </Box>
  </Box>
</Card>
```
--------
Platforms: all

---------
CRITICAL RULES
---------

* All `<Text>` children inside Card must have explicit `fontSize` (linter enforces this)
* Card auto-sizes to content unless explicit `width`/`height` provided in `style`
* Header section only renders if `title` or `subtitle` is provided
* Do not use Unicode symbols in Text children (see layout rules)
* For full-viewport cards, use `style={{ width: '100%', height: '100%' }}`
* Use `bodyStyle={{ gap: X }}` instead of manual spacing between children
* `headerStyle` and `bodyStyle` merge with defaults (use to override padding, colors, etc.)
* Card uses `overflow: 'hidden'` to clip content at rounded corners

---------
SEE ALSO
---------

- Badge component for status indicators inside cards
- Box primitive for custom containers without styling
- FlexRow/FlexCol helpers for card body layouts
- ScrollView for scrollable card content
- Layout Rules: auto-sizing behavior (CLAUDE.md)


========================================
ChartTooltip
========================================

Category: Components | Difficulty: beginner | Platforms: love2d, web, terminal, cc, nvim, hs, awesome

Positioned overlay tooltip with typed sub-components for use inside chart elements

---------
OVERVIEW
---------

ChartTooltip is a compound component that renders a dark-themed floating overlay anchored to any of the four cardinal sides of its parent. When `visible` is false the component returns null, making it safe to always declare in the render tree and control via state. Three typed sub-components — ChartTooltip.Label, ChartTooltip.Value, and ChartTooltip.Detail — provide pre-styled text tiers: a bold blue label for the category name, a large white value for the primary number, and a small muted detail line for secondary information.

---------
API / SYNTAX
---------

```tsx
import { ChartTooltip } from '@reactjit/core';
```

Props (ChartTooltip):
| Prop     | Type                              | Default   | Description                                                                            |
|----------|-----------------------------------|-----------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| visible  | boolean                           | required  | Controls whether the tooltip is rendered; false returns null                           |
| anchor   | 'top' \| 'right' \| 'bottom' \| 'left' | 'top' | Which side of the parent the tooltip floats toward                               |
| children | ReactNode                         | required  | Content to render inside the tooltip box; use the sub-components below                |

Sub-components (no props beyond children):
| Sub-component           | Text style                                    | Intended use                        |
|-------------------------|-----------------------------------------------|-------------------------------------|
| ChartTooltip.Label      | color: #61a6fa, fontSize: 10, fontWeight: bold | Category name or x-axis label       |
| ChartTooltip.Value      | color: #e1e4f0, fontSize: 12, fontWeight: bold | Primary numeric value               |
| ChartTooltip.Detail     | color: #8892a6, fontSize: 9               | Secondary info (percentage, series) |

Anchor positioning:
| Anchor | Position                          | Offset   |
|--------|-----------------------------------|----------|
| top    | bottom: '100%', alignSelf: center | 4px gap  |
| bottom | top: '100%', alignSelf: center    | 4px gap  |
| right  | left: '100%', top: 0             | 4px gap  |
| left   | right: '100%', top: 0            | 4px gap  |

---------
EXAMPLES
---------

EXAMPLE: Basic tooltip inside an interactive bar
--------
```tsx
import { useState } from 'react';
import { ChartTooltip } from '@reactjit/core';

export default function App() {
  const [hovered, setHovered] = useState(false);

  return (
    <Box style={{ width: '100%', height: '100%', padding: 40 }}>
      <Box
        onPointerEnter={() => setHovered(true)}
        onPointerLeave={() => setHovered(false)}
        style={{ width: 120, height: 60, backgroundColor: '#3b82f6', position: 'relative' }}
      >
        <ChartTooltip visible={hovered} anchor="top">
          <ChartTooltip.Label>Revenue</ChartTooltip.Label>
          <ChartTooltip.Value>$4,200</ChartTooltip.Value>
          <ChartTooltip.Detail>+12% vs last month</ChartTooltip.Detail>
        </ChartTooltip>
      </Box>
    </Box>
  );
}
```
--------
Platforms: love2d, web

EXAMPLE: Right-anchored tooltip for a horizontal bar row
--------
```tsx
import { useState } from 'react';
import { ChartTooltip } from '@reactjit/core';

export default function App() {
  const [hovered, setHovered] = useState(false);

  return (
    <Box style={{ width: '100%', height: '100%', padding: 40 }}>
      <Box
        onPointerEnter={() => setHovered(true)}
        onPointerLeave={() => setHovered(false)}
        style={{ flexDirection: 'row', alignItems: 'center', position: 'relative' }}
      >
        <Box style={{ width: 200, height: 16, backgroundColor: '#22c55e', borderRadius: 3 }} />
        <ChartTooltip visible={hovered} anchor="right">
          <ChartTooltip.Label>Downloads</ChartTooltip.Label>
          <ChartTooltip.Value>8,341</ChartTooltip.Value>
          <ChartTooltip.Detail>83% of max</ChartTooltip.Detail>
        </ChartTooltip>
      </Box>
    </Box>
  );
}
```
--------
Platforms: love2d, web

EXAMPLE: Dynamic tooltip driven by external state
--------
```tsx
import { useState } from 'react';
import { ChartTooltip } from '@reactjit/core';

const bars = [
  { label: 'Jan', value: 42 },
  { label: 'Feb', value: 58 },
  { label: 'Mar', value: 35 },
];

export default function App() {
  const [hoveredIdx, setHoveredIdx] = useState<number | null>(null);
  const max = Math.max(...bars.map(b => b.value));

  return (
    <Box style={{ width: '100%', height: '100%', padding: 20 }}>
      <Box style={{ flexDirection: 'row', alignItems: 'flex-end', gap: 10 }}>
        {bars.map((bar, i) => (
          <Box
            key={i}
            onPointerEnter={() => setHoveredIdx(i)}
            onPointerLeave={() => setHoveredIdx(null)}
            style={{
              width: 36,
              height: Math.round((bar.value / max) * 100),
              backgroundColor: '#6366f1',
              position: 'relative',
            }}
          >
            <ChartTooltip visible={hoveredIdx === i} anchor="top">
              <ChartTooltip.Label>{bar.label}</ChartTooltip.Label>
              <ChartTooltip.Value>{`${bar.value}`}</ChartTooltip.Value>
              <ChartTooltip.Detail>{`${Math.round((bar.value / max) * 100)}%`}</ChartTooltip.Detail>
            </ChartTooltip>
          </Box>
        ))}
      </Box>
    </Box>
  );
}
```
--------
Platforms: love2d, web

---------
CRITICAL RULES
---------

* The parent Box must have `position: 'relative'` set for the tooltip's absolute positioning to work correctly. Without it the tooltip will escape to the nearest positioned ancestor.
* ChartTooltip uses `position: 'absolute'` and `zIndex: 10`. If neighboring elements have higher z-index values, the tooltip may render beneath them.
* When `visible` is false, ChartTooltip returns null entirely — it does not render an invisible placeholder. This means no hit area or layout space is reserved for it.
* The `anchor` prop positions the tooltip relative to the parent element boundaries, not relative to the pointer position. Use `top` for bars (tooltip floats above), `right` for horizontal bars (tooltip floats to the right of the bar end).
* ChartTooltip.Label, ChartTooltip.Value, and ChartTooltip.Detail are plain Text wrappers with fixed styles. They do not accept style overrides — they are not general-purpose text components.
* You may pass any ReactNode as children, including custom Box layouts, but the built-in sub-components provide consistent chart-wide styling.
* Do not use Unicode symbols in any text passed to the sub-components.

---------
SEE ALSO
---------

- HorizontalBarChart — uses ChartTooltip internally with anchor="right"
- StackedBarChart — uses ChartTooltip internally with anchor="top"
- LineChart — uses ChartTooltip internally with anchor="top"
- AreaChart — inherits LineChart's tooltip behavior


========================================
Checkbox
========================================

Category: Components | Difficulty: beginner | Platforms: love2d, web, terminal, cc, nvim, hs, awesome

A toggleable checkbox with optional label, supporting controlled and uncontrolled modes.

---------
OVERVIEW
---------

Checkbox is a square toggle control that displays a filled inner square when checked. It optionally renders a text label to the right of the checkbox.

Checkbox supports both controlled and uncontrolled modes. Pass `value` for controlled behavior, or use `defaultValue` for uncontrolled. The checkbox box border color changes between `color` (checked) and `uncheckedColor` (unchecked), and the box fills with `color` when checked with a white inner indicator.

---------
API / SYNTAX
---------

Import: import { Checkbox } from '@reactjit/core';

Usage:
```tsx
<Checkbox
  value={accepted}
  onValueChange={(v) => setAccepted(v)}
  label="Accept terms"
/>
```

Props:

| Prop           | Type                       | Default    | Description                              |
|----------------|----------------------------|------------|------------------------------------------|
| value          | boolean                    | -          | Controlled value. Omit for uncontrolled  |
| defaultValue   | boolean                    | false      | Initial value in uncontrolled mode       |
| onValueChange  | (value: boolean) => void   | -          | Called when the checkbox is toggled       |
| disabled       | boolean                    | false      | Disables interaction and dims opacity    |
| label          | string                     | -          | Text label rendered beside the checkbox  |
| size           | number                     | 20         | Checkbox box size in pixels              |
| color          | Color                      | '#3b82f6'  | Border and fill color when checked       |
| uncheckedColor | Color                      | '#6b7280'  | Border color when unchecked              |
| style          | Style                      | -          | Container style overrides                |

---------
EXAMPLES
---------

EXAMPLE: Basic controlled checkbox with label
--------
```tsx
const [checked, setChecked] = useState(false);

<Checkbox
  value={checked}
  onValueChange={setChecked}
  label="Enable notifications"
/>
```
--------
Platforms: all

EXAMPLE: Uncontrolled with custom colors
--------
```tsx
<Checkbox
  defaultValue={true}
  color="#22c55e"
  uncheckedColor="#94a3b8"
  size={24}
  label="Remember me"
/>
```
--------
Platforms: all

EXAMPLE: Disabled checkbox
--------
```tsx
<Checkbox value={false} disabled label="Unavailable option" />
```
--------
Platforms: all

---------
CRITICAL RULES
---------

* The `size` prop controls only the checkbox square, not the label text size.
* When `disabled` is true, opacity is set to 0.5 and click events are ignored.
* In controlled mode, the visual state does not change unless the parent updates `value` in response to `onValueChange`.

---------
SEE ALSO
---------

- Switch
- Radio
- Pressable


========================================
CodeBlock
========================================

Category: Components | Difficulty: intermediate | Platforms: love2d, web

Syntax-highlighted code display with support for multiple languages

---------
OVERVIEW
---------

CodeBlock displays source code with syntax highlighting. On Love2D, it's a Lua-owned primitive that renders directly via love.graphics, giving precise control over colors, fonts, and line spacing. On web, it renders as a <pre> element with monospace font.

Use CodeBlock for documentation, tutorials, error messages, REPLs, or any read-only code display. For editable code, use TextEditor instead.

---------
API / SYNTAX
---------

<CodeBlock
  code={string}
  language?={string}
  fontSize?={number}
  style?={Style}
/>

code
  Required. The source code string to display.
  Supports multi-line code with \n line breaks.

language
  Optional. Language hint for syntax highlighting.
  Values: "auto" (default), "js", "jsx", "ts", "tsx", "lua", "py", "json", etc.
  "auto" attempts to detect; explicit language is more reliable.

fontSize
  Optional. Font size in pixels. Default: 10.

style
  Optional. Additional Style properties (backgroundColor, color, padding, etc.).
  On Love2D, these override the default theme.
  On web, these apply as CSS overrides.

---------
EXAMPLES
---------

EXAMPLE: Basic Code Display
--------
import { CodeBlock } from '@reactjit/core'

function CodeSnippet() {
  const code = `
const greeting = "Hello, world!"
console.log(greeting)
  `.trim()

  return (
    <CodeBlock
      code={code}
      language="js"
    />
  )
}

export default CodeSnippet
--------
Platforms: love2d, web

EXAMPLE: Custom Styling
--------
import { CodeBlock, Box } from '@reactjit/core'

function StyledCode() {
  const code = `
function fibonacci(n) {
  if (n <= 1) return n
  return fibonacci(n-1) + fibonacci(n-2)
}
  `.trim()

  return (
    <Box style={{ padding: 16 }}>
      <CodeBlock
        code={code}
        language="js"
        fontSize={12}
        style={{
          backgroundColor: { r: 0.05, g: 0.05, b: 0.1, a: 1 },
          color: { r: 0.9, g: 0.9, b: 0.9, a: 1 },
          padding: 16,
          borderRadius: 4,
        }}
      />
    </Box>
  )
}

export default StyledCode
--------
Platforms: love2d, web

EXAMPLE: Lua Code with Highlighting
--------
import { CodeBlock } from '@reactjit/core'

function LuaExample() {
  const code = `
-- Load Lua modules
local json = require("lua.json")
local http = require("lua.http")

-- Parse JSON
local data = json.decode('{"hello": "world"}')
print(data.hello)
  `.trim()

  return (
    <CodeBlock
      code={code}
      language="lua"
      fontSize={11}
    />
  )
}

export default LuaExample
--------
Platforms: love2d, web

EXAMPLE: Error Stack Trace
--------
import { CodeBlock, Box, Text } from '@reactjit/core'

function ErrorDisplay({ error }) {
  return (
    <Box
      style={{
        width: '100%',
        padding: 16,
        backgroundColor: { r: 0.2, g: 0.05, b: 0.05, a: 1 },
      }}
    >
      <Text style={{ fontSize: 14, color: { r: 1, g: 0.2, b: 0.2, a: 1 } }}>
        Stack Trace
      </Text>
      <CodeBlock
        code={error.stack}
        language="auto"
        fontSize={9}
      />
    </Box>
  )
}

export default ErrorDisplay
--------
Platforms: love2d, web

---------
PLATFORM NOTES
---------

Love2D:
CodeBlock is a Lua-owned element (like TextEditor). Rendering is done directly
via love.graphics, not through React layout. Dimensions are computed based on
text metrics and padding. Multi-line code is supported. Line wrapping is not
automatic; long lines may extend off-screen.

Web:
CodeBlock renders as an HTML <pre> element with monospace font. Overflow is
