scrollable (overflow: auto). Syntax highlighting is applied via CSS classes
(for server-side highlighting) or client-side JavaScript (if integrated).

---------
PERFORMANCE
---------

CodeBlock rendering on Love2D is re-computed each frame. For very large code blocks (5000+ lines), consider splitting into multiple smaller blocks or lazy-loading visible sections. Web rendering is CSS-based and performant.

---------
CRITICAL RULES
---------

* Do not use CodeBlock for user input; use TextEditor instead
* CodeBlock on Love2D is not scrollable within its bounds; wrap in ScrollView for long code
* Language hints are optional but recommended for accurate highlighting
* Code strings must be raw strings (no special escaping needed for \n)
* fontSize should be chosen to keep code readable; default 10 is often too small for complex code

---------
SEE ALSO
---------

- text.txt - Basic text rendering
- texteditor.txt - Editable text with line numbers
- rendering.txt - Advanced rendering features (codeblock.lua details)


========================================
ContextMenu
========================================

Category: Components | Difficulty: intermediate | Platforms: love2d, web

Right-click context menu with built-in text operations and custom actions

---------
OVERVIEW
---------

ContextMenu is a right-click context menu component that provides built-in text operations (Copy, Inspect) and supports custom menu items. It follows the "inverse pattern" where Lua handles all interaction state (rendering, hover, keyboard navigation) and JS receives only boundary events (open, close, select). On Love2D, the menu is rendered entirely by Lua; on web, it renders as a positioned dropdown overlay. The menu automatically detects text nodes under the cursor and enables/disables the Copy action based on active text selection.

---------
API / SYNTAX
---------

```tsx
import { ContextMenu } from '@reactjit/core';

<ContextMenu
  items={[...]}
  onSelect={(event) => {...}}
  onOpen={() => {...}}
  onClose={() => {...}}
>
  {children}
</ContextMenu>
```

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| `items` | `ContextMenuItem[]` | `undefined` | Custom menu items to append after built-in items |
| `onSelect` | `(event: ContextMenuEvent) => void` | `undefined` | Called when any menu item is selected (built-in or custom) |
| `onOpen` | `() => void` | `undefined` | Called when the context menu opens |
| `onClose` | `() => void` | `undefined` | Called when the context menu closes (click outside, Escape, or item selected) |
| `children` | `React.ReactNode` | `undefined` | Child elements that will trigger the context menu on right-click |

**ContextMenuItem interface:**
```tsx
interface ContextMenuItem {
  label: string;        // Display text
  action: string;       // Action identifier dispatched on selection
  disabled?: boolean;   // Gray out and make unclickable
  separator?: boolean;  // Render as divider line instead of item
}
```

**ContextMenuEvent interface:**
```tsx
interface ContextMenuEvent {
  action: string;           // Action identifier of selected item
  targetId?: number;        // ID of the node that was right-clicked
  hasSelection?: boolean;   // Whether text was selected when menu opened
  selectedText?: string;    // The selected text content (if any)
}
```

---------
PLATFORM NOTES
---------

Love2D:
- Context menu is entirely Lua-owned (lua/contextmenu.lua)
- Right-click detection happens in init.lua → calls contextmenu.open()
- Lua performs hit testing to find the node under cursor and detect text nodes
- Built-in items: Copy (enabled when text selected), Inspect (opens inspector on clicked node)
- Copy action automatically writes to clipboard via love.system.setClipboardText()
- Keyboard navigation: Arrow keys move selection, Enter confirms, Escape closes
- Mouse interaction: Hover highlights items, click selects, click outside closes
- Rendering: Dark panel with rounded corners, hover highlight, separators
- Custom items from ContextMenu ancestors are appended after built-in Copy, before Inspect
- Menu position is clamped to stay within viewport bounds

Web:
- Renders as positioned div overlay with fixed positioning
- Uses native onContextMenu handler to intercept right-clicks
- Automatically prevents default browser context menu
- Closes on click outside (via window click listener)
- Closes on Escape key (via window keydown listener)
- CSS styling matches Love2D appearance (dark theme, rounded corners, hover states)
- Text selection and clipboard operations use browser APIs

---------
CRITICAL RULES
---------

* ContextMenu wraps children and intercepts right-clicks anywhere within the subtree
* Built-in "Copy" item is ALWAYS present (disabled when no text selected, enabled when text selected)
* Built-in "Inspect" item is ALWAYS present on Love2D (opens visual inspector F12 on the clicked node)
* Custom items (via `items` prop) are inserted between Copy and Inspect
* Use `separator: true` to render divider lines between groups of items
* Action identifiers starting with `__` are reserved for built-in actions (`__copy`, `__inspect`)
* The nearest ContextMenu ancestor determines which custom items appear
* Nested ContextMenus: inner menus take precedence over outer ones
* On Love2D, text selection requires `userSelect: 'text'` style on Text nodes
* Menu automatically closes after item selection, click outside, or Escape key
* Disabled items are grayed out and unclickable but remain visible
* Separator items do not trigger hover or selection
* Empty items array is allowed (menu shows only built-in Copy and Inspect)
* JS receives only boundary events: open, close, select (NO hover, keyboard nav, or render events)
* On Love2D, the menu is positioned at click coordinates but clamped to viewport

---------
SEE ALSO
---------

- TextInput — Single-line text input with cursor and selection (can be wrapped with ContextMenu for Copy)
- TextEditor — Multi-line code editor (can be wrapped with ContextMenu for Copy)
- Box — Container primitive (can be wrapped with ContextMenu for custom actions)
- Modal — Overlay component (similar inverse pattern where Lua owns interaction)
- Text — Text primitive with `userSelect: 'text'` for text selection support


========================================
DebugOverlay
========================================

Category: Components | Difficulty: intermediate | Platforms: love2d, web

Context wrapper that visualizes layout boundaries with colored borders and optional dimension labels

---------
OVERVIEW
---------

DebugOverlay is a React context provider that wraps any subtree to visualize layout boundaries without opening the full inspector. It adds colored borders to every Box within its subtree and optionally displays dimension labels on each element. The overlay can be toggled on/off at runtime and customized per-subtree. Use DebugBox as a drop-in replacement for Box to respect the debug context automatically.

DebugOverlay is particularly useful when tracking down layout bugs, verifying flex container alignment, or understanding how nested boxes are sizing themselves. Unlike the full inspector (F12 in Love2D), DebugOverlay can be embedded directly in your component tree and toggled programmatically.

---------
API / SYNTAX
---------

```tsx
import { DebugOverlay, DebugBox, useDebugOverlay } from '@reactjit/core';
```

```tsx
<DebugOverlay enabled={true} borderColor="rgba(255, 100, 100, 0.6)" showLabels={false}>
  <DebugBox style={{ width: '100%', height: '100%' }}>
    {children}
  </DebugBox>
</DebugOverlay>
```

DebugOverlay Props:
| Prop        | Type             | Default                      | Description                               |
|-------------|------------------|------------------------------|-------------------------------------------|
| children    | ReactNode        | (required)                   | Subtree to wrap with debug context        |
| enabled     | boolean          | true                         | Toggle overlay on/off                     |
| borderColor | string           | 'rgba(255, 100, 100, 0.6)'   | Border color for debug outlines           |
| showLabels  | boolean          | false                        | Show dimension labels on each Box         |

DebugBox Props:
| Prop     | Type      | Default   | Description                                |
|----------|-----------|-----------|-------------------------------------------|
| children | ReactNode | undefined | Child elements                            |
| style    | Style     | undefined | Box style (border added when debug enabled)|
| ...props | any       | undefined | All other Box props (onClick, etc.)       |

useDebugOverlay Hook:
Returns `{ enabled: boolean, borderColor: string, showLabels: boolean }` from the nearest DebugOverlay context.

---------
EXAMPLES
---------

EXAMPLE: Basic overlay for a section
--------
```tsx
import { DebugOverlay, DebugBox, Text } from '@reactjit/core';

function MyComponent() {
  return (
    <DebugOverlay>
      <DebugBox style={{ width: '100%', height: '100%', flexDirection: 'column', gap: 16 }}>
        <DebugBox style={{ flexGrow: 1, backgroundColor: '#1e1e2e' }}>
          <Text style={{ fontSize: 16, color: '#cdd6f4' }}>Section 1</Text>
        </DebugBox>
        <DebugBox style={{ flexGrow: 2, backgroundColor: '#313244' }}>
          <Text style={{ fontSize: 16, color: '#cdd6f4' }}>Section 2</Text>
        </DebugBox>
      </DebugBox>
    </DebugOverlay>
  );
}
```
--------
Platforms: all

EXAMPLE: Toggle overlay with a button
--------
```tsx
import { useState } from 'react';
import { DebugOverlay, DebugBox, Box, Text, Pressable } from '@reactjit/core';

function LayoutDebugger() {
  const [debugEnabled, setDebugEnabled] = useState(false);

  return (
    <Box style={{ width: '100%', height: '100%', flexDirection: 'column' }}>
      <Pressable onPress={() => setDebugEnabled(!debugEnabled)}>
        <Box style={{ padding: 12, backgroundColor: '#313244' }}>
          <Text style={{ fontSize: 14, color: '#cdd6f4' }}>
            {debugEnabled ? 'Hide' : 'Show'} Debug Overlay
          </Text>
        </Box>
      </Pressable>

      <DebugOverlay enabled={debugEnabled}>
        <DebugBox style={{ flexGrow: 1, padding: 16 }}>
          <DebugBox style={{ width: '100%', flexDirection: 'row', gap: 12 }}>
            <DebugBox style={{ flexGrow: 1, height: 100, backgroundColor: '#45475a' }} />
            <DebugBox style={{ flexGrow: 1, height: 100, backgroundColor: '#585b70' }} />
          </DebugBox>
        </DebugBox>
      </DebugOverlay>
    </Box>
  );
}
```
--------
Platforms: all

EXAMPLE: Custom border color and labels
--------
```tsx
import { DebugOverlay, DebugBox, Text } from '@reactjit/core';

function GridDebug() {
  return (
    <DebugOverlay borderColor="rgba(0, 255, 200, 0.8)" showLabels={true}>
      <DebugBox style={{ width: 400, height: 300, flexDirection: 'column', gap: 8 }}>
        <DebugBox style={{ width: '100%', height: 50, backgroundColor: '#1e1e2e' }}>
          <Text style={{ fontSize: 14, color: '#cdd6f4' }}>Header</Text>
        </DebugBox>
        <DebugBox style={{ flexGrow: 1, backgroundColor: '#313244' }}>
          <Text style={{ fontSize: 14, color: '#cdd6f4' }}>Content</Text>
        </DebugBox>
      </DebugBox>
    </DebugOverlay>
  );
}
```
--------
Platforms: all

EXAMPLE: Using the hook to read debug state
--------
```tsx
import { DebugOverlay, useDebugOverlay, Box, Text } from '@reactjit/core';

function DebugAwareComponent() {
  const { enabled, borderColor } = useDebugOverlay();

  return (
    <Box style={{ padding: 16 }}>
      <Text style={{ fontSize: 14, color: enabled ? '#f38ba8' : '#cdd6f4' }}>
        Debug mode: {enabled ? 'ON' : 'OFF'}
      </Text>
      {enabled && (
        <Text style={{ fontSize: 12, color: '#a6adc8' }}>
          Border color: {borderColor}
        </Text>
      )}
    </Box>
  );
}

function App() {
  return (
    <DebugOverlay enabled={true}>
      <DebugAwareComponent />
    </DebugOverlay>
  );
}
```
--------
Platforms: all

---------
CRITICAL RULES
---------

* **DebugBox is required** — Regular `<Box>` elements inside a DebugOverlay will NOT show debug borders. You must use `<DebugBox>` for context awareness.
* **Context nesting** — If you nest multiple DebugOverlay providers, the innermost context wins. Each subtree can have its own border color and enabled state.
* **Performance** — DebugOverlay adds a style merge operation to every DebugBox render. For large subtrees (100+ boxes), consider wrapping only the problematic section instead of the entire tree.
* **Remove before production** — DebugOverlay is a development tool. Disable it or remove it entirely before building production distributions.
* **showLabels not implemented** — The `showLabels` prop is accepted but dimension label rendering is not yet implemented. Currently it only affects the context value returned by useDebugOverlay.

---------
SEE ALSO
---------

- Box — The fundamental layout primitive that DebugBox wraps
- Visual Inspector (F12) — Full-featured inspector for Love2D with tree navigation and interaction state
- Layout rules (CLAUDE.md) — Critical layout debugging tips and common pitfalls


========================================
DirectionalLight
========================================

Category: Components | Difficulty: intermediate | Platforms: love2d

A directional sun-like light source that provides shading and specular highlights

---------
OVERVIEW
---------

DirectionalLight simulates a distant light source such as a sun, with rays that are parallel across the entire scene. The direction prop points toward the light source (not away from it). It drives diffuse shading and Phong specular highlights on all lit meshes. Only one DirectionalLight per Scene is currently supported. It renders nothing on web targets.

---------
API / SYNTAX
---------

```tsx
import { DirectionalLight } from '@reactjit/3d';
```

Props:
| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| direction | [number, number, number] | — | Direction toward the light source as [x, y, z] — the vector is normalized automatically |
| color | string | '#ffffff' | Light color as a hex string |
| intensity | number | 1.0 | Intensity multiplier applied to the color |

---------
EXAMPLES
---------

EXAMPLE: Standard white directional light from above and to the side
--------
```tsx
import { Scene, Camera, Mesh, AmbientLight, DirectionalLight } from '@reactjit/3d';

function LitScene() {
  return (
    <Scene style={{ width: 800, height: 600 }}>
      <Camera position={[0, -5, 3]} lookAt={[0, 0, 0]} />
      <AmbientLight color="#1a1a2e" intensity={0.15} />
      <DirectionalLight direction={[1, -1, 1]} color="#ffffff" intensity={1.0} />
      <Mesh geometry="sphere" color="#89b4fa" specular={32} />
    </Scene>
  );
}
```
--------
Platforms: love2d

EXAMPLE: Warm sunlight for a planet scene
--------
```tsx
import { Scene, Camera, Mesh, AmbientLight, DirectionalLight } from '@reactjit/3d';

function PlanetScene() {
  return (
    <Scene style={{ width: 800, height: 600 }} stars backgroundColor="#020208">
      <Camera position={[0, -6, 2]} lookAt={[0, 0, 0]} />
      <AmbientLight color="#000814" intensity={0.08} />
      <DirectionalLight direction={[1, -0.5, 0.3]} color="#fffbe6" intensity={1.4} />
      <Mesh
        geometry="sphere"
        texture="planet"
        seed={12}
        specular={16}
        fresnel={4}
        opacity={0.95}
      />
    </Scene>
  );
}
```
--------
Platforms: love2d

EXAMPLE: Low-angle dramatic rim light
--------
```tsx
import { Scene, Camera, Mesh, AmbientLight, DirectionalLight } from '@reactjit/3d';

function DramaticLight() {
  return (
    <Scene style={{ width: 800, height: 600 }} backgroundColor="#0d0d0d">
      <Camera position={[0, -5, 1]} lookAt={[0, 0, 0]} />
      <AmbientLight color="#050510" intensity={0.05} />
      <DirectionalLight direction={[-1, 0.1, 0.1]} color="#4488ff" intensity={1.8} />
      <Mesh
        geometry="box"
        color="#222244"
        edgeColor="#000000"
        edgeWidth={0.025}
        specular={64}
      />
    </Scene>
  );
}
```
--------
Platforms: love2d

---------
CRITICAL RULES
---------

* DirectionalLight renders nothing on web targets and returns null — it is Love2D only
* Only one DirectionalLight per Scene is currently supported — placing multiple will produce undefined behavior
* DirectionalLight must be a direct child of Scene, not nested inside other elements
* The direction prop points TOWARD the light, not away from it — [1, -1, 1] means light comes from the positive-X, negative-Y, positive-Z direction
* The direction vector is normalized automatically; you do not need to provide a unit vector
* DirectionalLight with intensity set to 0 disables directional shading; only AmbientLight will remain
* Specular highlights on Mesh are driven by DirectionalLight — adjusting Mesh's specular prop changes highlight tightness

---------
SEE ALSO
---------

- Scene — the 3D viewport that DirectionalLight must be placed inside
- AmbientLight — provides base fill light to complement DirectionalLight
- Mesh — the geometry that receives directional shading and specular highlights
- Camera — the viewpoint whose angle relative to DirectionalLight affects specular intensity


========================================
Divider
========================================

Category: Components | Difficulty: beginner | Platforms: love2d, web, terminal, cc, nvim, hs, awesome

Horizontal or vertical separator line with configurable color and thickness

---------
OVERVIEW
---------

Divider renders a horizontal or vertical separator line between content sections. It's a styled Box with backgroundColor, defaulting to a 1-pixel horizontal line. Use it to visually separate groups of content, create borders, or add structure to complex layouts.

---------
API / SYNTAX
---------

import { Divider } from '@reactjit/core';

Props:
| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| direction | 'horizontal' \| 'vertical' | 'horizontal' | Orientation of the divider line |
| color | string | '#334155' | Color of the line (any valid CSS color: hex, rgb, named) |
| thickness | number | 1 | Line thickness in pixels |
| style | Style | undefined | Additional style overrides (merged after direction/color/thickness) |

---------
EXAMPLES
---------

EXAMPLE: Basic horizontal divider
--------
<Box style={{ width: '100%', flexDirection: 'column' }}>
  <Text fontSize={18}>Section 1</Text>
  <Divider />
  <Text fontSize={18}>Section 2</Text>
</Box>
--------
Platforms: all

EXAMPLE: Vertical divider in row
--------
<Box style={{ width: 300, height: 100, flexDirection: 'row', alignItems: 'center' }}>
  <Text fontSize={16}>Left</Text>
  <Divider direction="vertical" />
  <Text fontSize={16}>Right</Text>
</Box>
--------
Platforms: all

EXAMPLE: Custom color and thickness
--------
<Box style={{ width: '100%', flexDirection: 'column' }}>
  <Text fontSize={20}>Header</Text>
  <Divider color="#ef4444" thickness={3} />
  <Text fontSize={14}>Content below the bold red divider</Text>
</Box>
--------
Platforms: all

EXAMPLE: Divider with margin spacing
--------
<Box style={{ width: '100%', flexDirection: 'column' }}>
  <Text fontSize={16}>Item 1</Text>
  <Divider style={{ marginTop: 10, marginBottom: 10 }} />
  <Text fontSize={16}>Item 2</Text>
</Box>
--------
Platforms: all

EXAMPLE: Partial-width divider
--------
<Box style={{ width: '100%', flexDirection: 'column', alignItems: 'center' }}>
  <Text fontSize={18}>Centered Content</Text>
  <Divider style={{ width: '50%' }} />
  <Text fontSize={18}>More Content</Text>
</Box>
--------
Platforms: all

---------
CRITICAL RULES
---------

* Horizontal dividers default to width: '100%' — they fill the parent's width automatically
* Vertical dividers default to height: '100%' — they fill the parent's height automatically
* Vertical dividers require a parent with explicit height, or they collapse to zero
* The style prop is merged last, so you can override any default behavior (width, height, margins, etc.)
* Divider is purely visual — it doesn't affect layout spacing beyond its own thickness
* Combine Divider with Spacer for both visual separation and spacing (Divider for the line, Spacer for the gap)
* In grid targets (terminal, cc, nvim), dividers render as block characters at the cell level
* Default color (#334155) is a neutral slate gray that works on most backgrounds — adjust for light/dark themes

---------
SEE ALSO
---------

- Spacer — invisible spacing component (vs. Divider's visible line)
- Box — primitive that Divider is built on (use Box directly for borders or complex separators)
- Card — component that often contains Dividers to separate sections
- Badge — another layout helper component often used with Dividers


========================================
FlatList
========================================

Category: Components | Difficulty: intermediate | Platforms: love2d, web, terminal, cc, nvim, hs, awesome

Virtualized scrollable list that renders only visible items for efficient large dataset display.

---------
OVERVIEW
---------

FlatList is a virtualized scrollable list component that renders only items visible within the scroll viewport plus a configurable buffer zone. It uses spacer elements above and below the visible window to maintain correct total scroll height without mounting every item in the data array.

FlatList supports vertical and horizontal scrolling, multi-column grid layouts, inverted lists, infinite scrolling via `onEndReached`, and imperative scrolling methods. It delegates all scrolling behavior to the ScrollView component internally. FlatList accepts a `ref` for imperative control via `scrollToIndex` and `scrollToOffset`.

---------
API / SYNTAX
---------

Import: import { FlatList } from '@reactjit/core';

Usage:
```tsx
<FlatList
  data={items}
  renderItem={({ item, index }) => (
    <Box style={{ height: 50, padding: 8 }}>
      <Text style={{ fontSize: 14 }}>{item.name}</Text>
    </Box>
  )}
  itemHeight={50}
  style={{ height: 400 }}
/>
```

FlatListProps<T>:

| Prop                   | Type                                                  | Default  | Description                                        |
|------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------|----------|----------------------------------------------------|
| data                   | T[]                                                   | (required)| Array of items to render                          |
| renderItem             | (info: { item: T; index: number }) => ReactNode       | (required)| Render function for each item                     |
| keyExtractor           | (item: T, index: number) => string                    | -        | Unique key for each item (defaults to index)       |
| horizontal             | boolean                                               | false    | Enable horizontal scrolling                        |
| itemHeight             | number                                                | -        | Fixed item height (for vertical lists)             |
| itemWidth              | number                                                | -        | Fixed item width (for horizontal lists)            |
| estimatedItemSize      | number                                                | 50       | Estimated item size when exact size is unknown     |
| onEndReached           | () => void                                            | -        | Called when scroll nears the end of the list       |
| onEndReachedThreshold  | number                                                | 0.5      | Distance from end (in viewports) to trigger        |
| onScroll               | (event: ScrollEvent) => void                          | -        | Called on every scroll position change              |
| ListHeaderComponent    | ReactNode                                             | -        | Component rendered before the first item           |
| ListFooterComponent    | ReactNode                                             | -        | Component rendered after the last item             |
| ListEmptyComponent     | ReactNode                                             | -        | Component rendered when data is empty              |
| ItemSeparatorComponent | ReactNode                                             | -        | Component rendered between items                   |
| style                  | Style                                                 | -        | Outer ScrollView container style                   |
| contentContainerStyle  | Style                                                 | -        | Inner content container style                      |
| initialNumToRender     | number                                                | 10       | Number of items to render on first mount           |
| windowSize             | number                                                | 5        | Number of viewports to keep rendered (buffer)      |
| maxToRenderPerBatch    | number                                                | 10       | Max items to render per batch update               |
| inverted               | boolean                                               | false    | Reverses the item order                            |
| numColumns             | number                                                | -        | Number of columns for grid layout                  |

FlatListRef (via React.forwardRef):

| Method         | Signature                                              | Description                           |
|----------------|--------------------------------------------------------|---------------------------------------|
| scrollToIndex  | (params: { index: number; animated?: boolean }) => void| Scroll to a specific item by index    |
| scrollToOffset | (params: { offset: number; animated?: boolean }) => void| Scroll to a specific pixel offset    |

ScrollEvent type:
```ts
interface ScrollEvent {
  scrollX: number;
  scrollY: number;
  contentWidth: number;
  contentHeight: number;
}
```

---------
EXAMPLES
---------

EXAMPLE: Basic vertical list
--------
```tsx
const items = Array.from({ length: 1000 }, (_, i) => ({ id: String(i), title: `Item ${i}` }));

<FlatList
  data={items}
  renderItem={({ item }) => (
    <Box style={{ height: 40, padding: 8, borderBottomWidth: 1, borderColor: '#334155' }}>
      <Text style={{ fontSize: 12, color: '#e2e8f0' }}>{item.title}</Text>
    </Box>
  )}
  keyExtractor={(item) => item.id}
  itemHeight={40}
  style={{ height: 300, width: '100%' }}
/>
```
--------
Platforms: all

EXAMPLE: Grid layout with numColumns
--------
```tsx
<FlatList
  data={gridItems}
  renderItem={({ item }) => (
    <Box style={{ height: 80, flexGrow: 1, flexBasis: 0, margin: 4, backgroundColor: '#1e293b', borderRadius: 6 }}>
      <Text style={{ fontSize: 11, color: '#94a3b8' }}>{item.label}</Text>
    </Box>
  )}
  numColumns={3}
  itemHeight={80}
  style={{ height: 400, width: '100%' }}
/>
```
--------
Platforms: all

EXAMPLE: Infinite scroll with onEndReached
--------
```tsx
const listRef = useRef<FlatListRef>(null);

<FlatList
  ref={listRef}
  data={messages}
  renderItem={({ item }) => <MessageRow message={item} />}
  itemHeight={60}
  onEndReached={() => loadMore()}
  onEndReachedThreshold={0.8}
  ListEmptyComponent={<Text style={{ fontSize: 14, color: '#64748b' }}>No messages</Text>}
  style={{ height: '100%', width: '100%' }}
/>
```
--------
Platforms: all

---------
CRITICAL RULES
---------

* FlatList MUST have an explicit `height` (or parent with known height) in its style. Without it, the scroll container collapses and virtualization breaks.
* For best virtualization performance, provide `itemHeight` (vertical) or `itemWidth` (horizontal). Without these, `estimatedItemSize` (default: 50) is used, which may cause visual jumping.
* The `renderItem` function should return elements with a consistent, fixed height matching `itemHeight`. Variable-height items will cause virtualization misalignment.
* `keyExtractor` defaults to using the array index as a string. For data that changes order or gets inserted/removed, always provide a stable `keyExtractor`.

---------
SEE ALSO
---------

- ScrollView
- Table


========================================
FlexColumn
========================================

Category: Components | Difficulty: beginner | Platforms: love2d, web, terminal, cc, nvim, hs, awesome

Convenience wrapper around Box with vertical flex layout

---------
OVERVIEW
---------

FlexColumn is a convenience component that wraps Box with flexDirection='column' pre-configured. It provides simplified props for common vertical layout tasks: gap spacing, vertical justification, and horizontal alignment. While column is the default flexDirection for Box, FlexColumn offers a cleaner API when you want explicit vertical stacking with readable prop names.

---------
API / SYNTAX
---------

import { FlexColumn } from '@reactjit/core';

Props:
| Prop | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| gap | number | No | undefined | Spacing between children (pixels) |
| justify | 'flex-start' \| 'flex-end' \| 'center' \| 'space-between' \| 'space-around' \| 'space-evenly' | No | undefined | Vertical distribution (justifyContent) |
| align | 'flex-start' \| 'flex-end' \| 'center' \| 'stretch' | No | undefined | Horizontal alignment (alignItems) |
| style | Style | No | {} | Additional style properties (merged after defaults) |
| children | React.ReactNode | Yes | - | Child elements to lay out vertically |

---------
EXAMPLES
---------

EXAMPLE: Basic vertical stack with spacing
--------
import { FlexColumn, Text } from '@reactjit/core';

<FlexColumn gap={12}>
  <Text fontSize={20}>Heading</Text>
  <Text fontSize={16}>Subheading</Text>
  <Text fontSize={14}>Body text goes here</Text>
</FlexColumn>
--------
Platforms: all

EXAMPLE: Centered card layout
--------
import { FlexColumn, Text, Box } from '@reactjit/core';

<FlexColumn
  align="center"
  gap={8}
  style={{
    width: 200,
    padding: 16,
    backgroundColor: 'white',
    borderRadius: 8
  }}
>
  <Box style={{ width: 64, height: 64, backgroundColor: 'blue', borderRadius: 32 }} />
  <Text fontSize={16}>Username</Text>
  <Text fontSize={12} style={{ color: '#666' }}>@handle</Text>
</FlexColumn>
--------
Platforms: all

EXAMPLE: Space-between full height layout
--------
import { FlexColumn, Text } from '@reactjit/core';

<FlexColumn justify="space-between" style={{ width: '100%', height: '100%' }}>
  <Text fontSize={18}>Header</Text>
  <Text fontSize={14}>Content in the middle</Text>
  <Text fontSize={12} style={{ color: '#999' }}>Footer</Text>
</FlexColumn>
--------
Platforms: all

EXAMPLE: Left-aligned form layout
--------
import { FlexColumn, Text, TextInput } from '@reactjit/core';

<FlexColumn gap={16} align="flex-start" style={{ width: 300, padding: 20 }}>
  <Text fontSize={14}>Name:</Text>
  <TextInput
    placeholder="Enter name"
    fontSize={14}
    style={{ width: '100%', padding: 8, backgroundColor: '#f0f0f0' }}
  />
  <Text fontSize={14}>Email:</Text>
  <TextInput
    placeholder="Enter email"
    fontSize={14}
    style={{ width: '100%', padding: 8, backgroundColor: '#f0f0f0' }}
  />
</FlexColumn>
--------
Platforms: all

EXAMPLE: Vertically centered viewport content
--------
import { FlexColumn, Text, Box } from '@reactjit/core';

<FlexColumn
  justify="center"
  align="center"
  style={{ width: '100%', height: '100%' }}
>
  <Box style={{
    padding: 32,
    backgroundColor: '#333',
    borderRadius: 16
  }}>
    <Text fontSize={24} style={{ color: 'white' }}>Welcome</Text>
  </Box>
</FlexColumn>
--------
Platforms: all

---------
CRITICAL RULES
---------

* FlexColumn requires explicit height for justify to work properly. Without a height, justifyContent='center' or 'space-between' will have no effect on vertical distribution.
* Root-level FlexColumn components should use width: '100%', height: '100%' to fill the viewport. Do NOT use flexGrow: 1 at the root.
* Gap spacing is applied between children, not around the edges. Use padding in style prop for outer spacing.
* The style prop is merged last, so you can override gap, justify, and align by passing conflicting properties in style (though this defeats the purpose of the convenience props).
* FlexColumn auto-sizes to fit content if no width/height is specified. Height is sum of children heights plus gaps. Width is determined by the widest child.
* align='stretch' only works if children do not have explicit widths. Children will stretch to match the container's width.
* Do not use flexGrow on children without setting a height on FlexColumn. Percentage-based children need explicit parent dimensions.
* FlexColumn is functionally equivalent to Box with flexDirection='column', since column is the default. Use FlexColumn when you want self-documenting code with convenient props. Use Box when you need full style control.

---------
SEE ALSO
---------

- Box — The underlying primitive that FlexColumn wraps
- FlexRow — Horizontal layout convenience wrapper
- Spacer — Creates flexible empty space in flex layouts
- Layout Rules (CLAUDE.md) — Critical layout constraints and common pitfalls
- Auto-Sizing (CLAUDE.md) — How containers size to fit content


========================================
FlexRow
========================================

Category: Components | Difficulty: beginner | Platforms: love2d, web, terminal, cc, nvim, hs, awesome

Convenience wrapper around Box with horizontal flex layout

---------
OVERVIEW
---------

FlexRow is a convenience component that wraps Box with flexDirection='row' pre-configured. It provides simplified props for common horizontal layout tasks: gap spacing, horizontal justification, vertical alignment, and wrapping. Use FlexRow when you need to arrange children horizontally without manually setting flexDirection on every Box.

---------
API / SYNTAX
---------

import { FlexRow } from '@reactjit/core';

Props:
| Prop | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| gap | number | No | undefined | Spacing between children (pixels) |
| justify | 'flex-start' \| 'flex-end' \| 'center' \| 'space-between' \| 'space-around' \| 'space-evenly' | No | undefined | Horizontal distribution (justifyContent) |
| align | 'flex-start' \| 'flex-end' \| 'center' \| 'stretch' \| 'baseline' | No | undefined | Vertical alignment (alignItems) |
| wrap | boolean | No | false | Allow children to wrap to next line (flexWrap: 'wrap') |
| style | Style | No | {} | Additional style properties (merged after defaults) |
| children | React.ReactNode | Yes | - | Child elements to lay out horizontally |

---------
EXAMPLES
---------

EXAMPLE: Basic horizontal layout with spacing
--------
import { FlexRow, Text } from '@reactjit/core';

<FlexRow gap={16}>
  <Text fontSize={16}>First</Text>
  <Text fontSize={16}>Second</Text>
  <Text fontSize={16}>Third</Text>
</FlexRow>
--------
Platforms: all

EXAMPLE: Centered content with justify and align
--------
import { FlexRow, Text, Box } from '@reactjit/core';

<FlexRow
  justify="center"
  align="center"
  style={{ width: '100%', height: 100, backgroundColor: '#f0f0f0' }}
>
  <Box style={{ width: 50, height: 50, backgroundColor: 'blue' }} />
  <Text fontSize={16}>Centered</Text>
</FlexRow>
--------
Platforms: all

EXAMPLE: Space-between distribution (toolbar pattern)
--------
import { FlexRow, Text } from '@reactjit/core';

<FlexRow justify="space-between" style={{ width: '100%', padding: 8 }}>
  <Text fontSize={14}>Left</Text>
  <Text fontSize={14}>Center</Text>
  <Text fontSize={14}>Right</Text>
</FlexRow>
--------
Platforms: all

EXAMPLE: Wrapping horizontal grid
--------
import { FlexRow, Box } from '@reactjit/core';

<FlexRow wrap gap={8} style={{ width: 300 }}>
  {Array.from({ length: 10 }).map((_, i) => (
    <Box key={i} style={{ width: 60, height: 60, backgroundColor: 'green' }} />
  ))}
</FlexRow>
--------
Platforms: all

EXAMPLE: Combining with custom styles
--------
import { FlexRow, Text } from '@reactjit/core';

<FlexRow
  gap={12}
  align="baseline"
  style={{
    padding: 16,
    backgroundColor: '#333',
    borderRadius: 8
  }}
>
  <Text fontSize={24} style={{ color: 'white' }}>Title</Text>
  <Text fontSize={14} style={{ color: '#aaa' }}>Subtitle</Text>
</FlexRow>
--------
Platforms: all

---------
CRITICAL RULES
---------

* FlexRow requires explicit width for justify to work properly. Row containers have no intrinsic width (only Text nodes do). Without a width, justifyContent='center' or 'space-between' will have no effect. Always set width: '100%' or a fixed width when using justify.
* Do not use flexGrow on children without setting a width on FlexRow. Percentage-based children need explicit parent dimensions.
* Gap spacing is applied between children, not around the edges. Use padding in style prop for outer spacing.
* The style prop is merged last, so you can override gap, justify, and align by passing conflicting properties in style (though this defeats the purpose of the convenience props).
* FlexRow auto-sizes to fit content if no width/height is specified. Height is determined by the tallest child. Width is sum of children widths plus gaps.
* Use wrap={true} when you want a responsive horizontal grid that breaks to multiple rows. Requires explicit width on FlexRow.
* align='stretch' only works if children do not have explicit heights. Children will stretch to match the tallest sibling.

---------
SEE ALSO
---------

- Box — The underlying primitive that FlexRow wraps
- FlexColumn — Vertical layout convenience wrapper
- Spacer — Creates flexible empty space in flex layouts
- Layout Rules (CLAUDE.md) — Critical layout constraints and common pitfalls
- Auto-Sizing (CLAUDE.md) — How containers size to fit content


========================================
FocusGroup
========================================

Category: Components | Difficulty: intermediate | Platforms: love2d, web

Scoped focus region for keyboard and gamepad navigation

---------
OVERVIEW
---------

FocusGroup creates a scoped focus region for controller and keyboard navigation. It restricts Tab key and gamepad D-pad navigation to elements within the group, enabling isolated UI sections and multiplayer support where each controller navigates its own region. FocusGroups support automatic player-based ring colors (P1 blue, P2 red, P3 green, P4 yellow) and optional controller binding for split-screen or couch multiplayer scenarios.

When a FocusGroup is bound to a specific controller via the controller prop, only that controller can navigate elements within the group. If no controller is specified, the group accepts input from any controller. FocusGroups can be nested, and focus is automatically restored when switching between mouse and controller input modes. The framework tracks the last focused element per group and automatically scrolls focused elements into view within ScrollView parents.

---------
API / SYNTAX
---------

```tsx
import { FocusGroup } from '@reactjit/core';
```

```tsx
<FocusGroup
  controller={0}
  ringColor={[0.3, 0.6, 1.0, 0.9]}
  style={{ padding: 16, backgroundColor: '#1e1e2e' }}
>
  <Pressable onPress={() => {}}>
    <Text fontSize={14}>Option 1</Text>
  </Pressable>
  <Pressable onPress={() => {}}>
    <Text fontSize={14}>Option 2</Text>
  </Pressable>
</FocusGroup>
```

Props:
| Prop       | Type                           | Default   | Description                                       |
|------------|--------------------------------|-----------|---------------------------------------------------|
| controller | number                         | undefined | Joystick ID (0-based). Omit for any controller.  |
| ringColor  | [number, number, number, number?] | undefined | Focus ring color as [r, g, b, a] (0-1 range). Default: player-based (P1 blue, P2 red, P3 green, P4 yellow). |
| style      | Style                          | undefined | Container style (wraps Box internally).           |
| children   | ReactNode                      | REQUIRED  | Focusable elements (Pressable, TextInput, etc.).  |

RingColor format:
```ts
type RingColor = [number, number, number, number?];
// [r, g, b, a] where each is 0-1
// Example: [0.3, 0.6, 1.0, 0.9] = semi-transparent blue
```

---------
EXAMPLES
---------

EXAMPLE: Basic focus group (any controller)
--------
```tsx
<FocusGroup style={{ padding: 16, gap: 8 }}>
  <Pressable onPress={() => console.log('A')}>
    <Text fontSize={14}>Button A</Text>
  </Pressable>
  <Pressable onPress={() => console.log('B')}>
    <Text fontSize={14}>Button B</Text>
  </Pressable>
  <Pressable onPress={() => console.log('C')}>
    <Text fontSize={14}>Button C</Text>
  </Pressable>
</FocusGroup>
```
--------
Platforms: love2d, web

EXAMPLE: Multiplayer split-screen (controller-bound groups)
--------
```tsx
<Box direction="row" fill>
  {/* Player 1 (left half) */}
  <FocusGroup controller={0} style={{ width: '50%', padding: 16 }}>
    <Text fontSize={18} bold>Player 1</Text>
    <Pressable onPress={() => setP1Ready(true)}>
      <Text fontSize={14}>Ready</Text>
    </Pressable>
    <Pressable onPress={() => setP1Character('mage')}>
      <Text fontSize={14}>Select Mage</Text>
    </Pressable>
  </FocusGroup>

  {/* Player 2 (right half) */}
  <FocusGroup controller={1} style={{ width: '50%', padding: 16 }}>
    <Text fontSize={18} bold>Player 2</Text>
    <Pressable onPress={() => setP2Ready(true)}>
      <Text fontSize={14}>Ready</Text>
    </Pressable>
    <Pressable onPress={() => setP2Character('warrior')}>
      <Text fontSize={14}>Select Warrior</Text>
    </Pressable>
  </FocusGroup>
</Box>
```
--------
Platforms: love2d, web

EXAMPLE: Custom ring color
--------
```tsx
<FocusGroup ringColor={[1.0, 0.5, 0.0, 1.0]} style={{ padding: 16 }}>
  <Pressable onPress={() => {}}>
    <Text fontSize={14}>Orange-ringed button</Text>
  </Pressable>
</FocusGroup>
```
--------
Platforms: love2d, web

EXAMPLE: Nested focus groups (isolated navigation regions)
--------
```tsx
<Box fill padding={16} gap={16}>
  {/* Top toolbar (isolated) */}
  <FocusGroup style={{ flexDirection: 'row', gap: 8 }}>
    <Pressable onPress={() => {}}>
      <Text fontSize={14}>File</Text>
    </Pressable>
    <Pressable onPress={() => {}}>
      <Text fontSize={14}>Edit</Text>
    </Pressable>
  </FocusGroup>

  {/* Main content (isolated) */}
  <FocusGroup style={{ flexGrow: 1, padding: 16 }}>
    <Pressable onPress={() => {}}>
      <Text fontSize={14}>Item 1</Text>
    </Pressable>
    <Pressable onPress={() => {}}>
      <Text fontSize={14}>Item 2</Text>
    </Pressable>
  </FocusGroup>
</Box>
```
--------
Platforms: love2d, web

EXAMPLE: Focus group with TextInput
--------
```tsx
<FocusGroup style={{ padding: 16, gap: 8 }}>
  <TextInput
    placeholder="Username"
    style={{ padding: 8, backgroundColor: '#313244' }}
    textStyle={{ fontSize: 14 }}
  />
  <TextInput
    placeholder="Password"
    secureTextEntry
    style={{ padding: 8, backgroundColor: '#313244' }}
    textStyle={{ fontSize: 14 }}
  />
  <Pressable onPress={handleLogin}>
    <Text fontSize={14}>Login</Text>
  </Pressable>
</FocusGroup>
```
--------
Platforms: love2d, web

---------
PLATFORM NOTES
---------

Love2D:
FocusGroup is implemented in lua/focus.lua. It registers as a scoped navigation region on every layout pass. Spatial navigation (D-pad / left stick) uses center-point distance with directional filtering — the closest element in the pressed direction becomes focused. Focus state is preserved when switching between mouse and controller modes. Focused elements automatically scroll into view within ScrollView parents. The focus ring animates with exponential smoothing for visual polish.
Controller binding: joystick IDs are 0-based. Controller 0 is the first connected gamepad, Controller 1 is the second, etc. If a group has controller={0}, only joystick 0 can navigate it. If controller is undefined, any joystick can navigate the group. Unbound groups (no controller prop) default to accepting input from any controller that is not bound to a specific group.
Ring colors: If ringColor is not set and the group is bound to a controller, the framework auto-assigns a player color (P1 blue, P2 red, P3 green, P4 yellow) based on joystick ID modulo 4. If ringColor is set explicitly, that color is used regardless of controller. If ringColor is undefined and controller is undefined, the default blue ring is used.

Web:
FocusGroup renders as a <div> with tabIndex management. Tab key navigation is scoped using keyboard event handlers that trap focus within the group. Gamepad navigation is not implemented on web (browser gamepad API support is limited). Ring colors are rendered as CSS outline or box-shadow. Focus state is managed via React state and DOM focus events.

Terminal:
Not available on grid targets. Use terminal-specific focus management.

---------
CRITICAL RULES
---------

* FocusGroup only navigates elements with focusable={true} or interactive handlers (onClick, onPress, onKeyDown, etc.). Non-interactive elements are skipped.
* Tab key and D-pad navigation wrap within the group. Pressing right on the last element does NOT jump to the next group — it wraps to the first element.
* Controller-bound groups (controller={0}) ignore input from other controllers. If controller 1 presses D-pad and is not bound to any group, it navigates the default group (elements outside all FocusGroups) and any unclaimed FocusGroups.
* Nested FocusGroups create independent navigation scopes. A child group's elements are not navigable from the parent group. Each group maintains its own focus state.
* If a focused element is removed from the tree (unmounted), focus resets to the first focusable element in the group. If the entire group is unmounted, focus is lost.
* Focus restoration: when switching from mouse to controller mode, the framework restores the last focused element per group. If no element was previously focused, it focuses the first focusable element.
* Spatial navigation (D-pad) uses center-point distance with directional filtering. The algorithm prefers elements in the pressed direction and penalizes perpendicular distance. This prevents diagonal jumps when pressing left/right in a vertical list.

---------
SEE ALSO
---------

- Pressable
- TextInput
- Box
- ScrollView
- Modal


========================================
HorizontalBarChart
========================================

Category: Components | Difficulty: beginner | Platforms: love2d, web, terminal, cc, nvim, hs, awesome

Horizontal bar chart component for comparing labeled values side by side

---------
OVERVIEW
---------

HorizontalBarChart renders a series of labeled bars growing left-to-right, each scaled relative to the maximum value in the dataset. Labels appear on the left, the bar fills proportionally, and an optional value readout follows on the right. When interactive mode is enabled, hovering a bar dims all others and reveals a tooltip showing the label, raw value, and percentage of the maximum.

---------
API / SYNTAX
---------

```tsx
import { HorizontalBarChart } from '@reactjit/core';
```

Data shape:
```tsx
interface HorizontalBarChartBar {
  label: string;   // Row label shown on the left
  value: number;   // Numeric value driving bar width
  color?: Color;   // Per-bar override; falls back to the chart-level `color` prop
}
```

Props:
| Prop         | Type                                                              | Default      | Description                                                         |
|--------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------|--------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------|
| data         | HorizontalBarChartBar[]                                           | required     | Array of bars to render                                             |
| width        | number                                                            | 280          | Total width of the chart in pixels                                  |
| barHeight    | number                                                            | 20           | Height of each bar row in pixels                                    |
| gap          | number                                                            | 6            | Vertical gap between bar rows in pixels                             |
| showLabels   | boolean                                                           | true         | Whether to render the label column on the left                      |
| showValues   | boolean                                                           | false        | Whether to render the numeric value after each bar                  |
| labelWidth   | number                                                            | 60           | Fixed pixel width reserved for the label column                     |
| color        | Color                                                             | '#3b82f6'    | Default bar color used when a bar has no per-item `color`           |
| style        | Style                                                             | undefined    | Additional styles applied to the outer container Box                |
| interactive  | boolean                                                           | false        | Enable hover highlighting and tooltips                              |
| onBarHover   | (index: number \| null, bar: HorizontalBarChartBar \| null) => void | undefined  | Called when the pointer enters or leaves a bar                      |
| onBarPress   | (index: number, bar: HorizontalBarChartBar) => void               | undefined    | Called when a bar is clicked (requires interactive: true)           |

---------
EXAMPLES
---------

EXAMPLE: Basic labeled chart
--------
```tsx
import { HorizontalBarChart } from '@reactjit/core';

const languages = [
  { label: 'TypeScript', value: 84 },
  { label: 'Lua',        value: 61 },
  { label: 'Python',     value: 43 },
  { label: 'Rust',       value: 28 },
];

export default function App() {
  return (
    <Box style={{ width: '100%', height: '100%', padding: 20 }}>
      <HorizontalBarChart data={languages} width={320} />
    </Box>
  );
}
```
--------
Platforms: all

EXAMPLE: Per-bar colors with value readout
--------
```tsx
import { HorizontalBarChart } from '@reactjit/core';

const budget = [
  { label: 'Rent',    value: 1500, color: '#ef4444' },
  { label: 'Food',    value: 400,  color: '#f59e0b' },
  { label: 'Transit', value: 120,  color: '#3b82f6' },
  { label: 'Savings', value: 300,  color: '#22c55e' },
];

export default function App() {
  return (
    <Box style={{ width: '100%', height: '100%', padding: 20 }}>
      <HorizontalBarChart
        data={budget}
        width={300}
        barHeight={18}
        showValues
      />
    </Box>
  );
}
```
--------
Platforms: all

EXAMPLE: Interactive with hover callback
--------
```tsx
import { useState } from 'react';
import { HorizontalBarChart } from '@reactjit/core';

const scores = [
  { label: 'Alice', value: 92 },
  { label: 'Bob',   value: 74 },
  { label: 'Carol', value: 88 },
  { label: 'Dave',  value: 55 },
];

export default function App() {
  const [hovered, setHovered] = useState<string | null>(null);

  return (
    <Box style={{ width: '100%', height: '100%', padding: 20, gap: 12 }}>
      <HorizontalBarChart
        data={scores}
        width={300}
        interactive
        onBarHover={(_, bar) => setHovered(bar ? bar.label : null)}
      />
      {hovered && (
        <Text style={{ fontSize: 12, color: '#94a3b8' }}>
          {`Hovering: ${hovered}`}
        </Text>
      )}
    </Box>
  );
}
```
--------
Platforms: love2d, web

---------
CRITICAL RULES
---------

* The `width` prop controls the total chart width; bar fill width is computed from the remaining space after subtracting the label column (`labelWidth + 8`) and value column (40px) when those are shown.
* When `showLabels` is false, `labelWidth` has no effect but the space is still not reserved — set `showLabels={false}` to reclaim that space.
* Per-bar `color` takes priority over the chart-level `color` prop.
* `onBarPress` only fires when `interactive` is also `true`; setting `onBarPress` alone without `interactive` has no effect.
* Empty `data` arrays render a sized empty Box with no error — safe to pass live data that may start empty.
* Do not use Unicode symbols in any `<Text>` within this component or in labels passed as data — use Box-based geometry for visual indicators instead.

---------
SEE ALSO
---------

- BarChart — vertical bars for the same kind of comparison
- StackedBarChart — multi-series bars stacked in a single column
- Sparkline — compact inline trend line with no axes
- ChartTooltip — standalone tooltip primitive used internally by this component


========================================
Image
========================================

Category: Components | Difficulty: beginner | Platforms: love2d, web

Displays images with sizing and objectFit control

---------
OVERVIEW
---------

Image displays an image from a file path or URL. It supports sizing via width/height style properties and content fitting via the objectFit style property. Unlike Box, Image has no children. Unlike Text, Image has no intrinsic size derived from content -- you must specify width and height explicitly or let the parent constrain it.

In web mode, Image renders as an `<img>` element. In native mode, it outputs an `'Image'` host element that the Lua painter draws using love.graphics.draw() with the image loaded from the src path.

---------
API / SYNTAX
---------

```tsx
import { Image } from '@reactjit/core';
```

```tsx
<Image
  src="assets/logo.png"
  style={{ width: 128, height: 128, objectFit: 'contain' }}
/>
```

Props:
| Prop    | Type                         | Default   | Description                          |
|---------|------------------------------|-----------|--------------------------------------|
| src     | string                       | REQUIRED  | Image file path or URL               |
| style   | Style                        | undefined | Sizing and visual styling            |
| onClick | (event: LoveEvent) => void   | undefined | Click/press handler                  |
| onWheel | (event: LoveEvent) => void   | undefined | Mouse wheel handler                  |

objectFit values (set via style.objectFit):
| Value   | Behavior                                                        |
|---------|-----------------------------------------------------------------|
| fill    | Stretch image to fill the element (may distort aspect ratio)    |
| contain | Scale image to fit entirely within the element (letterboxed)    |
| cover   | Scale image to cover the element entirely (may crop)            |
| none    | Display at original size, no scaling                            |

---------
EXAMPLES
---------

EXAMPLE: Basic image with fixed size
--------
```tsx
<Image
  src="assets/icon.png"
  style={{ width: 64, height: 64 }}
/>
```
--------
Platforms: love2d, web

EXAMPLE: Image with objectFit contain
--------
```tsx
<Image
  src="assets/banner.png"
  style={{
    width: '100%',
    height: 200,
    objectFit: 'contain',
    backgroundColor: '#181825',
  }}
/>
```
--------
Platforms: love2d, web

EXAMPLE: Clickable image
--------
```tsx
<Image
  src="assets/button-sprite.png"
  style={{ width: 48, height: 48, borderRadius: 8 }}
  onClick={() => console.log('image clicked')}
/>
```
--------
Platforms: love2d, web

EXAMPLE: Cover image filling a card
--------
```tsx
<Box style={{ width: 300, height: 200, borderRadius: 12, overflow: 'hidden' }}>
  <Image
    src="assets/photo.jpg"
    style={{ width: '100%', height: '100%', objectFit: 'cover' }}
  />
</Box>
```
--------
Platforms: love2d, web

---------
PLATFORM NOTES
---------

Love2D:
Renders as an 'Image' host element. The Lua painter loads the image via love.graphics.newImage() and caches it. The src path is relative to the project root (where main.lua lives). Supported formats: PNG, JPEG, BMP, TGA, and other formats Love2D supports. objectFit is implemented by the painter via quad scaling.

Web:
Renders as an <img> element with display: block. objectFit maps directly to the CSS object-fit property. src can be a relative path, absolute URL, or data URI.

Terminal:
Image is not supported on grid targets. Terminal, ComputerCraft, Neovim, and other character-grid renderers cannot display raster images. Use colored Box elements to approximate pixel art if needed.

---------
CRITICAL RULES
---------

* Image has no intrinsic size. You must provide width and height via the style prop, or constrain the image via a parent Box with known dimensions. An Image without sizing will collapse to zero.
* The src prop is required. Omitting it will produce an error.
* Image paths in Love2D are relative to the project root directory, not the source file. Use paths like "assets/image.png", not "../assets/image.png".
* Image accepts no children. It is a leaf element.

---------
SEE ALSO
---------

- Box
- Text
- Style type reference


========================================
LineChart
========================================

Category: Components | Difficulty: beginner | Platforms: love2d, web, terminal, cc, nvim, hs, awesome

Column-based line chart that plots a series of values as dots with optional area fill

---------
OVERVIEW
---------

LineChart renders a fixed-size canvas divided into equal-width columns, one per data point. Each column positions a dot at the correct vertical height based on its normalized value, with the minimum value mapped to the bottom and the maximum to the top. An optional area fill extends from each dot to the bottom of the chart. When interactive mode is enabled, hovering a column shows a crosshair line and a tooltip with the optional x-label and raw value, while all other dots fade.

---------
API / SYNTAX
---------

```tsx
import { LineChart } from '@reactjit/core';
```

Data shape:
```tsx
interface LineChartPoint {
  x?:    string;  // Optional x-axis label shown in the hover tooltip
  value: number;  // Y-axis value; min and max across the dataset define the scale
}
```

Props:
| Prop          | Type                                                              | Default    | Description                                                           |
|---------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------|------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------|
| data          | LineChartPoint[]                                                  | required   | Array of data points to plot                                          |
| width         | number                                                            | 280        | Total chart width in pixels                                           |
| height        | number                                                            | 120        | Total chart height in pixels                                          |
| color         | Color                                                             | '#3b82f6'  | Color used for dots, area fill, and the hover crosshair               |
| showDots      | boolean                                                           | true       | Whether to render a dot at each data point                            |
| showArea      | boolean                                                           | false      | Whether to render a filled area from the dot down to the baseline     |
| areaOpacity   | number                                                            | 0.3        | Opacity of the area fill (0–1); has no effect when showArea is false  |
| interactive   | boolean                                                           | false      | Enable hover crosshair and per-point tooltips                         |
| onPointHover  | (index: number \| null, point: LineChartPoint \| null) => void    | undefined  | Called when the pointer enters or leaves a column                     |
| onPointPress  | (index: number, point: LineChartPoint) => void                    | undefined  | Called when a column is clicked (requires interactive: true)          |
| style         | Style                                                             | undefined  | Additional styles applied to the outer container Box                  |

---------
EXAMPLES
---------

EXAMPLE: Simple line chart with dots
--------
```tsx
import { LineChart } from '@reactjit/core';

const cpu = [
  { x: '00:00', value: 18 },
  { x: '01:00', value: 22 },
  { x: '02:00', value: 15 },
  { x: '03:00', value: 31 },
  { x: '04:00', value: 44 },
  { x: '05:00', value: 38 },
  { x: '06:00', value: 60 },
  { x: '07:00', value: 75 },
];

export default function App() {
  return (
    <Box style={{ width: '100%', height: '100%', padding: 20 }}>
      <LineChart data={cpu} width={300} height={100} color="#22c55e" />
    </Box>
  );
}
```
--------
Platforms: all

EXAMPLE: Area fill for cumulative data
--------
```tsx
import { LineChart } from '@reactjit/core';

const downloads = [
  { value: 120 }, { value: 145 }, { value: 162 },
  { value: 188 }, { value: 201 }, { value: 230 },
  { value: 215 }, { value: 248 },
];

export default function App() {
  return (
    <Box style={{ width: '100%', height: '100%', padding: 20 }}>
      <LineChart
        data={downloads}
        width={280}
        height={80}
        color="#6366f1"
        showArea
        areaOpacity={0.4}
        showDots={false}
      />
    </Box>
  );
}
```
--------
Platforms: all

EXAMPLE: Interactive with click handler
--------
```tsx
import { useState } from 'react';
import { LineChart } from '@reactjit/core';

const temps = [
  { x: 'Mon', value: 12 }, { x: 'Tue', value: 15 },
  { x: 'Wed', value: 11 }, { x: 'Thu', value: 18 },
  { x: 'Fri', value: 22 }, { x: 'Sat', value: 25 },
  { x: 'Sun', value: 19 },
];

export default function App() {
  const [selected, setSelected] = useState<string | null>(null);

  return (
    <Box style={{ width: '100%', height: '100%', padding: 20, gap: 12 }}>
      <LineChart
        data={temps}
        width={300}
        height={100}
        color="#f59e0b"
        interactive
        onPointPress={(_, point) => setSelected(point.x ?? null)}
      />
      {selected && (
        <Text style={{ fontSize: 12, color: '#94a3b8' }}>
          {`Selected: ${selected}`}
        </Text>
      )}
    </Box>
  );
}
```
--------
Platforms: love2d, web

---------
CRITICAL RULES
---------

* The chart uses column-based rendering: each data point occupies a column of width `width / data.length`. Dots are visually positioned within this column but there are no connecting line segments drawn between them. For a filled area presentation, use AreaChart instead.
* The vertical scale is relative: the minimum value in the dataset maps to the bottom and the maximum to the top. A flat dataset where all values are equal results in all dots at the bottom of their range.
* `onPointPress` only fires when `interactive` is also `true`.
* Hovered dots expand by 2px and non-hovered dots fade to 35% opacity. Hover requires pointer events, which are not available on all targets — check platform support before enabling `interactive`.
* Empty `data` arrays render a sized empty Box safely.
* Do not use Unicode symbols in tooltip labels (`x` field of each point).

---------
SEE ALSO
---------

- AreaChart — convenience wrapper over LineChart with area fill on and dots off by default
- Sparkline — minimal no-configuration trend line for embedding in tight layouts
- BarChart — vertical bars for the same dataset
- ChartTooltip — standalone tooltip primitive used internally by this component


========================================
Mesh
========================================

Category: Components | Difficulty: intermediate | Platforms: love2d

A renderable 3D object — built-in geometry or loaded OBJ model — inside a Scene

---------
OVERVIEW
---------

Mesh is the primary renderable object in a 3D scene. It supports four built-in geometry types (box, cube, sphere, plane) as well as externally loaded OBJ model files. Materials are defined through flat color, image textures, or procedural textures. Mesh supports a UV-based GLSL edge shader for wireframe borders, Phong specular highlights, Fresnel rim glow, and alpha transparency. Position, rotation, and scale are reactive and update each frame without reconciliation overhead.

---------
API / SYNTAX
---------

```tsx
import { Mesh } from '@reactjit/3d';
```

Props:
| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| geometry | 'box' \| 'cube' \| 'sphere' \| 'plane' | — | Built-in geometry type to render |
| model | string | — | Path to an OBJ model file |
| color | string | — | Flat color as a hex string — used when no texture is provided |
| texture | string | — | Path to an image file, or a procedural name such as 'planet' |
| seed | number | — | Seed for procedural textures — different seeds produce different terrain |
| position | [number, number, number] | [0, 0, 0] | Position in 3D world space as [x, y, z] |
| rotation | [number, number, number] | [0, 0, 0] | Euler angles in radians as [rx, ry, rz] |
| scale | number \| [number, number, number] | 1 | Uniform scale as a number, or per-axis scale as [sx, sy, sz] |
| edgeColor | string | — | Hex color for edge/wireframe borders drawn on face edges |
| edgeWidth | number | 0.03 | Edge line width as a fraction of face UV space |
| wireframe | boolean | — | Draw longitude/latitude grid lines — makes spheres visibly 3D |
| gridLines | number | 8 | Number of grid divisions per axis when wireframe is enabled |
| opacity | number | 1 | Opacity from 0 to 1 — values below 1 enable alpha blending |
| specular | number | 32 | Specular shininess power — higher values produce tighter highlights |
| fresnel | number | 0 | Fresnel rim power — 0 disables it, 3–5 produces atmosphere glow |
| unlit | boolean | — | Skip lighting and render with flat texture color only |
| onClick | (event: LoveEvent) => void | — | Click handler (raycasting, Phase 5) |
| onPointerEnter | (event: LoveEvent) => void | — | Pointer enter handler (raycasting, Phase 5) |
| onPointerLeave | (event: LoveEvent) => void | — | Pointer leave handler (raycasting, Phase 5) |

---------
EXAMPLES
---------

EXAMPLE: Colored box with edge borders
--------
```tsx
import { Scene, Camera, Mesh, AmbientLight, DirectionalLight } from '@reactjit/3d';

function ColoredBox() {
  return (
    <Scene style={{ width: 800, height: 600 }}>
      <Camera position={[0, -4, 2]} lookAt={[0, 0, 0]} />
      <AmbientLight color="#1a1a2e" intensity={0.15} />
      <DirectionalLight direction={[1, -1, 1]} color="#ffffff" intensity={1.0} />
      <Mesh
        geometry="box"
        color="#89b4fa"
        edgeColor="#000000"
        edgeWidth={0.03}
        position={[0, 0, 0]}
      />
    </Scene>
  );
}
```
--------
Platforms: love2d

EXAMPLE: Spinning sphere with procedural planet texture
--------
```tsx
import { Scene, Camera, Mesh, AmbientLight, DirectionalLight } from '@reactjit/3d';
import { useAnimationFrame } from '@reactjit/core';

function SpinningPlanet() {
  const [rotation, setRotation] = React.useState<[number, number, number]>([0, 0, 0]);

  useAnimationFrame((dt) => {
    setRotation(([rx, ry, rz]) => [rx, ry + dt * 0.3, rz]);
  });

  return (
    <Scene style={{ width: 800, height: 600 }} stars backgroundColor="#0a0a14">
      <Camera position={[0, -5, 2]} lookAt={[0, 0, 0]} />
      <AmbientLight color="#1a1a2e" intensity={0.1} />
      <DirectionalLight direction={[1, -0.5, 0.5]} color="#fffbe6" intensity={1.2} />
      <Mesh
        geometry="sphere"
        texture="planet"
        seed={7}
        rotation={rotation}
        fresnel={4}
        specular={16}
        wireframe
        gridLines={12}
      />
    </Scene>
  );
}
```
--------
Platforms: love2d

EXAMPLE: Multiple meshes at different positions
--------
```tsx
import { Scene, Camera, Mesh, AmbientLight, DirectionalLight } from '@reactjit/3d';

function MultiMesh() {
  return (
    <Scene style={{ width: 800, height: 600 }}>
      <Camera position={[0, -6, 3]} lookAt={[0, 0, 0]} />
      <AmbientLight intensity={0.2} />
      <DirectionalLight direction={[1, -1, 1]} intensity={1.0} />
      <Mesh geometry="box"    color="#89b4fa" position={[-2, 0, 0]} scale={0.8} />
      <Mesh geometry="sphere" color="#f5c2e7" position={[ 0, 0, 0]} scale={0.8} />
      <Mesh geometry="plane"  color="#a6e3a1" position={[ 2, 0, 0]} scale={[2, 2, 1]} />
    </Scene>
  );
}
```
--------
Platforms: love2d

EXAMPLE: Loading an OBJ model file
--------
```tsx
import { Scene, Camera, Mesh, AmbientLight, DirectionalLight } from '@reactjit/3d';

function ModelViewer() {
  return (
    <Scene style={{ width: 800, height: 600 }} orbitControls>
      <Camera position={[3, -3, 2]} lookAt={[0, 0, 0]} />
      <AmbientLight color="#1a1a2e" intensity={0.2} />
      <DirectionalLight direction={[1, -1, 1]} color="#ffffff" intensity={1.0} />
      <Mesh model="assets/spaceship.obj" scale={0.5} edgeColor="#222222" edgeWidth={0.02} />
    </Scene>
  );
}
```
--------
Platforms: love2d

EXAMPLE: Transparent mesh with Fresnel atmosphere glow
--------
```tsx
import { Scene, Camera, Mesh, AmbientLight, DirectionalLight } from '@reactjit/3d';

function AtmosphereEffect() {
  return (
    <Scene style={{ width: 800, height: 600 }} stars backgroundColor="#020208">
      <Camera position={[0, -5, 0]} lookAt={[0, 0, 0]} />
      <AmbientLight color="#000814" intensity={0.05} />
      <DirectionalLight direction={[1, -1, 0.3]} color="#fffbe6" intensity={1.5} />
      {/* Solid planet */}
      <Mesh geometry="sphere" texture="planet" seed={3} specular={24} />
      {/* Transparent atmosphere shell */}
      <Mesh
        geometry="sphere"
        color="#7eb3ff"
        scale={1.05}
        opacity={0.25}
        fresnel={5}
        unlit
      />
    </Scene>
  );
}
```
--------
Platforms: love2d

---------
CRITICAL RULES
---------

* Mesh renders nothing on web targets and returns null — it is Love2D only
* Provide either geometry or model, not both; the behavior when both are set is undefined
* color is ignored when texture is set — texture takes precedence for the surface material
* edgeColor and wireframe are independent features: edgeColor draws borders on cube/box faces via a UV shader; wireframe draws lat/lon grid lines and is most useful on spheres
* The fresnel prop controls alpha at glancing angles — it requires opacity to be less than 1 to be visible as transparency
* scale as a single number applies uniformly to all axes; scale as [sx, sy, sz] scales each axis independently
* rotation values are in radians, not degrees
* onClick, onPointerEnter, and onPointerLeave are Phase 5 raycasting features and may not be available in all versions

---------
SEE ALSO
---------

- Scene — the 3D viewport that Mesh must be placed inside
- Camera — defines the viewpoint that sees Mesh geometry
- AmbientLight — provides base illumination for Mesh shading
- DirectionalLight — provides directional shading and specular highlights on Mesh


========================================
Modal
========================================

Category: Components | Difficulty: intermediate | Platforms: love2d, web

Fullscreen overlay with backdrop, animations, and dismiss handling

---------
OVERVIEW
---------

Modal displays content in a fullscreen overlay that sits above the rest of the UI. It renders through the Portal system, ensuring correct z-index layering regardless of where it appears in the component tree. Modal supports a customizable backdrop (color, transparency, click-to-dismiss), simple enter/exit animations (none, fade, slide), and Escape key dismissal.

Modal is a controlled component -- you manage visibility via the `visible` prop and handle close events via `onRequestClose`. The backdrop press and Escape key both trigger onRequestClose; the component does not close itself. This gives you full control over dismiss conditions (e.g., confirmation dialogs that ignore backdrop clicks).

---------
API / SYNTAX
---------

```tsx
import { Modal } from '@reactjit/core';
```

```tsx
const [showModal, setShowModal] = useState(false);

<Modal
  visible={showModal}
  onRequestClose={() => setShowModal(false)}
  animationType="fade"
>
  <Box style={{ width: 300, padding: 24, backgroundColor: '#1e1e2e', borderRadius: 12 }}>
    <Text style={{ fontSize: 18, color: '#cdd6f4' }}>Dialog Title</Text>
    <Pressable onPress={() => setShowModal(false)}>
      <Text style={{ fontSize: 14, color: '#89b4fa' }}>Close</Text>
    </Pressable>
  </Box>
</Modal>
```

Props:
| Prop            | Type                            | Default       | Description                                    |
|-----------------|---------------------------------|---------------|------------------------------------------------|
| visible         | boolean                         | REQUIRED      | Show or hide the modal                         |
| onRequestClose  | () => void                      | undefined     | Called on Escape key or backdrop press          |
| onShow          | () => void                      | undefined     | Called when modal finishes appearing            |
| animationType   | 'none' | 'fade' | 'slide'       | 'none'        | Enter/exit animation style                     |
| transparent     | boolean                         | false         | Use transparent backdrop (overrides color)      |
| backdropColor   | Color                           | [0,0,0,0.5]   | Backdrop color (RGBA or CSS string)            |
| backdropDismiss | boolean                         | true          | Whether backdrop press triggers onRequestClose |
| children        | ReactNode                       | REQUIRED      | Modal content                                  |
| style           | Style                           | undefined     | Applied to the content wrapper                 |

Animation types:
| Type  | Enter behavior                   | Exit behavior                    |
|-------|----------------------------------|----------------------------------|
| none  | Instant appear                   | Instant disappear                |
| fade  | Fades in from transparent        | Fades out to transparent         |
| slide | Slides up from below             | Slides down to below             |

Animation duration is 200ms for both enter and exit.

---------
EXAMPLES
---------

EXAMPLE: Basic confirmation dialog
--------
```tsx
const [show, setShow] = useState(false);

<Pressable onPress={() => setShow(true)}>
  <Text style={{ fontSize: 14, color: '#89b4fa' }}>Delete item</Text>
</Pressable>

<Modal visible={show} onRequestClose={() => setShow(false)} animationType="fade">
  <Box style={{
    width: 320,
    padding: 24,
    backgroundColor: '#1e1e2e',
    borderRadius: 12,
    gap: 16,
  }}>
    <Text style={{ fontSize: 18, fontWeight: 'bold', color: '#cdd6f4' }}>
      Confirm Delete
    </Text>
    <Text style={{ fontSize: 14, color: '#a6adc8' }}>
      Are you sure? This cannot be undone.
    </Text>
    <Box style={{ flexDirection: 'row', justifyContent: 'end', gap: 12, width: '100%' }}>
      <Pressable onPress={() => setShow(false)}>
        <Text style={{ fontSize: 14, color: '#a6adc8' }}>Cancel</Text>
      </Pressable>
      <Pressable onPress={() => { handleDelete(); setShow(false); }}>
        <Text style={{ fontSize: 14, color: '#f38ba8' }}>Delete</Text>
      </Pressable>
    </Box>
  </Box>
</Modal>
```
--------
Platforms: love2d, web

EXAMPLE: Non-dismissable modal (ignores backdrop click)
--------
```tsx
<Modal
  visible={isProcessing}
  backdropDismiss={false}
  backdropColor={[0, 0, 0, 0.8]}
>
  <Box style={{ padding: 32, backgroundColor: '#1e1e2e', borderRadius: 12, alignItems: 'center' }}>
    <Text style={{ fontSize: 16, color: '#cdd6f4' }}>Processing...</Text>
  </Box>
</Modal>
```
--------
Platforms: love2d, web

EXAMPLE: Transparent overlay
--------
```tsx
<Modal visible={showTooltip} transparent onRequestClose={() => setShowTooltip(false)}>
  <Box style={{
    position: 'absolute',
    top: 100,
    left: 200,
    padding: 8,
    backgroundColor: '#313244',
    borderRadius: 4,
  }}>
    <Text style={{ fontSize: 12, color: '#cdd6f4' }}>Tooltip content</Text>
  </Box>
</Modal>
```
--------
Platforms: love2d, web

EXAMPLE: Slide-up modal with onShow callback
--------
```tsx
<Modal
  visible={showPanel}
  onRequestClose={() => setShowPanel(false)}
  onShow={() => console.log('modal fully visible')}
  animationType="slide"
>
  <Box style={{
    width: '100%',
    height: '50%',
    backgroundColor: '#1e1e2e',
    borderRadius: 16,
    padding: 24,
  }}>
    <Text style={{ fontSize: 20, color: '#cdd6f4' }}>Bottom Sheet</Text>
  </Box>
</Modal>
```
--------
Platforms: love2d, web

---------
PLATFORM NOTES
---------

Love2D:
Modal renders through the Portal system which places content at the top of the instance tree for z-index layering. The backdrop is a full-screen Pressable that intercepts clicks. Escape key handling is wired through the Box onKeyDown handler in native mode. Animations are stepped (4 steps over 200ms) rather than continuous -- adequate for modal transitions but not smooth enough for complex animation needs.

Web:
Uses the Portal system (React portal to a PortalHost). The backdrop prevents body scroll by setting document.body.style.overflow = 'hidden'. Escape key handler uses document.addEventListener('keydown'). CSS transitions are used for fade/slide animations in addition to the stepped state machine. The web renderer restores the original body overflow on unmount.

Terminal:
Not available on grid targets. For modal-like behavior on character-grid renderers, overlay a view with higher z-index and handle input routing manually.

---------
CRITICAL RULES
---------

* Modal is controlled: it will not close itself. You must update the `visible` prop to false in your onRequestClose handler.
* The `children` prop is required. A Modal with no children renders an empty overlay with just the backdrop.
* Do not conditionally render Modal itself based on visibility -- always render it and control with `visible`. The component handles its own mount/unmount animation lifecycle internally.
