  name         string               Human-readable name for the server (used in error messages)
  transport    MCPTransportType     'stdio' | 'sse' | 'streamable-http'
  command      string?              (stdio) Command to spawn (e.g. 'npx')
  args         string[]?            (stdio) Arguments to the command
  env          Record<string,string>? (stdio) Extra environment variables
  url          string?              (sse / streamable-http) Server endpoint URL
  headers      Record<string,string>? (sse / streamable-http) Extra request headers
  timeout      number?              Request timeout in ms (default: 30000)
  permissions  MCPPermissionsConfig?  Allowlist of tools to expose (required — no permissions = no tools)
  onConfirm    (toolName: string, args: any) => Promise<boolean>?  Gate for tools with confirm: true

MCPPermissionsConfig shape:
  tools   Record<string, MCPToolPermission>   Tool name → permission entry
  lastDiscovered   string?                    ISO timestamp of last tool discovery
  tokenBudget      { totalIfAllEnabled: number; note: string }?

MCPToolPermission fields:
  enabled        boolean   Whether to expose this tool to the model
  confirm        boolean   Whether to call onConfirm before executing
  description    string?   Override the tool description shown to the model
  inputSchema    Record<string, any>?   Override the tool's input schema
  tokenEstimate  number?   Pre-computed token cost of this tool's definition

Returns: MCPServerResult
  status          'connecting' | 'ready' | 'error'   Current connection state
  tools           ToolDefinition[]                    Enabled tools, ready for useChat
  availableTools  string[]                            All tool names discovered from the server (regardless of permissions)
  error           Error | null
  disconnect      () => void                          Manually close the connection

--- MCPClient ---

Low-level MCP client class. Used by useMCPServer internally and available for imperative use cases such as the CLI linter, build-time tool discovery, or custom React effects.

  import { MCPClient } from '@reactjit/ai';

  const client = new MCPClient(config: MCPServerConfig);

Methods:
  connect()                                       Connect and complete the initialize handshake
  listTools(): Promise<MCPTool[]>                 Discover all tools from the server
  callTool(name, args): Promise<MCPToolCallResult>  Call a named tool
  close()                                         Close the connection
  getServerInfo()                                 Return the server name and version from the handshake

Properties:
  status  MCPClientStatus   'disconnected' | 'connecting' | 'ready' | 'error'
  error   Error | null

MCPTool fields:
  name         string
  description  string
  inputSchema  Record<string, any>   JSON Schema for arguments

--- estimateToolTokens / estimateToolBudget ---

Estimate the context window cost of tool definitions before passing them to a model. Useful for deciding which MCP tools to enable when working with tight context budgets.

  import { estimateToolTokens, estimateToolBudget } from '@reactjit/ai';

  const tokens: number = estimateToolTokens(tool: MCPTool);
  const budget: { total: number; note: string } = estimateToolBudget(tools: MCPTool[]);

estimateToolTokens:
  Parameters: tool  MCPTool
  Returns: number — estimated token count for that tool's definition

estimateToolBudget:
  Parameters: tools  MCPTool[]
  Returns: { total: number; note: string } — total tokens and a human-readable percentage of a 128K context window

---------
SEE ALSO
---------

- useFetch — plain HTTP requests without the LLM layer
- useWebSocket — raw WebSocket connections for custom streaming
- useLoveRPC — low-level Lua bridge used internally by the browse integration
- StorageProvider — required ancestor for useAPIKeys persistence across sessions


========================================
Audio Hooks
========================================

Category: Hooks | Difficulty: intermediate | Platforms: love2d

Hooks for building modular synthesizers, samplers, drum machines, sequencers, and MIDI controllers in Love2D.

---------
OVERVIEW
---------

The @reactjit/audio package exposes a modular synthesis system as React hooks. Audio modules (oscillators, filters, envelopes, mixers, samplers, clocks, sequencers) are wired together into a signal graph called the rack. State flows from the Lua audio engine into React via bridge events; commands flow back via RPC. MIDI input is also surfaced through this package, including device enumeration, CC-to-parameter mapping, and a learn mode for real-time controller assignment. The package also provides sample file loading, microphone recording, a sample-accurate BPM clock, and a step sequencer for building drum machines and other rhythmic applications.

---------
API / SYNTAX
---------

--- useAudioInit ---

Initialize the audio engine. Call this once at the root of your app before using any other audio hooks. Returns true when the engine is ready. Resolves even if audio hardware is unavailable, so the UI always renders.

  import { useAudioInit } from '@reactjit/audio';

  const ready: boolean = useAudioInit();

Parameters: none
Returns: boolean — true once the engine has initialized (or failed gracefully)

--- useRack ---

Access the full audio rack: the list of active modules, the connections between them, and operations to mutate the graph.

  import { useRack } from '@reactjit/audio';

  const rack: UseRackResult = useRack();

Parameters: none

Returns: UseRackResult
  modules       ModuleState[]   All modules currently in the rack
  connections   Connection[]    All active port connections
  addModule     (type: string, id: string, params?: Record<string, any>) => Promise<any>
  removeModule  (id: string) => Promise<any>
  connect       (fromId: string, fromPort: string, toId: string, toPort: string) => Promise<any>
  disconnect    (fromId: string, fromPort: string, toId: string, toPort: string) => Promise<any>

ModuleState fields:
  id           string
  type         string
  params       Record<string, any>
  ports        Record<string, { type: PortType; direction: PortDirection }>
  activeNotes  Record<string, { note: number; envelope: number }> | undefined

Connection fields:
  fromId    string
  fromPort  string
  toId      string
  toPort    string
  type      PortType   ('audio' | 'control' | 'midi')

--- useModule ---

Access and control a single module by its ID. Subscribes to rack state updates and surfaces only the named module's params and ports.

  import { useModule } from '@reactjit/audio';

  const mod: UseModuleResult = useModule(moduleId: string);

Parameters:
  moduleId  string  The ID assigned to the module when it was added

Returns: UseModuleResult
  id           string
  type         string
  params       Record<string, any>
  ports        Record<string, { type: PortType; direction: PortDirection }>
  activeNotes  Record<string, { note: number; envelope: number }> | undefined
  setParam     (name: string, value: any) => Promise<any>

--- useParam ---

Read and write a single parameter on a module. Provides an optimistic update: the local value is set immediately while the RPC call propagates to Lua.

  import { useParam } from '@reactjit/audio';

  const [value, setParam] = useParam(moduleId: string, paramName: string);

Parameters:
  moduleId   string  Module to target
  paramName  string  Parameter name on that module

Returns: [any, (value: any) => Promise<any>]
  [0]  Current parameter value (null until first rack state event)
  [1]  Setter — sends RPC and applies optimistic update

--- useMIDI ---

Access connected MIDI devices, current CC mappings, learn mode state, and operations to add or remove mappings.

  import { useMIDI } from '@reactjit/audio';

  const midi: UseMIDIResult = useMIDI();

Parameters: none

Returns: UseMIDIResult
  available  boolean          Whether a MIDI subsystem is available
  devices    MIDIDevice[]     Connected devices
  mappings   MIDIMapping[]    Active CC-to-parameter mappings
  learning   { moduleId: string; param: string } | null   Active learn target, or null
  learn      (moduleId: string, param: string) => Promise<any>   Arm learn mode
  map        (moduleId: string, param: string, channel: number, cc: number) => Promise<any>
  unmap      (moduleId: string, param: string) => Promise<any>

MIDIDevice fields:
  id         string
  name       string
  connected  boolean

MIDIMapping fields:
  channel   number
  cc        number
  moduleId  string
  param     string

--- useMIDINote ---

Subscribe to raw MIDI note on/off events from any connected device. The handler is stable — reassigning it between renders does not re-subscribe.

  import { useMIDINote } from '@reactjit/audio';

  useMIDINote(handler: (event: MIDINoteEvent) => void);

Parameters:
  handler  (event: MIDINoteEvent) => void

Returns: void

MIDINoteEvent fields:
  note      number   MIDI note number (0–127)
  velocity  number   Velocity (0–127; 0 on note-off)
  on        boolean  true = note on, false = note off
  channel   number   MIDI channel (1–16)
  device    string   Device ID

--- useMIDICC ---

Subscribe to raw MIDI CC (continuous controller) events from any connected device.

  import { useMIDICC } from '@reactjit/audio';

  useMIDICC(handler: (event: MIDICCEvent) => void);

Parameters:
  handler  (event: MIDICCEvent) => void

Returns: void

MIDICCEvent fields:
  cc       number   CC number (0–127)
  value    number   CC value (0–127)
  channel  number   MIDI channel (1–16)
  device   string   Device ID

--- useClock ---

Access a clock module's beat position and transport controls. The clock runs sample-accurate in the Lua audio engine. Position updates arrive via the 30fps state push.

  import { useClock } from '@reactjit/audio';

  const clock: UseClockResult = useClock(moduleId: string);

Parameters:
  moduleId  string  The clock module's ID

Returns: UseClockResult
  beat         number   Current beat within the bar (0–3)
  bar          number   Current bar number
  step         number   Current step (depends on division)
  phase        number   Fractional position within current step (0–1)
  running      boolean  Whether the clock is ticking
  bpm          number   Current tempo
  start        () => Promise<any>   Start the clock
  stop         () => Promise<any>   Stop the clock
  setBpm       (bpm: number) => Promise<any>   Set tempo
  setDivision  (division: string) => Promise<any>   Set division ("1/4", "1/8", "1/16", "1/32")
  setSwing     (swing: number) => Promise<any>   Set swing amount (0–1)

--- useClockEvent ---

Subscribe to clock tick events. Fires on each tick at the audio engine's rate. The handler is stable across re-renders.

  import { useClockEvent } from '@reactjit/audio';

  useClockEvent(handler: (event: ClockTickEvent) => void);

Parameters:
  handler  (event: ClockTickEvent) => void

Returns: void

ClockTickEvent fields:
  beat  number  Beat within bar
  bar   number  Bar number
  step  number  Step number
  bpm   number  Current tempo

--- useSampler ---

Manage a sampler module's slots: load audio files, clear slots, trigger playback. Samples are loaded from the Love2D filesystem. MIDI note 36 = slot 1, 37 = slot 2, etc. (GM drum map starting at C2).

  import { useSampler } from '@reactjit/audio';

  const sampler: UseSamplerResult = useSampler(moduleId: string);

Parameters:
  moduleId  string  The sampler module's ID

Returns: UseSamplerResult
  slots        Record<number, SampleSlot | null>  Loaded sample info per slot (1–16)
  voices       SamplerVoice[]                     Currently playing voices
  loadSample   (slot: number, path: string, mode?: 'oneshot' | 'loop') => Promise<any>
  clearSample  (slot: number) => Promise<any>
  trigger      (slot: number, velocity?: number) => Promise<any>

SampleSlot fields:
  name        string   Filename of the loaded sample
  duration    number   Duration in seconds
  sampleRate  number   Original sample rate
  mode        string   "oneshot" or "loop"

SamplerVoice fields:
  slot      number  Which slot is playing
  position  number  Current playback position in seconds
  duration  number  Total duration of the sample

--- useRecorder ---

Record audio from a microphone or input device into a sampler slot. Uses Love2D's RecordingDevice API. Call listDevices() to enumerate available inputs, startRecording() to begin capture, and stopRecording() to finalize the sample into its target slot.

  import { useRecorder } from '@reactjit/audio';

  const recorder: UseRecorderResult = useRecorder();

Parameters: none

Returns: UseRecorderResult
  devices         AudioRecordingDevice[]  Available recording devices
  recording       RecordingState          Current recording status
  listDevices     () => Promise<any>      Enumerate available devices
  startRecording  (moduleId: string, slot: number, deviceIndex?: number) => Promise<any>
  stopRecording   () => Promise<any>

AudioRecordingDevice fields:
  index  number  Device index (1-based)
  name   string  Device name

RecordingState fields:
  active    boolean        Whether recording is in progress
  moduleId  string | null  Target sampler module
  slot      number | null  Target slot
  device    string | null  Recording device name
  duration  number         Elapsed recording time in seconds

--- useSequencer ---

Control a step sequencer module's pattern and track assignments. The sequencer advances on rising edges from a connected clock module's gate output. Each track targets a different module for note triggering.

  import { useSequencer } from '@reactjit/audio';

  const seq: UseSequencerResult = useSequencer(moduleId: string);

Parameters:
  moduleId  string  The sequencer module's ID

Returns: UseSequencerResult
  pattern         Record<string, Record<string, StepData>>  Pattern grid [track][step]
  currentStep     number                                     Current step position
  trackTargets    Record<string, string>                     Track → target module ID
  setStep         (track: number, step: number, active: boolean, note?: number, velocity?: number) => Promise<any>
  setTrackTarget  (track: number, targetModuleId: string) => Promise<any>
  clearPattern    () => Promise<any>

StepData fields:
  active    boolean  Whether this step fires
  note      number   MIDI note number (default 36)
  velocity  number   Velocity (default 100)

---------
SEE ALSO
---------

- useLoveRPC — low-level RPC bridge used internally by all audio hooks
- useLoveEvent — low-level event subscription used internally by all audio hooks


========================================
Crypto Hooks
========================================

Category: Hooks | Difficulty: intermediate | Platforms: love2d, web, terminal, cc, nvim, hs, awesome

Audited cryptographic primitives — hashing, symmetric encryption, digital signatures, key exchange, and secure token generation — built on @noble/hashes, @noble/ciphers, and @noble/curves.

---------
OVERVIEW
---------

The @reactjit/crypto package exposes pure-JavaScript cryptographic primitives that work across every ReactJIT target, including QuickJS inside Love2D. All implementations come from the @noble suite — a set of audited, dependency-free libraries that are widely used in production (millions of weekly downloads, no native code). The package is organized into four functional groups: hashing (sha256, sha512, BLAKE2b/2s/3, HMAC), symmetric encryption (AES-256-GCM, ChaCha20-Poly1305 with scrypt KDF), asymmetric signatures/key exchange (Ed25519, X25519), and token generation. Encoding utilities (hex, base64) are also re-exported. None of these are React hooks — they are synchronous or Promise-returning functions you call directly from hooks, effects, or event handlers.

---------
API / SYNTAX
---------

--- sha256 ---

Compute a SHA-256 digest.

  import { sha256 } from '@reactjit/crypto';

  const result: HashResult = sha256(input: string | Uint8Array);

Parameters:
  input  string | Uint8Array   Data to hash (strings are UTF-8 encoded)

Returns: HashResult
  hex    string       Lowercase hex digest (64 chars)
  base64 string       Base64-encoded digest
  bytes  Uint8Array   Raw 32-byte digest

--- sha512 ---

Compute a SHA-512 digest.

  import { sha512 } from '@reactjit/crypto';

  const result: HashResult = sha512(input: string | Uint8Array);

Parameters:
  input  string | Uint8Array

Returns: HashResult (128-char hex, 64 raw bytes)

--- hash_blake2b ---

BLAKE2b with configurable output length. Default: 32 bytes.

  import { hash_blake2b } from '@reactjit/crypto';

  const result: HashResult = hash_blake2b(input: string | Uint8Array, bytes?: number);

Parameters:
  input  string | Uint8Array
  bytes  number   Output length in bytes. Default: 32. Range: 1–64.

Returns: HashResult

--- hash_blake2s ---

BLAKE2s (always 32 bytes). Faster than BLAKE2b on 32-bit platforms.

  import { hash_blake2s } from '@reactjit/crypto';

  const result: HashResult = hash_blake2s(input: string | Uint8Array);

Parameters:
  input  string | Uint8Array

Returns: HashResult

--- hash_blake3 ---

BLAKE3 with configurable output length. Default: 32 bytes. Fastest of the three BLAKE variants.

  import { hash_blake3 } from '@reactjit/crypto';

  const result: HashResult = hash_blake3(input: string | Uint8Array, bytes?: number);

Parameters:
  input  string | Uint8Array
  bytes  number   Output length in bytes. Default: 32.

Returns: HashResult

--- hmacSHA256 ---

HMAC-SHA256. Use for message authentication, API request signing, and webhook signature generation.

  import { hmacSHA256 } from '@reactjit/crypto';

  const result: HashResult = hmacSHA256(key: string | Uint8Array, message: string | Uint8Array);

Parameters:
  key      string | Uint8Array   Shared secret
  message  string | Uint8Array   Message to authenticate

Returns: HashResult (64-char hex)

--- hmacSHA512 ---

HMAC-SHA512. Same semantics as hmacSHA256 with a 128-char hex output.

  import { hmacSHA512 } from '@reactjit/crypto';

  const result: HashResult = hmacSHA512(key: string | Uint8Array, message: string | Uint8Array);

Returns: HashResult

--- timingSafeEqual ---

Compare two byte sequences in constant time. Safe for comparing secrets — the comparison time does not depend on where the first difference appears.

  import { timingSafeEqual } from '@reactjit/crypto';

  const equal: boolean = timingSafeEqual(a: string | Uint8Array, b: string | Uint8Array);

Parameters:
  a  string | Uint8Array
  b  string | Uint8Array

Returns: boolean — false immediately (with length leak) if lengths differ; otherwise constant-time value comparison.

--- encrypt ---

Password-based authenticated encryption using scrypt KDF + AES-256-GCM (default) or ChaCha20-Poly1305. Returns a JSON-serializable EncryptedData envelope that bundles the algorithm, ciphertext, nonce, salt, and KDF parameters together so the receiver does not need to remember them separately.

  import { encrypt } from '@reactjit/crypto';

  const envelope: EncryptedData = encrypt(plaintext: string, password: string, options?: EncryptOptions);

Parameters:
  plaintext  string          Data to encrypt
  password   string          User-provided password (derived to a 256-bit key via scrypt)
  options    EncryptOptions  Optional configuration

EncryptOptions fields:
  algorithm  'aes-256-gcm' | 'chacha20-poly1305' | 'xchacha20-poly1305'
             Default: 'aes-256-gcm'. Choose chacha20-poly1305 on platforms without hardware AES acceleration.

Returns: EncryptedData
  algorithm   'aes-256-gcm' | 'chacha20-poly1305' | 'xchacha20-poly1305'
  ciphertext  string   Base64-encoded ciphertext (includes GCM/Poly1305 authentication tag)
  nonce       string   Base64-encoded nonce/IV
  salt        string   Base64-encoded 32-byte salt
  kdf         'scrypt'
  kdfParams   { N, r, p }   scrypt cost parameters (N=131072, r=8, p=1 by default)

--- decrypt ---

Decrypt an EncryptedData envelope produced by encrypt. Reads KDF parameters from the envelope, re-derives the key, and authenticates + decrypts.

  import { decrypt } from '@reactjit/crypto';

  const plaintext: string = decrypt(data: EncryptedData, password: string);

Parameters:
  data      EncryptedData   Envelope from encrypt (or stored JSON)
  password  string          The same password used to encrypt

Returns: string — decrypted plaintext
Throws on wrong password (authentication tag mismatch) or malformed input.

--- encryptRaw ---

Encrypt raw bytes with a caller-managed 32-byte key. No KDF step. Use this when you already have a key from a Diffie-Hellman exchange or another key derivation flow.

  import { encryptRaw } from '@reactjit/crypto';

  const { ciphertext, nonce } = encryptRaw(
    plaintext: Uint8Array,
    key: Uint8Array,
    algo?: 'aes-256-gcm' | 'chacha20-poly1305' | 'xchacha20-poly1305',
  );

Parameters:
  plaintext  Uint8Array   Data to encrypt
  key        Uint8Array   32-byte key
  algo       string       Encryption algorithm. Default: 'aes-256-gcm'

Returns: { ciphertext: Uint8Array, nonce: Uint8Array }

--- decryptRaw ---

Decrypt raw bytes with a caller-managed key and nonce.

  import { decryptRaw } from '@reactjit/crypto';

  const plaintext: Uint8Array = decryptRaw(
    ciphertext: Uint8Array,
    key: Uint8Array,
    nonce: Uint8Array,
    algo?: 'aes-256-gcm' | 'chacha20-poly1305' | 'xchacha20-poly1305',
  );

Parameters:
  ciphertext  Uint8Array
  key         Uint8Array   32-byte key
  nonce       Uint8Array   Nonce used during encryption
  algo        string       Must match the algorithm used during encryption. Default: 'aes-256-gcm'

Returns: Uint8Array — decrypted bytes
Throws on authentication failure.

--- generateSigningKeys ---

Generate an Ed25519 key pair for digital signatures.

  import { generateSigningKeys } from '@reactjit/crypto';

  const keys: KeyPair = generateSigningKeys();

Parameters: none

Returns: KeyPair
  publicKey   string   Hex-encoded Ed25519 public key (64 chars)
  privateKey  string   Hex-encoded Ed25519 private key (64 chars)
  curve       'ed25519'

--- sign ---

Sign a message string with an Ed25519 private key.

  import { sign } from '@reactjit/crypto';

  const signed: SignedMessage = sign(privateKeyHex: string, message: string);

Parameters:
  privateKeyHex  string   Hex-encoded private key from generateSigningKeys
  message        string   Message to sign

Returns: SignedMessage
  message    string   The original message
  signature  string   Hex-encoded Ed25519 signature (128 chars)
  publicKey  string   Hex-encoded public key derived from the private key
  algorithm  'ed25519'

--- verify ---

Verify a SignedMessage produced by sign.

  import { verify } from '@reactjit/crypto';

  const valid: boolean = verify(signed: SignedMessage);

Parameters:
  signed  SignedMessage   Envelope from sign (message + signature + publicKey)

Returns: boolean

--- verifyDetached ---

Verify a detached signature (when you have the three components separately rather than a SignedMessage envelope).

  import { verifyDetached } from '@reactjit/crypto';

  const valid: boolean = verifyDetached(
    message: string,
    signatureHex: string,
    publicKeyHex: string,
  );

Parameters:
  message       string   Original message
  signatureHex  string   Hex-encoded signature
  publicKeyHex  string   Hex-encoded public key

Returns: boolean

--- generateDHKeys ---

Generate an X25519 key pair for Diffie-Hellman key exchange.

  import { generateDHKeys } from '@reactjit/crypto';

  const keys: KeyPair = generateDHKeys();

Parameters: none

Returns: KeyPair
  publicKey   string   Hex-encoded X25519 public key (64 chars)
  privateKey  string   Hex-encoded X25519 private key (64 chars)
  curve       'x25519'

--- diffieHellman ---

Compute a shared secret from your X25519 private key and the other party's public key. Both sides derive the same secret without transmitting it.

  import { diffieHellman } from '@reactjit/crypto';

  const sharedSecret: string = diffieHellman(privateKeyHex: string, publicKeyHex: string);

Parameters:
  privateKeyHex  string   Your hex-encoded X25519 private key
  publicKeyHex   string   The other party's hex-encoded X25519 public key

Returns: string — hex-encoded 32-byte shared secret

--- randomBytes ---

Generate cryptographically random bytes. Re-exported from @noble/hashes.

  import { randomBytes } from '@reactjit/crypto';

  const bytes: Uint8Array = randomBytes(length: number);

Parameters:
  length  number   Number of bytes

Returns: Uint8Array

--- randomToken ---

Generate a cryptographically random hex string. Use for API keys, session IDs, and CSRF tokens.

  import { randomToken } from '@reactjit/crypto';

  const token: string = randomToken(bytes?: number);

Parameters:
  bytes  number   Byte length. Default: 32 (produces a 64-char hex string)

Returns: string — lowercase hex (length = bytes * 2)

--- randomBase64 ---

Generate a cryptographically random base64 string.

  import { randomBase64 } from '@reactjit/crypto';

  const token: string = randomBase64(bytes?: number);

Parameters:
  bytes  number   Byte length. Default: 32 (produces ~44 base64 chars)

Returns: string

--- randomId ---

Generate a cryptographically random alphanumeric string. URL-safe with no special characters.

  import { randomId } from '@reactjit/crypto';

  const id: string = randomId(length?: number);

Parameters:
  length  number   Character length of the output. Default: 16

Returns: string — alphanumeric characters only (A-Z, a-z, 0-9)

--- Encoding utilities ---

  import { toHex, fromHex, toBase64, fromBase64 } from '@reactjit/crypto';

  toHex(bytes: Uint8Array): string            — Uint8Array to lowercase hex
  fromHex(hex: string): Uint8Array            — hex string to Uint8Array
  toBase64(bytes: Uint8Array): string         — Uint8Array to base64 string
  fromBase64(base64: string): Uint8Array      — base64 string to Uint8Array

---------
SEE ALSO
---------

- useWebhook — uses hmacSHA256 for incoming webhook signature verification
- useStorage — pairs well with encrypt/decrypt for persisting sensitive settings
- useFetch — for authenticated API requests where you need to sign request bodies


========================================
Media Hooks
========================================

Category: Hooks | Difficulty: intermediate | Platforms: love2d

Hooks for scanning media libraries, listing archive contents, reading files from inside archives, and deep-indexing directories.

---------
OVERVIEW
---------

The @reactjit/media package provides two sets of hooks: archive hooks for working with compressed files (RAR, ZIP, 7z, TAR, and others), and media library hooks for scanning directories of media files. The archive hooks delegate to a Lua-side libarchive integration; the scan hooks walk the filesystem. useMediaIndex combines both — it looks inside archives to surface their contents as first-class MediaFile entries alongside regular files.

---------
API / SYNTAX
---------

--- useArchive ---

List all entries inside an archive. Fetches automatically when filepath changes. Provides a refresh function for manual re-fetching.

  import { useArchive } from '@reactjit/media';

  const { entries, loading, error, refresh } = useArchive(filepath: string | null);

Parameters:
  filepath  string | null  Absolute path to the archive file. Pass null to idle.

Returns:
  entries   ArchiveEntry[]  All entries in the archive (files, directories, symlinks)
  loading   boolean
  error     string | null
  refresh   () => Promise<void>   Re-fetch the entry list

ArchiveEntry fields:
  path       string                        Path within the archive
  size       number                        Size in bytes
  type       'file' | 'directory' | 'symlink'
  mtime      number                        Modification timestamp (Unix seconds)
  encrypted  boolean

--- useArchiveInfo ---

Fetch a summary of an archive without listing every entry. Returns counts, total size, and an extension histogram.

  import { useArchiveInfo } from '@reactjit/media';

  const { info, loading, error } = useArchiveInfo(filepath: string | null);

Parameters:
  filepath  string | null

Returns:
  info     ArchiveInfo | null
  loading  boolean
  error    string | null

ArchiveInfo fields:
  totalEntries  number
  totalSize     number                        Total uncompressed bytes
  fileCount     number
  dirCount      number
  extensions    Record<string, number>        Counts per extension, e.g. { mp4: 3, srt: 3 }

--- useArchiveRead ---

Returns an imperative function that reads a single file from inside an archive. Call it on demand rather than on mount. Throws on error.

  import { useArchiveRead } from '@reactjit/media';

  const read = useArchiveRead();
  const { content, size } = await read(filepath, entryPath, maxBytes?);

Parameters (of the returned function):
  filepath   string   Absolute path to the archive
  entryPath  string   Path of the entry within the archive
  maxBytes   number   (optional) Byte limit on the returned content

Returns: Promise<{ content: string; size: number }>
  content  string   File content as a string
  size     number   Full entry size in bytes (may exceed maxBytes if truncated)

Throws: Error with message from Lua if the entry cannot be read.

--- useArchiveSearch ---

Search archive entries by a Lua pattern. Fetches automatically when filepath or pattern changes.

  import { useArchiveSearch } from '@reactjit/media';

  const { matches, loading } = useArchiveSearch(filepath: string | null, pattern: string);

Parameters:
  filepath  string | null  Archive to search. Pass null to idle.
  pattern   string         Lua pattern matched against each entry path (e.g. '%.mp4$')

Returns:
  matches  ArchiveEntry[]
  loading  boolean

--- useMediaLibrary ---

Scan a directory for media files. Returns typed MediaFile entries with source set to 'filesystem'. Does not look inside archives; use useMediaIndex for that.

  import { useMediaLibrary } from '@reactjit/media';

  const { files, stats, loading, error, rescan } = useMediaLibrary(
    directory: string | null,
    options?: MediaLibraryOptions,
  );

Parameters:
  directory  string | null
  options    MediaLibraryOptions (optional)

MediaLibraryOptions fields:
  recursive  boolean      Scan subdirectories (default true)
  maxDepth   number       Maximum recursion depth (default 10)
  autoScan   boolean      Scan on mount (default true)
  filter     MediaType[]  Only return entries of these types

Returns:
  files    MediaFile[]   All scanned files matching the filter
  stats    DirStats      Computed from the returned files (see below)
  loading  boolean
  error    string | null
  rescan   () => Promise<void>

MediaFile fields:
  path          string                        Full filesystem path
  name          string                        Filename only
  size          number                        Bytes
  mtime         number | undefined            Modification timestamp (Unix seconds)
  type          MediaType                     Classified type
  source        'filesystem' | 'archive'
  archivePath   string | undefined            Set when source is 'archive'
  archiveEntry  string | undefined            Set when source is 'archive'

MediaType values:
  'video' | 'audio' | 'image' | 'subtitle' | 'document' | 'archive' | 'metadata' | 'unknown'

DirStats fields:
  total        number
  byType       Partial<Record<MediaType, number>>
  totalSize    number
  largestFile  MediaFile | null

--- useMediaStats ---

Fetch quick directory statistics without loading every file. Lighter than useMediaLibrary when you only need counts and sizes.

  import { useMediaStats } from '@reactjit/media';

  const { stats, loading } = useMediaStats(directory: string | null);

Parameters:
  directory  string | null

Returns:
  stats    DirStats | null
  loading  boolean

--- useMediaIndex ---

Deep-index a directory, scanning both filesystem files and the contents of archives. Archive contents appear as MediaFile entries with source set to 'archive'. This is the archive walker.

  import { useMediaIndex } from '@reactjit/media';

  const { index, loading, error, rescan } = useMediaIndex(
    directory: string | null,
    options?: MediaIndexOptions,
  );

Parameters:
  directory  string | null
  options    MediaIndexOptions (optional)

MediaIndexOptions fields:
  indexArchives    boolean   Look inside archives (default true)
  archivePattern   string    Only index archives whose filename matches this Lua pattern
  filter           MediaType[]   Filter results by type

Returns:
  index    MediaFile[]   Flat list of all discovered files including archive contents
  loading  boolean
  error    string | null
  rescan   () => Promise<void>

---------
SEE ALSO
---------

- useLoveRPC — low-level RPC bridge used internally by all media hooks
- classifyFile — exported utility that classifies a filename to a MediaType without an RPC call
- formatSize — exported utility that formats a byte count to a human-readable string (KB/MB/GB/TB)


========================================
RSS Hooks
========================================

Category: Hooks | Difficulty: beginner | Platforms: love2d, web, terminal, cc, nvim, hs, awesome

Hooks for subscribing to RSS 2.0, Atom, and RSS 1.0 feeds with polling, deduplication, and multi-feed aggregation.

---------
OVERVIEW
---------

The @reactjit/rss package turns any RSS/Atom feed URL into a reactive data source. It ships a zero-dependency XML parser that runs in QuickJS (Love2D), Node.js, and browsers without requiring DOMParser. The two main hooks — useRSSFeed and useRSSAggregate — handle fetching, polling, deduplication, and limit enforcement. Imperative helpers (fetchFeed, parseFeed) and OPML import/export utilities round out the package for building complete feed readers.

---------
API / SYNTAX
---------

--- useRSSFeed ---

Subscribe to a single RSS, Atom, or RSS 1.0 feed. Fetches immediately on mount, re-fetches on url change or manual refetch, and polls if an interval is configured. New items are merged on top of the existing list with deduplication by item ID.

  import { useRSSFeed } from '@reactjit/rss';

  const result: RSSFeedResult = useRSSFeed(url: string | null, options?: RSSFeedOptions);

Parameters:
  url      string | null     Feed URL to fetch. Pass null to disable and reset state.
  options  RSSFeedOptions    Optional configuration (see below)

RSSFeedOptions fields:
  interval   number    Polling interval in milliseconds. 0 or omitted = no polling.
  limit      number    Maximum items to keep (newest first). Default: keep all.
  dedupe     boolean   Deduplicate items by their id/guid across polls. Default: true.
  corsProxy  string    CORS proxy prefix prepended to the URL (e.g. 'https://corsproxy.io/?'). Useful for the web target.

Returns: RSSFeedResult
  feed     Feed | null   Parsed feed metadata, or null while loading/on error
  items    FeedItem[]    Items from the feed (empty array until first successful fetch)
  loading  boolean       True during the initial fetch and each re-fetch
  error    Error | null  Last fetch or parse error, or null on success
  refetch  () => void    Trigger an immediate re-fetch outside the polling interval

Feed fields:
  title          string
  description    string
  link           string
  language       string | null
  lastBuildDate  string | null   ISO date string
  image          string | null   Feed logo URL
  type           'rss2' | 'atom' | 'rss1' | 'unknown'
  items          FeedItem[]

FeedItem fields:
  id           string              Unique identifier (guid/id, falls back to link or title)
  title        string
  link         string
  description  string              Summary or full HTML content
  content      string | null       Full content (content:encoded or Atom content element)
  pubDate      string | null       ISO date string
  author       string | null
  categories   string[]
  enclosure    { url, type, length } | null   Podcast audio, attached files, etc.
  thumbnail    string | null       Image URL from media:thumbnail, media:content, or itunes:image

--- useRSSAggregate ---

Subscribe to multiple feeds simultaneously and receive a single merged, chronologically sorted item list. All feeds are fetched in parallel. Each feed failure is isolated — one broken feed does not prevent the others from rendering.

  import { useRSSAggregate } from '@reactjit/rss';

  const result: RSSAggregateResult = useRSSAggregate(urls: string[], options?: RSSAggregateOptions);

Parameters:
  urls     string[]              Array of feed URLs to subscribe to
  options  RSSAggregateOptions   Optional configuration (see below)

RSSAggregateOptions fields:
  interval   number   Polling interval in milliseconds. Applied to all feeds. Default: no polling.
  limit      number   Maximum total items after merge. Default: keep all.
  corsProxy  string   CORS proxy prefix applied to every URL.

Returns: RSSAggregateResult
  items    Array<FeedItem & { feedTitle: string; feedUrl: string }>
           Merged item list sorted by pubDate descending. Each item carries feedTitle and feedUrl
           from its originating feed.
  feeds    Array<{ url: string; feed: Feed | null; error: Error | null }>
           Per-feed status — inspect individual failures without losing the rest.
  loading  boolean     True while any feed is still being fetched on the current cycle.
  refetch  () => void  Trigger an immediate re-fetch of all feeds.

--- fetchFeed (imperative) ---

Fetch and parse a feed outside of React. Returns a Promise<Feed>. Use this in event handlers, background tasks, or server-side scripts where hooks are not available.

  import { fetchFeed } from '@reactjit/rss';

  const feed: Feed = await fetchFeed(url: string, corsProxy?: string);

Parameters:
  url        string   Feed URL to fetch
  corsProxy  string   Optional CORS proxy prefix

Returns: Promise<Feed>
Throws on network errors or non-2xx HTTP responses.

--- parseFeed (utility) ---

Parse a raw XML string into a normalized Feed object. Supports RSS 2.0, Atom 1.0, and RSS 1.0 (RDF). No DOMParser — works in QuickJS.

  import { parseFeed } from '@reactjit/rss';

  const feed: Feed = parseFeed(xml: string);

Parameters:
  xml  string  Raw XML string (RSS or Atom document)

Returns: Feed — never throws; returns an empty feed with type 'unknown' on unrecognized input.

--- parseOPML (utility) ---

Parse an OPML subscription list into an array of feed descriptors. Useful for importing browser bookmark exports or shared subscription bundles.

  import { parseOPML } from '@reactjit/rss';

  const outlines: OPMLOutline[] = parseOPML(xml: string);

Parameters:
  xml  string  Raw OPML XML string

Returns: OPMLOutline[]

OPMLOutline fields:
  text      string   Display name
  title     string   Feed title (falls back to text)
  xmlUrl    string   RSS/Atom feed URL
  htmlUrl   string   Website URL
  type      string   Usually 'rss'
  category  string   Optional category tag

--- generateOPML (utility) ---

Serialize a list of subscriptions as an OPML 2.0 document. Use this to export a user's feed list for sharing or backup.

  import { generateOPML } from '@reactjit/rss';

  const xml: string = generateOPML(
    title: string,
    feeds: Array<{ title: string; xmlUrl: string; htmlUrl?: string; category?: string }>,
  );

Parameters:
  title  string   Title for the OPML document
  feeds  Array    Subscription entries

Returns: string — valid OPML 2.0 XML

---------
SEE ALSO
---------

- useFetch — general-purpose HTTP hook for non-XML APIs
- useWebSocket — live data streams that don't require polling


========================================
Server Hooks
========================================

Category: Hooks | Difficulty: intermediate | Platforms: love2d

HTTP server hooks for Love2D — static file serving, dynamic routes, and media library indexing

---------
OVERVIEW
---------

@reactjit/server provides React hooks that start and manage a non-blocking HTTP server running inside Love2D via lua/httpserver.lua. Static files (media, assets) are served entirely from the Lua side with zero bridge overhead. Dynamic API routes forward requests across the QuickJS bridge to React handlers that return response objects. useLibrary adds on-demand filesystem indexing so you can build searchable media servers with a single call.

Import: import { useServer, useStaticServer, useLibrary } from '@reactjit/server';

---------
API / SYNTAX
---------

---- useServer(config) -------------------------------------------------------

Start an HTTP server. The server is bound on mount and torn down on unmount.
Changing port or host restarts the server; changing routes or static entries
does not restart it (route table updates are live).

  useServer(config: ServerConfig | null): UseServerResult

  Arguments:
    config  ServerConfig | null   Pass null to disable the server without
                                  unmounting the hook.

  ServerConfig:
    port     number           Required. TCP port to listen on.
    host?    string           Bind address. Default: '0.0.0.0' (all interfaces).
    routes?  Route[]          Dynamic API routes handled in React.
    static?  StaticRoute[]    Static directories served from Lua.

  Route:
    method?   string         HTTP method filter ('GET', 'POST', ...).
                             Omit to match any method.
    path      string         Path pattern. Supports :param and * wildcards.
    handler   RouteHandler   Function called when a request matches.

  RouteHandler:
    (req: HttpRequest) => HttpResponse | Promise<HttpResponse>

  HttpRequest:
    clientId  number                   Internal client socket id.
    serverId  string                   Internal server instance id.
    method    string                   HTTP method (uppercase).
    path      string                   Matched path with params resolved.
    rawPath   string                   Exact path as received from the client.
    query     Record<string, string>   Parsed query string parameters.
    headers   Record<string, string>   Request headers (lowercase keys).
    body      string                   Raw request body string.
    params    Record<string, string>   Named URL parameters from the route pattern.
    route     string                   The route pattern that matched.

  HttpResponse:
    status    number                     HTTP status code.
    headers?  Record<string, string>     Response headers.
    body?     string                     Response body string.

  StaticRoute:
    path   string   URL prefix to match (e.g. '/media', '/').
    root   string   Absolute OS filesystem path (e.g. '/home/user/music').

  UseServerResult:
    ready     boolean         True once the socket is bound and listening.
    port      number | null   Listening port, or null if not ready.
    requests  HttpRequest[]   Recent requests, most recent first. Capped at 50.
    close     () => void      Manually shut down the server.

---- useStaticServer(port, root, host?) -------------------------------------

One-liner for serving a single directory over HTTP. A convenience wrapper
around useServer with a single static route at path '/'.

  useStaticServer(
    port:   number | null,
    root:   string,
    host?:  string,
  ): UseServerResult

  Arguments:
    port   number | null   Port to listen on. Pass null to disable.
    root   string          Absolute path to the directory to serve.
    host   string          Bind address. Default: '0.0.0.0'.

  Returns the same UseServerResult as useServer.

---- useLibrary(port, directories, host?) -----------------------------------

Index one or more directories, start a server that serves the files directly,
and expose a searchable JSON index endpoint. Files are served by Lua (zero
bridge overhead). The React side receives a full in-memory file index with
category breakdowns and per-directory stats.

  useLibrary(
    port:         number | null,
    directories:  string[],
    host?:        string,
  ): UseLibraryResult

  Arguments:
    port         number | null   Port. Pass null to disable.
    directories  string[]        Absolute OS paths to index and serve.
                                 Directory name (last path segment) becomes
                                 the URL namespace.
    host         string          Bind address. Default: '0.0.0.0'.

  UseLibraryResult:
    ready        boolean              True once server is up and index is built.
    port         number | null        Listening port, or null if not ready.
    files        IndexedFile[]        Full flat file index.
    stats        LibraryIndex.stats   Count totals by category.
    directories  DirectoryStats[]     Per-directory name/path/count/size.
    reindex      () => Promise<void>  Re-scan all directories and rebuild index.
    close        () => void           Shut down the server.

  IndexedFile:
    name      string        Filename with extension (e.g. 'song.mp3').
    path      string        Absolute OS path.
    relPath   string        Path relative to the indexed directory root.
    size      number        File size in bytes.
    modified  number        Last modified epoch seconds.
    ext       string | null Lowercase file extension (e.g. 'mp3'), or null.
    category  FileCategory  'audio' | 'video' | 'image' | 'document' | 'other'
    dir       string        Name of the parent directory (matches URL namespace).

  DirectoryStats:
    name   string   Directory name (last path segment).
    path   string   Absolute OS path.
    count  number   Total files indexed in this directory.
    size   number   Total bytes of all files.

  Auto-served endpoints (directory name derived from last path segment):
    GET /files/<dir>/<relPath>   Serve actual file (Lua, zero bridge).
    GET /api/library             Full JSON index.
    GET /api/library?type=audio  Filter by category (audio/video/image/...).
    GET /api/library?dir=music   Filter by directory name.
    GET /api/library?q=<term>    Search by filename (case-insensitive).

---------
SEE ALSO
---------

- useServer
- useStaticServer
- useLibrary


========================================
Storage Hooks
========================================

Category: Hooks | Difficulty: intermediate | Platforms: love2d, web, terminal, cc, nvim, hs, awesome

Schema-validated CRUD storage with pluggable adapters for every target

---------
OVERVIEW
---------

@reactjit/storage provides schema-validated CRUD operations with reactive React hooks and a plain-JS interface for non-React code. The same API works on every target: Love2D stores data in love.filesystem, terminals use SQLite via Node.js built-in node:sqlite, web targets use localStorage or IndexedDB, and any target can use the in-memory adapter for tests or ephemeral state. Schema validation uses the built-in z builder — a lightweight Zod-inspired system that gives you runtime validation and TypeScript type inference with no external dependencies.

Import: import { StorageProvider, useCRUD, useStorage, createCRUD, z, MemoryAdapter, Love2DFileAdapter, TerminalSQLiteAdapter, LocalStorageAdapter, IndexedDBAdapter } from '@reactjit/storage';

---------
API / SYNTAX
---------

---- z — Schema builder ----------------------------------------------------

The z object is the entry point for defining schemas. All methods return a
Schema<T> instance that carries both a TypeScript type and a runtime parse
function.

  z.string()                       StringSchema
  z.number()                       NumberSchema
  z.boolean()                      BooleanSchema
  z.date()                         DateSchema
  z.array(itemSchema)              ArraySchema<T>
  z.object(shape)                  ObjectSchema<S>
  z.enum(values)                   EnumSchema<T>
  z.literal(value)                 LiteralSchema<T>

Common modifiers (chainable on any schema):
  .optional()                      Makes field optional (T | undefined)
  .nullable()                      Allows null (T | null)
  .default(value)                  Provides a default (implies optional)

String-specific:
  .min(n)                          Minimum character count
  .max(n)                          Maximum character count
  .email()                         Email format check
  .url()                           http/https URL format check
  .matches(regexp, message?)       Custom regex

Number-specific:
  .min(n)                          Minimum value (inclusive)
  .max(n)                          Maximum value (inclusive)
  .int()                           Integer only

Array-specific:
  .min(n)                          Minimum item count

Object-specific:
  .strict()                        Strip unknown fields
  .merge(other)                    Merge two object schemas
  .pick(...keys)                   New schema with only named keys
  .omit(...keys)                   New schema without named keys
  .partial()                       Make all fields optional

Schema methods:
  schema.parse(value): T           Parse and validate. Throws ValidationError.
  schema.safeParse(value):         Returns { success, data } | { success, error }
    SafeParseResult<T>

Type inference:
  type MyType = z.infer<typeof MySchema>

---- StorageProvider -------------------------------------------------------

Context provider. Wrap your app or a subtree with one StorageProvider. All
useCRUD and useStorage calls in that subtree will use the provided adapter by
default.

  <StorageProvider adapter={StorageAdapter}>
    {children}
  </StorageProvider>

  Props:
    adapter   StorageAdapter   Required. See adapter section below.
    children  ReactNode        Required.

---- useStorage() ----------------------------------------------------------

Returns the raw StorageAdapter from context. Use this when you need direct
adapter access beyond what useCRUD provides.

  useStorage(): StorageAdapter

  Throws if called outside a <StorageProvider>.

  StorageAdapter interface:
    get(collection, id): Promise<any | null>
    set(collection, id, data): Promise<void>
    delete(collection, id): Promise<boolean>
    list(collection, query?): Promise<any[]>
    format?: StorageFormat             // 'json' | 'markdown' | 'text'

---- useCRUD<T>(collection, schema, options?) --------------------------------

Full CRUD interface for a single collection. All operations validate data
against the provided schema. Returns an object with async methods and two
reactive query hooks for subscribing to data in components.

  useCRUD<T extends Record<string, any>>(
    collection: string,
    schema:     Schema<T>,
    options?:   CRUDOptions,
  ): CRUDHandle<T>

  Arguments:
    collection  string       Collection name (acts as a namespace / table name).
    schema      Schema<T>    z.object(...) schema for type T.
    options     CRUDOptions  Optional overrides.

  CRUDOptions:
    adapter?:      StorageAdapter                  Override context adapter.
    format?:       'json' | 'markdown' | 'text'   Storage format.
    migrations?:   Record<number, MigrationFn>     Version migration map.
    autoMigrate?:  boolean                         Apply migrations on read.

  CRUDHandle<T>:
    create(data: T): Promise<string>
      Validates data, generates an id if not present, writes record.
      Returns the id string.

    get(id: string): Promise<T | null>
      Reads and validates one record. Returns null if not found.

    update(id: string, partial: Partial<T>): Promise<void>
      Merges partial into existing record, re-validates, writes back.
      Throws if record does not exist.

    delete(id: string): Promise<void>
      Removes a record. Resolves even if the record was not found.

    list(query?: Query): Promise<T[]>
      Returns all records matching the optional query, schema-validated.

    useQuery(id: string): QueryResult<T>
      Reactive hook. Fetches one record and subscribes to refetch.
      QueryResult<T>: { data: T | null; loading: boolean;
                        error: Error | null; refetch: () => void }

    useListQuery(query?: Query): ListResult<T>
      Reactive hook. Fetches all matching records.
      ListResult<T>: { data: T[]; loading: boolean;
                       error: Error | null; refetch: () => void }

  Note: useQuery and useListQuery are hooks returned from useCRUD, not
  standalone hooks. They follow React hook rules and must be called
  unconditionally inside a component.

---- createCRUD<T>(collection, schema, adapter, options?) -------------------

Non-React version of useCRUD. Identical CRUD operations (create, get, update,
delete, list) but without useQuery / useListQuery. Use in scripts, CLI tools,
background workers, or any context without a React tree.

  createCRUD<T extends Record<string, any>>(
    collection: string,
    schema:     Schema<T>,
    adapter:    StorageAdapter,
    options?:   CreateCRUDOptions,
  ): CRUDMethods<T>

  CreateCRUDOptions:
    migrations?:   Record<number, MigrationFn>
    autoMigrate?:  boolean

  CRUDMethods<T>:
    create(data: T): Promise<string>
    get(id: string): Promise<T | null>
    update(id: string, partial: Partial<T>): Promise<void>
    delete(id: string): Promise<void>
    list(query?: Query): Promise<T[]>

---- Query -----------------------------------------------------------------

All list() calls and useListQuery() accept an optional Query object.

  interface Query {
    where?:   WhereClause        Field-level filter conditions.
    orderBy?: string             Field name to sort by.
    order?:   'asc' | 'desc'    Sort direction. Default: 'asc'.
    limit?:   number             Maximum records to return.
    offset?:  number             Number of records to skip.
  }

  WhereClause:
    Record<string, value | Partial<Record<ComparisonOp, value>>>

  ComparisonOp values:
    '$eq'        Equal (same as plain value)
    '$ne'        Not equal
    '$gt'        Greater than
    '$gte'       Greater than or equal
    '$lt'        Less than
    '$lte'       Less than or equal
    '$in'        Value is in array
    '$contains'  String contains substring

---- Adapters ---------------------------------------------------------------

MemoryAdapter
  In-memory. No persistence. Works on every target. Useful for tests and
  development.

    new MemoryAdapter()
    .clear()          Wipes all data across all collections.

Love2DFileAdapter
  Love2D target only. Reads/writes files via love.filesystem RPC.
  Data lives in the Love2D save directory: save/<collection>/<id>.<ext>

    new Love2DFileAdapter({ rpc, format? })

    Options:
      rpc     (method, args?) => Promise<T>   Required. Pass the result of
                                              useLoveRPC or bridge.rpc.
      format  'json' | 'markdown' | 'text'   Default: 'json'.

TerminalSQLiteAdapter
  Terminal target. Uses Node.js v22+ built-in node:sqlite for a SQLite file.
  Each collection is a separate table. Synchronous reads/writes wrapped in
  async interface.

    new TerminalSQLiteAdapter({ dbPath? })

    Options:
      dbPath  string   Path to SQLite file. Default: './data.db'.

    Extra methods:
      rawQuery(sql, params?): Promise<any[]>   Parameterized raw SQL.
      transaction(fn): Promise<void>           Wraps fn in BEGIN/COMMIT/ROLLBACK.
      close(): void                            Release the database file handle.

LocalStorageAdapter
  Web target. Uses window.localStorage. Suitable for small data (< 5 MB total).

    new LocalStorageAdapter({ prefix? })

    Options:
      prefix  string   Key prefix to namespace this adapter. Default: 'rjit:'.

    Extra method:
      clearCollection(collection): Promise<void>

IndexedDBAdapter
  Web target. Uses window.indexedDB. Better for larger datasets.

    new IndexedDBAdapter({ dbName?, collections? })

    Options:
      dbName?      string     IndexedDB database name. Default: 'reactjit-storage'.
      collections? string[]   Known collection names for initial schema creation.

    Extra method:
      clearCollection(collection): Promise<void>

---- Migrations -------------------------------------------------------------

Migrations track schema evolution via a _version integer on each record. When
autoMigrate is true, records are migrated on every read and written back with
the new version.

  migrations: Record<number, MigrationFn>
  MigrationFn = (data: any) => any

  Migrations run sequentially. A record at version 0 with migrations defined
  for versions 1, 2, 3 will have all three functions applied in order.

  Helper utilities (importable from @reactjit/storage):
    getLatestVersion(migrations): number
    migrateRecord(data, migrations, autoMigrate): any
    stampVersion(data, migrations): any
    createMigratingAdapter(adapter, migrations, autoMigrate): StorageAdapter

---------
SEE ALSO
---------

- useStorage
- useCRUD
- createCRUD


========================================
useBridge
========================================

Category: Hooks | Difficulty: advanced | Platforms: love2d, terminal, nvim, cc, hs, awesome

Access the IBridge transport instance for direct bridge communication

---------
OVERVIEW
---------

Returns the `IBridge` transport instance that handles communication between JavaScript (React) and the native runtime (Lua for Love2D, Node.js IPC for grid targets). Allows direct access to low-level bridge methods: pushing events, calling Lua functions, subscribing to bridge events, and querying runtime state. Required for custom integrations, native API bindings, and performance-critical operations that bypass the reconciler. Only works on native targets — throws error if used in web mode.

---------
API / SYNTAX
---------

Import:
```typescript
import { useBridge, BridgeProvider } from '@reactjit/core';
import type { IBridge } from '@reactjit/core';
```

Hook signature:
```typescript
useBridge(): IBridge
```

Return value: `IBridge` instance

Provider signature:
```typescript
<BridgeProvider bridge={IBridge}>
  {children}
</BridgeProvider>
```

IBridge interface (simplified):
```typescript
interface IBridge {
  // Push mutation to native runtime
  push(mutation: Mutation): void;

  // Call Lua function and get result (Love2D only)
  call(fn: string, ...args: any[]): any;

  // Subscribe to bridge events
  on(event: string, handler: Function): void;
  off(event: string, handler: Function): void;

  // Query runtime capabilities
  query(key: string): any;

  // Batch mutations (performance)
  batch(fn: () => void): void;
}
```

Common bridge events:
- `pointerDown`, `pointerUp`, `pointerMove`
- `keyDown`, `keyUp`, `textInput`
- `resize`, `focus`, `blur`
- `tick` (frame event, Love2D only)
- Custom events from Lua via `love.event.push('reactEvent', ...)`

---------
EXAMPLES
---------

EXAMPLE: Direct Lua function call (Love2D)
--------
function GameAPI() {
  const bridge = useBridge();

  const loadLevel = (levelId: number) => {
    // Calls Lua: love.handlers.loadLevel(levelId)
    const result = bridge.call('love.handlers.loadLevel', levelId);
    console.log('Level loaded:', result);
  };

  return (
    <Pressable onPress={() => loadLevel(1)}>
      <Text fontSize={16}>Load Level 1</Text>
    </Pressable>
  );
}
--------
Platforms: love2d

EXAMPLE: Subscribe to custom bridge events
--------
function CustomEventListener() {
  const bridge = useBridge();
  const [message, setMessage] = useState('');

  useEffect(() => {
    const handler = (data: any) => {
      setMessage(data.text);
    };

    bridge.on('customEvent', handler);
    return () => bridge.off('customEvent', handler);
  }, [bridge]);

  return <Text fontSize={14}>{message}</Text>;
}

// From Lua side:
// love.event.push('reactEvent', 'customEvent', { text = "Hello from Lua" })
--------
Platforms: love2d, terminal, nvim, cc, hs, awesome

EXAMPLE: Query runtime capabilities
--------
function RuntimeInfo() {
  const bridge = useBridge();
  const [info, setInfo] = useState(null);

  useEffect(() => {
    const capabilities = {
      version: bridge.query('version'),
      platform: bridge.query('platform'),
      renderer: bridge.query('renderer'),
    };
    setInfo(capabilities);
  }, [bridge]);

  if (!info) return null;

  return (
    <Box>
      <Text fontSize={14}>Version: {info.version}</Text>
      <Text fontSize={14}>Platform: {info.platform}</Text>
      <Text fontSize={14}>Renderer: {info.renderer}</Text>
    </Box>
  );
}
--------
Platforms: love2d, terminal, nvim, cc, hs, awesome

EXAMPLE: Batched mutations for performance
--------
function BulkUpdate({ items }) {
  const bridge = useBridge();

  const updateAll = () => {
    // Batches all mutations in the callback into a single bridge roundtrip
    bridge.batch(() => {
      items.forEach(item => {
        // Each update normally triggers a bridge push
        // Batch mode accumulates them
        updateItem(item);
      });
    });
  };

  return (
    <Pressable onPress={updateAll}>
      <Text fontSize={16}>Update {items.length} items</Text>
    </Pressable>
  );
}
--------
Platforms: love2d, terminal, nvim, cc, hs, awesome

EXAMPLE: Direct mutation push (advanced)
--------
function DirectMutation() {
  const bridge = useBridge();

  const setProperty = () => {
    // Bypass reconciler: push mutation directly
    bridge.push({
      type: 'setProperty',
      nodeId: 123,
      property: 'backgroundColor',
      value: '#ff0000',
    });
  };

  // WARNING: This bypasses React's reconciler state.
  // Only use for performance-critical hot paths or native integrations.

  return (
    <Pressable onPress={setProperty}>
      <Text fontSize={16}>Direct Mutation</Text>
    </Pressable>
  );
}
--------
Platforms: love2d, terminal, nvim, cc, hs, awesome

EXAMPLE: Frame tick subscription (Love2D)
--------
function GameLoop() {
  const bridge = useBridge();
  const [frame, setFrame] = useState(0);
  const [deltaTime, setDeltaTime] = useState(0);

  useEffect(() => {
    const tick = (dt: number) => {
      setDeltaTime(dt);
      setFrame(f => f + 1);
    };

    bridge.on('tick', tick);
    return () => bridge.off('tick', tick);
  }, [bridge]);

  const fps = deltaTime > 0 ? Math.round(1 / deltaTime) : 0;

  return (
    <Box>
      <Text fontSize={14}>Frame: {frame}</Text>
      <Text fontSize={14}>FPS: {fps}</Text>
    </Box>
  );
}
--------
Platforms: love2d

EXAMPLE: Provider setup at app root
--------
import { createRoot } from '@reactjit/native';
import { BridgeProvider, RendererProvider } from '@reactjit/core';
import { createBridge } from '@reactjit/native';

const bridge = createBridge();

function App() {
  return (
    <Box style={{ width: '100%', height: '100%' }}>
      <CustomComponent />
    </Box>
  );
}

const root = createRoot();
root.render(
  <BridgeProvider bridge={bridge}>
    <RendererProvider mode="native">
      <App />
    </RendererProvider>
  </BridgeProvider>
);
--------
Platforms: love2d, terminal, nvim, cc, hs, awesome

EXAMPLE: File system integration (Love2D)
--------
function FileLoader() {
  const bridge = useBridge();
  const [content, setContent] = useState('');

  const loadFile = async (path: string) => {
    // Calls Lua: love.filesystem.read(path)
    const result = bridge.call('love.filesystem.read', path);
    setContent(result);
  };

  return (
    <Box>
      <Pressable onPress={() => loadFile('config.json')}>
        <Text fontSize={16}>Load Config</Text>
      </Pressable>
      {content && (
        <Text fontSize={12}>{content}</Text>
      )}
    </Box>
  );
}
--------
Platforms: love2d

---------
CRITICAL RULES
---------

* Must be used within a `<BridgeProvider>`. Throws error if context is missing.
* Only works on native targets (Love2D, terminal, nvim, cc, hs, awesome). Web mode has no bridge.
* Direct mutations bypass React reconciler state. Use sparingly and only for performance-critical hot paths.
* Event handlers registered via `bridge.on()` MUST be cleaned up via `bridge.off()` in useEffect cleanup. Memory leaks otherwise.
* `bridge.call()` is synchronous on Love2D (QuickJS FFI), asynchronous on grid targets (IPC). Check target before use.
* Batched mutations improve performance for bulk updates (10+ mutations per frame). Non-batched is fine for typical component updates.
* Bridge events are lowercase (`pointerDown`, not `PointerDown`). Event names are target-specific — check bridge implementation.
* `bridge.query()` is target-specific. Love2D supports `version`, `platform`, `renderer`. Grid targets vary.
* Mutation format is internal and unstable. Prefer reconciler methods (setState, props) unless implementing a native binding.
* Bridge is NOT reactive. Changes pushed directly don't trigger React re-renders. Use state if you need reactivity.

---------
SEE ALSO
---------

- BridgeProvider: Context provider for bridge instance
- IBridge interface: Full bridge API documentation
- useRendererMode: Detect web vs native mode
- Event dispatcher: High-level event handling via props (onPress, onPointerMove, etc.)
- createBridge: Factory for bridge instances (native targets)


========================================
useClipboard
========================================

Category: Hooks | Difficulty: beginner | Platforms: love2d, web

Read and write the system clipboard with a transient "copied" feedback boolean.

---------
OVERVIEW
---------

useClipboard provides async copy and paste functions that route through the bridge RPC layer to reach the host clipboard — love.system on Love2D and the Web Clipboard API on web targets. After a successful copy call the `copied` boolean flips to true automatically and resets to false after 2 seconds, making it easy to show transient "Copied!" feedback in the UI without managing a timer manually. The cleanup timer is cancelled automatically on unmount.

---------
API / SYNTAX
---------

```tsx
import { useClipboard } from '@reactjit/core';
```

Parameters:
None.

Returns:
| Field  | Type                        | Description |
|--------|-----------------------------|-------------|
| copy   | (text: string) => Promise<void> | Write the given string to the system clipboard. Sets copied to true for 2 seconds after success. |
| paste  | () => Promise<string>       | Read the current system clipboard contents and return them as a string. |
| copied | boolean                     | True for 2 seconds after the last successful copy call, then resets to false. Use this for "Copied!" UI feedback. |

---------
EXAMPLES
---------

EXAMPLE: Copy button with transient feedback
--------
```tsx
import { useClipboard, Box, Text, Pressable } from '@reactjit/core';

function CopyButton({ value }: { value: string }) {
  const { copy, copied } = useClipboard();

  return (
    <Pressable onPress={() => copy(value)}>
      <Box backgroundColor={copied ? '#2a7' : '#444'} padding={8} borderRadius={4}>
        <Text fontSize={13}>{copied ? 'Copied!' : 'Copy'}</Text>
      </Box>
    </Pressable>
  );
}
```
--------
Platforms: love2d, web

EXAMPLE: Paste button that reads and displays clipboard text
--------
```tsx
import { useState } from 'react';
import { useClipboard, Box, Text, Pressable } from '@reactjit/core';

function PastePreview() {
  const { paste } = useClipboard();
  const [content, setContent] = useState('');

  return (
    <Box flexDirection="column" gap={8} padding={12}>
      <Pressable onPress={async () => setContent(await paste())}>
        <Box backgroundColor="#333" padding={8}>
          <Text fontSize={13}>Paste from clipboard</Text>
        </Box>
      </Pressable>
      {content !== '' && (
        <Box backgroundColor="#1a1a1a" padding={8}>
          <Text fontSize={12}>{content}</Text>
        </Box>
      )}
    </Box>
  );
}
```
--------
Platforms: love2d, web

EXAMPLE: Trigger copy via a hotkey
--------
```tsx
import { useClipboard, useHotkey, Box, Text } from '@reactjit/core';

function TextDisplay({ text }: { text: string }) {
  const { copy, copied } = useClipboard();

  useHotkey('ctrl+c', () => copy(text));

  return (
    <Box padding={12}>
      <Text fontSize={14}>{text}</Text>
      {copied && <Text fontSize={12}>Copied to clipboard!</Text>}
    </Box>
  );
}
```
--------
Platforms: love2d, web

EXAMPLE: Copy a JSON payload to clipboard
--------
```tsx
import { useClipboard, Pressable, Text } from '@reactjit/core';

function ExportButton({ data }: { data: object }) {
  const { copy, copied } = useClipboard();

  return (
    <Pressable onPress={() => copy(JSON.stringify(data, null, 2))}>
      <Text fontSize={14}>{copied ? 'Exported!' : 'Export JSON'}</Text>
    </Pressable>
  );
}
```
--------
Platforms: love2d, web
