========================================
usePorts
========================================

Category: Hooks | Difficulty: intermediate | Platforms: love2d

Monitor open TCP/UDP ports with process details and kill processes by PID.

---------
OVERVIEW
---------

usePorts polls the host system for all listening TCP and UDP ports, returning each port's number, bound address, protocol, PID, and process name. It also provides a kill function to send signals to processes by PID. Data is refreshed on a configurable interval via the Love2D bridge RPC, and each port entry carries a .toSysLog(path) method for structured logging. The whole result set can also be logged in one call via the top-level toSysLog method.

---------
API / SYNTAX
---------

```tsx
import { usePorts } from '@reactjit/core';
```

Hook signature:
```tsx
usePorts(interval?: number): PortMonitor
```

Parameters:

| Parameter | Type   | Default | Description                        |
|-----------|--------|---------|------------------------------------|
| interval  | number | 2000    | ms between port list refreshes.    |

Returns: PortMonitor

PortMonitor shape:

| Field    | Type       | Description                                                      |
|----------|------------|------------------------------------------------------------------|
| list     | PortInfo[] | All currently listening ports.                                   |
| loading  | boolean    | true until the first fetch completes.                            |
| kill     | fn         | (pid: number, signal?: string) => Promise<boolean>               |
| toSysLog | fn         | (path: string) => void — log all ports to file.                  |

kill parameters:

| Parameter | Type   | Default  | Description                                             |
|-----------|--------|----------|---------------------------------------------------------|
| pid       | number | —        | Process ID to signal.                                   |
| signal    | string | 'TERM'   | Signal name: 'TERM', 'KILL', 'HUP', 'INT', etc.       |

kill returns Promise<boolean> — true if the signal was sent successfully.

PortInfo shape:

| Field    | Type           | Description                                |
|----------|----------------|--------------------------------------------|
| port     | number         | Listening port number.                     |
| host     | string         | Bound address, e.g. "0.0.0.0", "127.0.0.1"|
| pid      | number         | Process ID owning the socket.              |
| process  | string         | Process name, e.g. "node", "nginx".        |
| protocol | 'tcp' | 'udp'  | Socket protocol.                           |
| state    | string         | Socket state, e.g. "LISTEN".               |
| toSysLog | fn             | (path: string) => void — log this entry.   |

---------
EXAMPLES
---------

EXAMPLE: Display all listening ports in a table
--------
```tsx
import { usePorts } from '@reactjit/core';

function PortTable() {
  const ports = usePorts(2000);

  if (ports.loading) {
    return <Text fontSize={13}>Scanning ports...</Text>;
  }

  return (
    <Box style={{ padding: 12, gap: 4 }}>
      <Box style={{ flexDirection: 'row', gap: 12 }}>
        <Text fontSize={11} style={{ width: 60, color: '#aaa' }}>PORT</Text>
        <Text fontSize={11} style={{ width: 40, color: '#aaa' }}>PROTO</Text>
        <Text fontSize={11} style={{ width: 50, color: '#aaa' }}>PID</Text>
        <Text fontSize={11} style={{ color: '#aaa' }}>PROCESS</Text>
      </Box>
      {ports.list.map(p => (
        <Box key={`${p.protocol}-${p.port}`} style={{ flexDirection: 'row', gap: 12 }}>
          <Text fontSize={11} style={{ width: 60 }}>{p.port}</Text>
          <Text fontSize={11} style={{ width: 40 }}>{p.protocol.toUpperCase()}</Text>
          <Text fontSize={11} style={{ width: 50 }}>{p.pid}</Text>
          <Text fontSize={11}>{p.process}</Text>
        </Box>
      ))}
    </Box>
  );
}
```
--------
Platforms: love2d

EXAMPLE: Kill a process by clicking its port row
--------
```tsx
import { useState } from 'react';
import { usePorts } from '@reactjit/core';

function PortManager() {
  const ports = usePorts(3000);
  const [status, setStatus] = useState('');

  const handleKill = async (pid: number, processName: string) => {
    const ok = await ports.kill(pid);
    setStatus(ok ? `Sent SIGTERM to ${processName} (${pid})` : `Failed to kill ${pid}`);
  };

  return (
    <Box style={{ padding: 12, gap: 6 }}>
      {status ? <Text fontSize={12} style={{ color: '#ffaa00' }}>{status}</Text> : null}
      {ports.list.map(p => (
        <Box
          key={`${p.protocol}-${p.port}`}
          style={{ flexDirection: 'row', gap: 12, alignItems: 'center' }}
        >
          <Text fontSize={12} style={{ width: 60 }}>{p.port}</Text>
          <Text fontSize={12} style={{ flex: 1 }}>{p.process}</Text>
          <Box
            onPress={() => handleKill(p.pid, p.process)}
            style={{ backgroundColor: '#cc2200', padding: 4 }}
          >
            <Text fontSize={11}>Kill</Text>
          </Box>
        </Box>
      ))}
    </Box>
  );
}
```
--------
Platforms: love2d

EXAMPLE: Filter to a specific protocol or address
--------
```tsx
import { usePorts } from '@reactjit/core';

function TcpPorts() {
  const ports = usePorts(2000);
  const tcpPorts = ports.list.filter(p => p.protocol === 'tcp');

  return (
    <Box style={{ gap: 4 }}>
      <Text fontSize={14}>TCP Ports ({tcpPorts.length})</Text>
      {tcpPorts.map(p => (
        <Text key={p.port} fontSize={12}>
          {p.host}:{p.port} — {p.process} ({p.pid})
        </Text>
      ))}
    </Box>
  );
}
```
--------
Platforms: love2d

EXAMPLE: Force-kill with SIGKILL
--------
```tsx
import { usePorts } from '@reactjit/core';

function ForceKillButton({ pid }: { pid: number }) {
  const ports = usePorts();

  const forceKill = async () => {
    const ok = await ports.kill(pid, 'KILL');
    console.log(ok ? 'Process killed' : 'Kill failed');
  };

  return (
    <Box onPress={forceKill} style={{ backgroundColor: '#880000', padding: 6 }}>
      <Text fontSize={12}>Force Kill</Text>
    </Box>
  );
}
```
--------
Platforms: love2d

EXAMPLE: Log current port snapshot to file
--------
```tsx
import { useEffect } from 'react';
import { usePorts } from '@reactjit/core';

function PortLogger() {
  const ports = usePorts(5000);

  useEffect(() => {
    if (!ports.loading) {
      ports.toSysLog('/tmp/ports.log');
    }
  }, [ports.list]);

  return null;
}
```
--------
Platforms: love2d

---------
SEE ALSO
---------

- useSystemMonitor — per-core CPU, process list, network I/O, GPU, disk
- useSystemInfo — static OS facts (hostname, CPU model, shell, uptime)


========================================
useRendererMode
========================================

Category: Hooks | Difficulty: beginner | Platforms: all

Returns the current renderer mode ('web' or 'native')

---------
OVERVIEW
---------

Returns the current renderer mode as either `'web'` or `'native'`. The mode is set by `<RendererProvider>` at the root of the application. Primitives like `Box`, `Text`, and `Image` use this internally to switch between DOM elements (web) and reconciler instances (native). Application code can use it for conditional rendering based on the rendering target.

---------
API / SYNTAX
---------

Import:
```typescript
import { useRendererMode, RendererProvider } from '@reactjit/core';
```

Hook signature:
```typescript
useRendererMode(): RendererMode
```

Type:
```typescript
type RendererMode = 'web' | 'native';
```

Return value: `'web'` or `'native'`

Provider signature:
```typescript
<RendererProvider mode={'web' | 'native'}>
  {children}
</RendererProvider>
```

---------
EXAMPLES
---------

EXAMPLE: Conditional feature based on renderer
--------
function InteractiveDemo() {
  const mode = useRendererMode();

  return (
    <Box>
      <Text fontSize={16}>Renderer: {mode}</Text>
      {mode === 'native' && (
        <Text fontSize={14}>Inspector available (F12)</Text>
      )}
      {mode === 'web' && (
        <Text fontSize={14}>Use browser DevTools</Text>
      )}
    </Box>
  );
}
--------
Platforms: all

EXAMPLE: Platform-specific styling
--------
function Button({ label, onPress }) {
  const mode = useRendererMode();

  const buttonStyle = {
    padding: mode === 'web' ? 12 : 16,
    backgroundColor: mode === 'web' ? '#007bff' : '#0066cc',
    borderRadius: mode === 'web' ? 4 : 0,
  };

  return (
    <Pressable onPress={onPress}>
      <Box style={buttonStyle}>
        <Text fontSize={14} color="#ffffff">{label}</Text>
      </Box>
    </Pressable>
  );
}
--------
Platforms: all

EXAMPLE: Conditional hook usage
--------
function AnimatedComponent() {
  const mode = useRendererMode();

  // useSpring works on all targets, but might have different configs
  const springConfig = mode === 'web'
    ? { tension: 170, friction: 26 }
    : { tension: 120, friction: 20 };

  const spring = useSpring({
    from: { opacity: 0 },
    to: { opacity: 1 },
    config: springConfig,
  });

  return <Box style={{ opacity: spring.opacity }}>...</Box>;
}
--------
Platforms: all

EXAMPLE: Debug overlay for native targets
--------
function DebugOverlay({ visible, stats }) {
  const mode = useRendererMode();

  // Only show debug overlay on native targets
  if (mode !== 'native' || !visible) return null;

  return (
    <Box style={{
      position: 'absolute',
      top: 0,
      left: 0,
      padding: 8,
      backgroundColor: 'rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.8)',
    }}>
      <Text fontSize={12} color="#00ff00">FPS: {stats.fps}</Text>
      <Text fontSize={12} color="#00ff00">Memory: {stats.memory}MB</Text>
    </Box>
  );
}
--------
Platforms: love2d, terminal, nvim, cc, hs, awesome

EXAMPLE: Provider setup at app root
--------
import { createRoot } from '@reactjit/native';
import { RendererProvider } from '@reactjit/core';

function App() {
  return (
    <Box style={{ width: '100%', height: '100%' }}>
      <Text fontSize={24}>Hello ReactJIT</Text>
    </Box>
  );
}

// Native target setup
const root = createRoot();
root.render(
  <RendererProvider mode="native">
    <App />
  </RendererProvider>
);
--------
Platforms: love2d, terminal, nvim, cc, hs, awesome

EXAMPLE: Web overlay setup
--------
import { createRoot } from 'react-dom/client';
import { RendererProvider } from '@reactjit/core';

function App() {
  return (
    <Box style={{ width: 800, height: 600 }}>
      <Text fontSize={24}>Web Overlay</Text>
    </Box>
  );
}

// Web target setup
const container = document.getElementById('root');
const root = createRoot(container);
root.render(
  <RendererProvider mode="web">
    <App />
  </RendererProvider>
);
--------
Platforms: web

EXAMPLE: Feature detection pattern
--------
function FeatureGate({ feature, children }) {
  const mode = useRendererMode();

  const features = {
    web: ['dom-events', 'css-animations'],
    native: ['inspector', 'lua-bridge', 'visual-debugger'],
  };

  const available = features[mode].includes(feature);

  return available ? <>{children}</> : null;
}

function Demo() {
  return (
    <Box>
      <FeatureGate feature="inspector">
        <Text fontSize={14}>Press F12 to open inspector</Text>
      </FeatureGate>
      <FeatureGate feature="dom-events">
        <Text fontSize={14}>Right-click for context menu</Text>
      </FeatureGate>
    </Box>
  );
}
--------
Platforms: all

EXAMPLE: Logging wrapper
--------
function Logger({ message }) {
  const mode = useRendererMode();

  useEffect(() => {
    if (mode === 'native') {
      // Logs to Love2D console or Lua print
      console.log(`[NATIVE] ${message}`);
    } else {
      // Logs to browser DevTools
      console.log(`[WEB] ${message}`);
    }
  }, [message, mode]);

  return null;
}
--------
Platforms: all

---------
CRITICAL RULES
---------

* Must be used within a `<RendererProvider>`. Throws error if context is missing.
* The mode is static for the lifetime of the app. Switching modes requires remounting the entire tree.
* Primitives (`Box`, `Text`, `Image`) automatically detect mode. Application code rarely needs to switch on mode unless implementing platform-specific features.
* `'native'` mode = Love2D, terminal, nvim, cc, hs, awesome targets. `'web'` mode = browser DOM.
* Default context value is `'web'` if no provider exists (for compatibility), but production apps should always use explicit provider.
* Do NOT use this for target-specific builds. Use esbuild `--define` or environment variables for compile-time target detection.
* Use this for runtime conditional rendering, not bundling logic.
* Mode does NOT indicate specific target (Love2D vs terminal vs nvim). Use environment variables or bridge queries for target-specific logic.

---------
SEE ALSO
---------

- RendererProvider: Context provider for renderer mode
- useBridge: Access the transport layer (native targets only)
- Primitives: Box, Text, Image — use this hook internally
- BridgeProvider: Required for native targets, provides IBridge instance


========================================
useSystemInfo
========================================

Category: Hooks | Difficulty: beginner | Platforms: love2d

Fetch real OS, CPU, memory, uptime, and user information from the host system.

---------
OVERVIEW
---------

useSystemInfo retrieves static and semi-static system facts — OS name, kernel version, hostname, logged-in user, shell, CPU model and core count, architecture, memory usage, and uptime — via the Love2D bridge RPC layer. On Love2D, data is gathered by Lua from /proc, io.popen, and os.getenv. Every returned sub-object carries a .toSysLog(path) method for structured append-only JSON logging. The hook fetches once on mount by default; pass a refresh interval in milliseconds to poll periodically for dynamic fields like memory and uptime.

---------
API / SYNTAX
---------

```tsx
import {
  useSystemInfo,
  formatUptime,
  formatMemory,
  formatBytes,
} from '@reactjit/core';
```

Hook signature:
```tsx
useSystemInfo(refreshInterval?: number): SystemInfo
```

Parameters:

| Parameter       | Type   | Default | Description                                                      |
|-----------------|--------|---------|------------------------------------------------------------------|
| refreshInterval | number | 0       | ms between re-polls. 0 = fetch once on mount, no further polls. |

Returns: SystemInfo

SystemInfo shape:

| Field          | Type        | Description                                          |
|----------------|-------------|------------------------------------------------------|
| os             | string      | Distro name, e.g. "Ubuntu 24.04.1 LTS"              |
| kernel         | string      | Kernel release string, e.g. "6.8.0-51-generic"      |
| hostname       | string      | Machine hostname                                     |
| user           | string      | Logged-in username                                   |
| shell          | string      | Shell name, e.g. "bash", "zsh"                       |
| cpu            | string      | CPU model + core count, e.g. "Ryzen 9 7950X (32)"   |
| arch           | string      | Architecture, e.g. "x86_64"                         |
| memory         | MemoryInfo  | { used, total, unit: 'GiB' | 'MiB', toSysLog }     |
| uptime         | UptimeInfo  | { days, hours, minutes, toSysLog }                   |
| loading        | boolean     | true until the first fetch completes                 |
| toSysLog       | fn          | (path: string) => void — append all fields to file   |

MemoryInfo:

| Field    | Type          | Description                  |
|----------|---------------|------------------------------|
| used     | number        | Used memory in unit          |
| total    | number        | Total memory in unit         |
| unit     | 'GiB' | 'MiB' | Display unit              |
| toSysLog | fn            | Log this section to a file   |

UptimeInfo:

| Field    | Type   | Description                |
|----------|--------|----------------------------|
| days     | number | Whole days of uptime       |
| hours    | number | Remainder hours            |
| minutes  | number | Remainder minutes          |
| toSysLog | fn     | Log this section to a file |

Formatting utilities:
```tsx
formatUptime(u: { days: number; hours: number; minutes: number }): string
// "2 days, 5 hours, 30 mins" or "< 1 min"

formatMemory(m: { used: number; total: number; unit: string }): string
// "7.2 GiB / 31.2 GiB"

formatBytes(bytes: number): string
// "14.5 GiB" or "512 MiB"
```

---------
EXAMPLES
---------

EXAMPLE: Display a neofetch-style system summary
--------
```tsx
import { useSystemInfo, formatUptime, formatMemory } from '@reactjit/core';

function SystemCard() {
  const info = useSystemInfo();

  if (info.loading) {
    return <Text fontSize={14}>Loading...</Text>;
  }

  return (
    <Box style={{ padding: 16, gap: 6 }}>
      <Text fontSize={16}>{info.user}@{info.hostname}</Text>
      <Text fontSize={13}>OS:     {info.os}</Text>
      <Text fontSize={13}>Kernel: {info.kernel}</Text>
      <Text fontSize={13}>Shell:  {info.shell}</Text>
      <Text fontSize={13}>CPU:    {info.cpu}</Text>
      <Text fontSize={13}>Arch:   {info.arch}</Text>
      <Text fontSize={13}>RAM:    {formatMemory(info.memory)}</Text>
      <Text fontSize={13}>Uptime: {formatUptime(info.uptime)}</Text>
    </Box>
  );
}
```
--------
Platforms: love2d

EXAMPLE: Live memory polling every 5 seconds
--------
```tsx
import { useSystemInfo, formatMemory } from '@reactjit/core';

function MemoryWidget() {
  const info = useSystemInfo(5000);

  const pct = info.memory.total > 0
    ? Math.round((info.memory.used / info.memory.total) * 100)
    : 0;

  return (
    <Box style={{ width: 200, gap: 4 }}>
      <Text fontSize={13}>RAM: {formatMemory(info.memory)} ({pct}%)</Text>
      <Box style={{ width: '100%', height: 6, backgroundColor: '#333' }}>
        <Box style={{ width: `${pct}%`, height: 6, backgroundColor: '#00cc88' }} />
      </Box>
    </Box>
  );
}
```
--------
Platforms: love2d

EXAMPLE: Log a system snapshot to a file on mount
--------
```tsx
import { useEffect } from 'react';
import { useSystemInfo } from '@reactjit/core';

function DiagnosticsLogger() {
  const info = useSystemInfo();

  useEffect(() => {
    if (!info.loading) {
      info.toSysLog('/tmp/system-snapshot.log');
      info.memory.toSysLog('/tmp/memory.log');
      info.uptime.toSysLog('/tmp/uptime.log');
    }
  }, [info.loading]);

  return null;
}
```
--------
Platforms: love2d

EXAMPLE: Conditional content based on available memory
--------
```tsx
import { useSystemInfo } from '@reactjit/core';

function AdaptiveApp() {
  const info = useSystemInfo();

  const highMemory = !info.loading && info.memory.total >= 16;

  return (
    <Box style={{ width: '100%', height: '100%' }}>
      {highMemory ? <FullResScene /> : <LowResScene />}
    </Box>
  );
}
```
--------
Platforms: love2d

---------
SEE ALSO
---------

- useSystemMonitor — per-core CPU, process list, GPU, network, disk I/O
- formatUptime — format UptimeInfo into a human-readable string
- formatMemory — format MemoryInfo into "used / total unit"
- formatBytes — format raw byte counts into GiB/MiB strings


========================================
useSystemMonitor
========================================

Category: Hooks | Difficulty: intermediate | Platforms: love2d

Real-time htop/nvtop-level system monitoring — per-core CPU, processes, GPU, network, and disk I/O.

---------
OVERVIEW
---------

useSystemMonitor provides comprehensive real-time system telemetry — per-core CPU usage, load averages, detailed memory breakdown (buffers, cached, swap), a sorted process list, GPU stats (NVIDIA and AMD), per-interface network rates, and per-device disk I/O rates. Data is refreshed on a configurable interval via the Love2D bridge RPC. Every sub-object exposes a .toSysLog(path) method for structured append-only JSON logging. Unlike useSystemInfo (which fetches static facts once), useSystemMonitor is designed for continuous live dashboards.

---------
API / SYNTAX
---------

```tsx
import {
  useSystemMonitor,
  formatRate,
  formatTotalBytes,
} from '@reactjit/core';
```

Hook signature:
```tsx
useSystemMonitor(interval?: number, opts?: { processLimit?: number }): SystemMonitor
```

Parameters:

| Parameter          | Type   | Default | Description                                              |
|--------------------|--------|---------|----------------------------------------------------------|
| interval           | number | 1000    | ms between data refreshes.                               |
| opts.processLimit  | number | 20      | Maximum number of processes to return in the list.       |

Returns: SystemMonitor

SystemMonitor shape:

| Field     | Type              | Description                                              |
|-----------|-------------------|----------------------------------------------------------|
| cpu       | CpuInfo           | Aggregate and per-core CPU usage, load averages.         |
| memory    | DetailedMemory    | Full memory breakdown in GiB including swap.             |
| processes | ProcessInfo[]     | Top processes sorted by CPU usage.                       |
| tasks     | TaskCounts        | Total, running, sleeping, stopped, zombie counts.        |
| gpu       | GpuInfo[] or null | GPU stats if a supported GPU is present, else null.      |
| network   | NetworkInterface[]| Per-interface byte totals and transfer rates.            |
| disk      | DiskDevice[]      | Per-device read/write totals and rates.                  |
| loading   | boolean           | true until the first fetch completes.                    |
| toSysLog  | fn                | (path: string) => void — log full snapshot to file.      |

CpuInfo:

| Field    | Type                    | Description                              |
|----------|-------------------------|------------------------------------------|
| cores    | CoreInfo[]              | Per-core breakdown with toSysLog each.   |
| total    | number                  | Aggregate CPU usage 0-100.               |
| loadAvg  | [number, number, number]| 1-min, 5-min, 15-min load averages.      |
| toSysLog | fn                      | Log this section.                        |

CoreInfo:

| Field   | Type   | Description                          |
|---------|--------|--------------------------------------|
| id      | number | Core index (0-based).                |
| usage   | number | Total usage 0-100.                   |
| user    | number | User-space usage 0-100.              |
| system  | number | Kernel usage 0-100.                  |
| iowait  | number | I/O wait 0-100.                      |

DetailedMemory (all values in GiB):

| Field     | Type              | Description              |
|-----------|-------------------|--------------------------|
| total     | number            | Total physical RAM.      |
| used      | number            | Used (total - available).|
| free      | number            | Unused RAM.              |
| available | number            | Actually reclaimable.    |
| buffers   | number            | Kernel buffers.          |
| cached    | number            | Page cache.              |
| swap      | { total, used }   | Swap totals in GiB.      |
| unit      | 'GiB'             | Always GiB.              |

ProcessInfo:

| Field   | Type   | Description                        |
|---------|--------|------------------------------------|
| user    | string | Owning username.                   |
| pid     | number | Process ID.                        |
| cpu     | number | CPU usage % for this process.      |
| mem     | number | Memory usage % for this process.   |
| vsz     | number | Virtual size in KB.                |
| rss     | number | Resident set size in KB.           |
| tty     | string | Controlling terminal.              |
| stat    | string | Process state string (e.g. "S").   |
| command | string | Command name or path.              |

GpuInfo:

| Field       | Type             | Description                       |
|-------------|------------------|-----------------------------------|
| name        | string           | GPU model name.                   |
| vendor      | 'nvidia' | 'amd' | GPU vendor.                    |
| utilization | number           | GPU compute usage 0-100.          |
| memUsed     | number           | Used VRAM.                        |
| memTotal    | number           | Total VRAM.                       |
| memUnit     | string           | Display unit for VRAM values.     |
| temperature | number           | Degrees Celsius.                  |
| power       | number           | Power draw in watts.              |

NetworkInterface:

| Field   | Type   | Description                  |
|---------|--------|------------------------------|
| name    | string | Interface name, e.g. "eth0". |
| rxBytes | number | Total bytes received.        |
| txBytes | number | Total bytes transmitted.     |
| rxRate  | number | Current receive rate B/s.    |
| txRate  | number | Current transmit rate B/s.   |

DiskDevice:

| Field     | Type   | Description                        |
|-----------|--------|------------------------------------|
| name      | string | Device name, e.g. "sda".          |
| readRate  | number | Current read rate in B/s.          |
| writeRate | number | Current write rate in B/s.         |
| reads     | number | Cumulative read operations.        |
| writes    | number | Cumulative write operations.       |

Formatting utilities:
```tsx
formatRate(bytesPerSec: number): string
// "12.3 MB/s", "512.0 KB/s", "800 B/s"

formatTotalBytes(bytes: number): string
// "4.2 GB", "512.0 MB", "1.0 KB"
```

---------
EXAMPLES
---------

EXAMPLE: CPU usage bar per core
--------
```tsx
import { useSystemMonitor } from '@reactjit/core';

function CpuPanel() {
  const sys = useSystemMonitor(1000);

  if (sys.loading) return <Text fontSize={13}>Loading...</Text>;

  return (
    <Box style={{ padding: 12, gap: 6, width: 300 }}>
      <Text fontSize={15}>CPU — {sys.cpu.total.toFixed(1)}%</Text>
      <Text fontSize={11} style={{ color: '#888' }}>
        Load: {sys.cpu.loadAvg.map(v => v.toFixed(2)).join(' ')}
      </Text>
      {sys.cpu.cores.map(core => (
        <Box key={core.id} style={{ flexDirection: 'row', gap: 8, alignItems: 'center' }}>
          <Text fontSize={11} style={{ width: 20 }}>C{core.id}</Text>
          <Box style={{ flex: 1, height: 8, backgroundColor: '#333' }}>
            <Box style={{
              width: `${core.usage}%`,
              height: 8,
              backgroundColor: core.usage > 80 ? '#ff4444' : '#00cc88',
            }} />
          </Box>
          <Text fontSize={11} style={{ width: 36 }}>{core.usage.toFixed(0)}%</Text>
        </Box>
      ))}
    </Box>
  );
}
```
--------
Platforms: love2d

EXAMPLE: Process list sorted by CPU (top 10)
--------
```tsx
import { useSystemMonitor } from '@reactjit/core';

function ProcessList() {
  const sys = useSystemMonitor(2000, { processLimit: 10 });

  return (
    <Box style={{ padding: 12, gap: 4 }}>
      <Box style={{ flexDirection: 'row', gap: 16 }}>
        <Text fontSize={11} style={{ width: 50, color: '#aaa' }}>PID</Text>
        <Text fontSize={11} style={{ width: 40, color: '#aaa' }}>CPU%</Text>
        <Text fontSize={11} style={{ width: 40, color: '#aaa' }}>MEM%</Text>
        <Text fontSize={11} style={{ color: '#aaa' }}>COMMAND</Text>
      </Box>
      {sys.processes.map(p => (
        <Box key={p.pid} style={{ flexDirection: 'row', gap: 16 }}>
          <Text fontSize={11} style={{ width: 50 }}>{p.pid}</Text>
          <Text fontSize={11} style={{ width: 40 }}>{p.cpu.toFixed(1)}</Text>
          <Text fontSize={11} style={{ width: 40 }}>{p.mem.toFixed(1)}</Text>
          <Text fontSize={11}>{p.command}</Text>
        </Box>
      ))}
    </Box>
  );
}
```
--------
Platforms: love2d

EXAMPLE: GPU temperature and VRAM gauge
--------
```tsx
import { useSystemMonitor } from '@reactjit/core';

function GpuPanel() {
  const sys = useSystemMonitor(1000);

  if (!sys.gpu || sys.gpu.length === 0) {
    return <Text fontSize={13}>No GPU detected</Text>;
  }

  const gpu = sys.gpu[0];
  const vramPct = gpu.memTotal > 0
    ? Math.round((gpu.memUsed / gpu.memTotal) * 100)
    : 0;

  return (
    <Box style={{ padding: 12, gap: 6 }}>
      <Text fontSize={14}>{gpu.name}</Text>
      <Text fontSize={12}>Utilization: {gpu.utilization}%</Text>
      <Text fontSize={12}>Temp: {gpu.temperature}C</Text>
      <Text fontSize={12}>Power: {gpu.power}W</Text>
      <Text fontSize={12}>VRAM: {gpu.memUsed}/{gpu.memTotal} {gpu.memUnit} ({vramPct}%)</Text>
    </Box>
  );
}
```
--------
Platforms: love2d

EXAMPLE: Network I/O rates per interface
--------
```tsx
import { useSystemMonitor, formatRate } from '@reactjit/core';

function NetworkPanel() {
  const sys = useSystemMonitor(1000);

  return (
    <Box style={{ padding: 12, gap: 6 }}>
      <Text fontSize={14}>Network</Text>
      {sys.network.map(iface => (
        <Box key={iface.name} style={{ gap: 2 }}>
          <Text fontSize={12} style={{ color: '#aaa' }}>{iface.name}</Text>
          <Text fontSize={11}>Rx: {formatRate(iface.rxRate)}</Text>
          <Text fontSize={11}>Tx: {formatRate(iface.txRate)}</Text>
        </Box>
      ))}
    </Box>
  );
}
```
--------
Platforms: love2d

EXAMPLE: Log full monitor snapshot every 10 seconds
--------
```tsx
import { useEffect } from 'react';
import { useSystemMonitor } from '@reactjit/core';

function TelemetryLogger() {
  const sys = useSystemMonitor(10000);

  useEffect(() => {
    if (!sys.loading) {
      sys.toSysLog('/tmp/monitor.log');
      sys.cpu.toSysLog('/tmp/cpu.log');
      sys.memory.toSysLog('/tmp/memory.log');
      sys.network.forEach(n => n.toSysLog('/tmp/network.log'));
    }
  }, [sys]);

  return null;
}
```

Platforms: love2d

SEE ALSO
---------

- useSystemInfo — one-shot static system facts (OS, hostname, kernel, shell)
- formatRate — format bytes-per-second into KB/s, MB/s, GB/s strings
- formatTotalBytes — format total byte counts into human-readable strings


========================================
useWebSocket
========================================

Category: Hooks | Difficulty: intermediate | Platforms: love2d, web, terminal, cc, nvim, hs, awesome

Open a persistent WebSocket client connection and expose send, lastMessage, and status state.

---------
OVERVIEW
---------

useWebSocket opens a WebSocket client connection to the given URL and keeps it alive for the lifetime of the component. It works across all ReactJIT targets: on Love2D the WebSocket global is polyfilled through the bridge using lua-websocket; on web and Node targets the platform's native WebSocket is used. The hook tracks the connection lifecycle through a typed status field and exposes the most recently received message string. Pass null as the URL to remain disconnected. The send function is a no-op when the socket is not in the open state, so it is always safe to call.

---------
API / SYNTAX
---------

```tsx
import { useWebSocket } from '@reactjit/core';
import type { WebSocketStatus } from '@reactjit/core';
```

Parameters:
| Param | Type           | Default  | Description |
|-------|----------------|----------|-------------|
| url   | string \| null | required | WebSocket server URL (e.g. "ws://localhost:9050"). Pass null to stay closed. |

Returns:
| Field       | Type            | Description |
|-------------|-----------------|-------------|
| status      | WebSocketStatus | Current connection state: "connecting", "open", "closed", or "error". |
| send        | (data: string) => void | Send a string message. No-op if the connection is not in the "open" state. |
| lastMessage | string \| null  | The most recently received message string, or null if nothing has arrived yet. |
| error       | string \| null  | Human-readable error string if status is "error", otherwise null. |

---------
EXAMPLES
---------

EXAMPLE: Connect to a server and display live data
--------
```tsx
import { useWebSocket, Box, Text } from '@reactjit/core';

interface GameState {
  score: number;
  level: number;
}

function LiveHUD() {
  const { status, lastMessage } = useWebSocket('ws://localhost:9050');
  const state: GameState | null = lastMessage ? JSON.parse(lastMessage) : null;

  return (
    <Box flexDirection="column" gap={4} padding={12}>
      <Text fontSize={12}>Status: {status}</Text>
      {state && (
        <>
          <Text fontSize={16}>Score: {state.score}</Text>
          <Text fontSize={14}>Level: {state.level}</Text>
        </>
      )}
    </Box>
  );
}
```
--------
Platforms: all

EXAMPLE: Send commands to a server on button press
--------
```tsx
import { useWebSocket, Box, Text, Pressable } from '@reactjit/core';

function CommandPanel() {
  const { status, send } = useWebSocket('ws://localhost:8080');

  return (
    <Box flexDirection="column" gap={8} padding={12}>
      <Text fontSize={12}>Connection: {status}</Text>
      <Box flexDirection="row" gap={8} width="100%">
        <Pressable onPress={() => send(JSON.stringify({ cmd: 'start' }))}>
          <Box backgroundColor="#2a7" padding={8}>
            <Text fontSize={14}>Start</Text>
          </Box>
        </Pressable>
        <Pressable onPress={() => send(JSON.stringify({ cmd: 'stop' }))}>
          <Box backgroundColor="#a22" padding={8}>
            <Text fontSize={14}>Stop</Text>
          </Box>
        </Pressable>
      </Box>
    </Box>
  );
}
```
--------
Platforms: all

EXAMPLE: Conditional connection — connect only when a toggle is on
--------
```tsx
import { useState } from 'react';
import { useWebSocket, Box, Text, Pressable } from '@reactjit/core';

function ConditionalSocket() {
  const [connected, setConnected] = useState(false);
  const url = connected ? 'ws://localhost:9050' : null;
  const { status, lastMessage } = useWebSocket(url);

  return (
    <Box flexDirection="column" gap={8} padding={12}>
      <Pressable onPress={() => setConnected(c => !c)}>
        <Box backgroundColor={connected ? '#a22' : '#2a7'} padding={8}>
          <Text fontSize={14}>{connected ? 'Disconnect' : 'Connect'}</Text>
        </Box>
      </Pressable>
      <Text fontSize={13}>Status: {status}</Text>
      {lastMessage && <Text fontSize={13}>Last: {lastMessage}</Text>}
    </Box>
  );
}
```
--------
Platforms: all

EXAMPLE: Display an error when the connection fails
--------
```tsx
import { useWebSocket, Box, Text } from '@reactjit/core';

function ReliableSocket() {
  const { status, error, lastMessage } = useWebSocket('ws://localhost:9050');

  return (
    <Box flexDirection="column" gap={4} padding={12}>
      {status === 'error' && (
        <Box backgroundColor="#500" padding={8}>
          <Text fontSize={13}>Connection error: {error}</Text>
        </Box>
      )}
      {status === 'open' && lastMessage && (
        <Text fontSize={14}>{lastMessage}</Text>
      )}
    </Box>
  );
}
```
--------
Platforms: all

---------
SEE ALSO
---------

- usePeerServer
- useFetch
- useLove


========================================
useWindowDimensions
========================================

Category: Hooks | Difficulty: beginner | Platforms: love2d, web, terminal, cc, nvim, hs, awesome

Get the current viewport width and height with reactive updates on window resize.

---------
OVERVIEW
---------

useWindowDimensions returns an object containing the current viewport width and height. The values update reactively whenever the window is resized, allowing components to respond to layout changes dynamically.

The hook subscribes to the 'viewport' bridge event and updates state whenever new dimensions are received from the Lua runtime. Initial dimensions may be {width: 0, height: 0} until the first viewport event is dispatched.

---------
API / SYNTAX
---------

```tsx
import { useWindowDimensions } from '@reactjit/core';
```

Signature:
  function useWindowDimensions(): { width: number; height: number }

Parameters:
  (none)

Returns:
  Object with two properties:
    width: number
      Current viewport width in pixels (or character columns for grid targets)
    height: number
      Current viewport height in pixels (or character rows for grid targets)

---------
EXAMPLES
---------

EXAMPLE: Responsive layout
--------
import { useWindowDimensions, Box, Text } from '@reactjit/core';

function ResponsiveLayout() {
  const { width, height } = useWindowDimensions();
  const isWide = width > 800;
  const isTall = height > 600;

  return (
    <Box style={{
      width: '100%',
      height: '100%',
      flexDirection: isWide ? 'row' : 'column',
      backgroundColor: '#1e1e2e'
    }}>
      <Box style={{ flex: 1, justifyContent: 'center', alignItems: 'center' }}>
        <Text style={{ fontSize: 20, color: '#cdd6f4' }}>
          {width} x {height}
        </Text>
      </Box>
      <Box style={{ flex: 1, justifyContent: 'center', alignItems: 'center' }}>
        <Text style={{ fontSize: 16, color: '#a6adc8' }}>
          Layout: {isWide ? 'Wide' : 'Narrow'}
        </Text>
      </Box>
    </Box>
  );
}
--------
Platforms: all

EXAMPLE: Conditional rendering based on viewport size
--------
import { useWindowDimensions, Box, Text } from '@reactjit/core';

function AdaptiveUI() {
  const { width } = useWindowDimensions();
  const showSidebar = width >= 1024;
  const showMobileNav = width < 768;

  return (
    <Box style={{ width: '100%', height: '100%', flexDirection: 'row' }}>
      {showSidebar && (
        <Box style={{ width: 200, backgroundColor: '#313244' }}>
          <Text style={{ fontSize: 16, color: '#cdd6f4', padding: 10 }}>Sidebar</Text>
        </Box>
      )}
      <Box style={{ flex: 1 }}>
        {showMobileNav && (
          <Box style={{ height: 50, backgroundColor: '#45475a' }}>
            <Text style={{ fontSize: 14, color: '#cdd6f4', padding: 10 }}>Mobile Nav</Text>
          </Box>
        )}
        <Box style={{ flex: 1, justifyContent: 'center', alignItems: 'center' }}>
          <Text style={{ fontSize: 18, color: '#cdd6f4' }}>Content</Text>
        </Box>
      </Box>
    </Box>
  );
}
--------
Platforms: all

EXAMPLE: Calculate aspect ratio
--------
import { useWindowDimensions, Box, Text } from '@reactjit/core';

function AspectRatioDisplay() {
  const { width, height } = useWindowDimensions();
  const aspectRatio = height > 0 ? (width / height).toFixed(2) : '0.00';
  const orientation = width > height ? 'Landscape' : 'Portrait';

  return (
    <Box style={{ width: '100%', height: '100%', justifyContent: 'center', alignItems: 'center' }}>
      <Text style={{ fontSize: 24, color: '#cdd6f4' }}>
        {width} x {height}
      </Text>
      <Text style={{ fontSize: 18, color: '#a6adc8', marginTop: 10 }}>
        Aspect Ratio: {aspectRatio}
      </Text>
      <Text style={{ fontSize: 16, color: '#89b4fa', marginTop: 5 }}>
        {orientation}
      </Text>
    </Box>
  );
}
--------
Platforms: all

---------
CRITICAL RULES
---------

* Initial dimensions may be {width: 0, height: 0} until the first viewport event fires
* Check for zero dimensions before dividing (e.g., calculating aspect ratio)
* Must be used inside a BridgeProvider context
* Updates are reactive -- components re-render automatically on resize
* For Love2D, dimensions are set in conf.lua via t.window.width and t.window.height
* Grid targets measure in character units, not pixels

---------
SEE ALSO
---------

- useloveevent.txt -- Subscribe to custom viewport-related events
- uselove.txt -- General-purpose bridge event subscription
- Box primitive -- Use percentage widths/heights relative to these dimensions


========================================
Webhook Hooks
========================================

Category: Hooks | Difficulty: intermediate | Platforms: love2d, terminal, cc, nvim, hs, awesome

Hooks for receiving incoming webhooks over HTTP and sending signed webhook payloads with retries.

---------
OVERVIEW
---------

The @reactjit/webhooks package covers both sides of the webhook pattern: receiving HTTP POST payloads from external services (GitHub, Stripe, CI systems, etc.) and sending signed JSON payloads to external endpoints. The receiver hook (useWebhook) starts an HTTP server via the Love2D bridge and verifies HMAC-SHA256 signatures using the same algorithm GitHub, Slack, and most modern platforms use. The sender hook (useWebhookSender) wraps fetch with automatic signing, exponential-backoff retries, and React state for loading/error tracking. An imperative sendWebhook function is also exported for use outside React.

---------
API / SYNTAX
---------

--- useWebhook ---

Start an HTTP listener and receive incoming webhook events as React state. Each arriving request is parsed, optionally signature-verified, and prepended to the events array. Requires the Love2D HTTP server bridge (Lua-side httpserver module).

  import { useWebhook } from '@reactjit/webhooks';

  const result: WebhookReceiverResult = useWebhook(
    port: number | null,
    path: string,
    options?: WebhookReceiverOptions,
  );

Parameters:
  port     number | null   Port to listen on. Pass null to disable the server.
  path     string          URL path to handle (e.g. '/webhook/github')
  options  WebhookReceiverOptions   Optional configuration (see below)

WebhookReceiverOptions fields:
  secret           string     Shared secret for HMAC-SHA256 signature verification. If omitted, event.verified is always null.
  signatureHeader  string     Header name carrying the signature. Default: 'x-hub-signature-256' (GitHub format).
  maxEvents        number     Maximum events to keep in the queue (newest first). Default: 100.
  responseStatus   number     HTTP status code sent back to the caller. Default: 200.
  responseBody     string     Response body sent back. Default: '{"ok":true}'.
  methods          string[]   Filter by HTTP method. Default: accepts all methods.
  host             string     Host/interface to bind to. Default: Lua server default.

Returns: WebhookReceiverResult
  events         WebhookEvent[]      All received events, newest first (up to maxEvents)
  latest         WebhookEvent | null The most recently received event, or null if empty
  ready          boolean             True once the HTTP server is bound and listening
  totalReceived  number              Running count of all events received (including evicted ones)
  clear          () => void          Empty the event queue without stopping the server

WebhookEvent fields:
  id        string                        Auto-generated unique event ID (e.g. 'wh_1700000000_abc123')
  timestamp string                        ISO timestamp of when the event was received
  method    string                        HTTP method (usually 'POST')
  path      string                        Request path
  headers   Record<string, string>        All request headers
  body      T                             Parsed body — JSON object if Content-Type is application/json, raw string otherwise
  rawBody   string                        Original unparsed request body
  query     Record<string, string>        Query parameters from the URL
  verified  boolean | null                HMAC verification result. true = valid, false = invalid, null = no secret configured

--- useWebhookSender ---

React hook for sending webhook payloads with loading and error state tracking. Wraps sendWebhook with default options that apply to every call made through the returned send function.

  import { useWebhookSender } from '@reactjit/webhooks';

  const result: WebhookSenderResult = useWebhookSender(options?: WebhookSenderOptions);

Parameters:
  options  WebhookSenderOptions   Optional defaults for all sends

WebhookSenderOptions fields:
  headers  Record<string, string>   Default headers merged into every request
  secret   string                   Default shared secret for HMAC signing
  retries  number                   Default retry count. Default: 3

Returns: WebhookSenderResult
  send        (url: string, payload: any, options?: WebhookSendOptions) => Promise<WebhookSendResult>
              Send a payload. Per-call options override the hook-level defaults.
  sending     boolean                  True while a send is in flight
  lastResult  WebhookSendResult | null Result of the last completed send
  error       Error | null             Error from the last failed send, or null

WebhookSendResult fields:
  ok        boolean   True if the server responded with a 2xx status
  status    number    HTTP response status code (0 on network failure)
  body      string    Raw response body
  attempts  number    Total number of attempts made (1 + retries used)

--- sendWebhook (imperative) ---

Send a webhook payload to a URL without React state. Use this in event handlers, Lua RPC callbacks, or any context where a hook is not appropriate.

  import { sendWebhook } from '@reactjit/webhooks';

  const result: WebhookSendResult = await sendWebhook(
    url: string,
    payload: any,
    options?: WebhookSendOptions,
  );

Parameters:
  url      string               Destination URL
  payload  any                  Payload to send. Objects are serialized as JSON automatically.
  options  WebhookSendOptions   Optional per-call configuration

WebhookSendOptions fields:
  method           string                   HTTP method. Default: 'POST'
  headers          Record<string, string>   Additional headers merged with Content-Type: application/json
  secret           string                   Shared secret — generates an HMAC-SHA256 signature header
  signatureHeader  string                   Header name for the signature. Default: 'x-hub-signature-256'
  retries          number                   Retry count on 5xx or network failure. Default: 0 (no retries)
  retryDelay       number                   Base delay in ms between retries. Doubles each retry (exponential backoff). Default: 1000

Returns: Promise<WebhookSendResult>
Does not throw — errors are captured in the result object.

--- hmacSHA256 (utility) ---

Compute an HMAC-SHA256 hex digest. Exported for use in custom signing schemes or signature verification outside of webhooks. Backed by @noble/hashes (audited).

  import { hmacSHA256 } from '@reactjit/webhooks';

  const hex: string = hmacSHA256(key: string, message: string);

Parameters:
  key      string   Shared secret
  message  string   Message to sign

Returns: string — lowercase hex digest (64 characters)

--- timingSafeEqual (utility) ---

Compare two hex strings in constant time to prevent timing side-channel attacks. Use this when comparing received signatures against computed ones.

  import { timingSafeEqual } from '@reactjit/webhooks';

  const equal: boolean = timingSafeEqual(a: string, b: string);

Parameters:
  a  string   First hex string
  b  string   Second hex string

Returns: boolean — false immediately if lengths differ (length leakage is acceptable; value is not)

---------
SEE ALSO
---------

- useServer — lower-level HTTP server hook that useWebhook builds upon
- useFetch — outbound HTTP requests without webhook-specific signing logic
- useWebSocket — persistent bidirectional connections


========================================
Animation System
========================================

Category: Animation | Difficulty: intermediate | Platforms: love2d, web, terminal, cc, nvim, hs, awesome

Overview of the ReactJIT animation system including animated values, timing, springs, easing, and composite animations.

---------
OVERVIEW
---------

ReactJIT includes a complete animation system built on the AnimatedValue class. It provides timing-based animations (with easing functions), physics-based spring animations, and composite animations (parallel, sequence, stagger, loop). Three React hooks make it easy to integrate animations into components: useAnimation for full control, useSpring for automatic spring transitions, and useTransition for automatic timing-based transitions.

The animation system works in both web mode (using requestAnimationFrame) and native mode (falling back to setTimeout at ~60fps when requestAnimationFrame is unavailable). All active animations share a single frame loop for efficiency. Animations are batched to avoid excessive re-renders.

---------
API / SYNTAX
---------

All animation APIs are imported from '@reactjit/core':

  import {
    AnimatedValue,
    useAnimation,
    useSpring,
    useTransition,
    Easing,
    parallel,
    sequence,
    stagger,
    loop,
  } from '@reactjit/core';

Core types:
  Animation: { start(callback?), stop() }
  TimingConfig: { toValue, duration?, easing?, delay? }
  SpringConfig: { toValue, stiffness?, damping?, mass?, velocity?, restThreshold? }
  InterpolationConfig: { inputRange, outputRange, extrapolate? }
  EasingFunction: (t: number) => number

---------
EXAMPLES
---------

EXAMPLE: Fade-in animation
--------
import { useEffect } from 'react';
import { useAnimation, Box, Text } from '@reactjit/core';

function FadeIn({ children }: { children: React.ReactNode }) {
  const [opacity, opacityValue] = useAnimation(0);

  useEffect(() => {
    opacity.timing({ toValue: 1, duration: 500 }).start();
  }, [opacity]);

  return (
    <Box style={{ width: '100%', height: '100%', opacity: opacityValue }}>
      {children}
    </Box>
  );
}
--------
Platforms: all

EXAMPLE: Spring animation on state change
--------
import { useState } from 'react';
import { useSpring, Box, Text } from '@reactjit/core';

function ToggleBox() {
  const [expanded, setExpanded] = useState(false);
  const width = useSpring(expanded ? 300 : 100, { stiffness: 200, damping: 20 });

  return (
    <Box style={{ width: '100%', height: '100%', padding: 20 }}>
      <Box
        style={{ width, height: 100, backgroundColor: '#89b4fa', borderRadius: 8 }}
        onClick={() => setExpanded(!expanded)}
      />
    </Box>
  );
}
--------
Platforms: all

---------
CRITICAL RULES
---------

* AnimatedValue.setValue() stops any active animation before setting the value
* Starting a new animation on an AnimatedValue automatically stops the previous one
* useAnimation cleanup stops active animations on unmount
* useSpring and useTransition skip animation on first render (value starts at target)
* Composite animations (parallel, sequence, etc.) return Animation objects with start/stop
* Animation callbacks receive { finished: boolean } -- false if stop() was called

---------
SEE ALSO
---------

- animatedvalue.txt -- AnimatedValue class reference
- useanimation.txt -- Primary animation hook
- usespring.txt -- Automatic spring transitions
- usetransition.txt -- Automatic timing transitions
- easing.txt -- Easing function library
- composite.txt -- Parallel, sequence, stagger, loop


========================================
AnimatedValue
========================================

Category: Animation | Difficulty: intermediate | Platforms: love2d, web, terminal, cc, nvim, hs, awesome

Mutable value container that drives animations through timing, spring, and interpolation methods.

---------
OVERVIEW
---------

AnimatedValue is the core class of the animation system. It holds a mutable numeric value and provides methods to animate it (timing and spring), interpolate it to other ranges, and listen for changes. When an animation is active, the value updates every frame and notifies all listeners.

AnimatedValue instances are typically created via the useAnimation hook, which handles React lifecycle integration. You can also create them directly for non-React usage.

---------
API / SYNTAX
---------

Constructor:
  new AnimatedValue(initialValue: number)

Methods:

  getValue(): number
    Returns the current numeric value.

  setValue(value: number): void
    Immediately sets the value. Stops any active animation first. Notifies all listeners.

  addListener(callback: (value: number) => void): () => void
    Registers a callback that fires whenever the value changes (from animation or setValue).
    Returns an unsubscribe function.

  timing(config: TimingConfig): Animation
    Creates a timing-based animation. Returns an Animation object with start() and stop().

    TimingConfig:
      toValue: number              Target value
      duration?: number            Duration in milliseconds (default: 300)
      easing?: EasingFunction      Easing function (default: Easing.easeInOut)
      delay?: number               Delay before animation starts (default: 0)

  spring(config: SpringConfig): Animation
    Creates a physics-based spring animation. Returns an Animation object.

    SpringConfig:
      toValue: number              Target value
      stiffness?: number           Spring stiffness (default: 100)
      damping?: number             Damping coefficient (default: 10)
      mass?: number                Mass of the object (default: 1)
      velocity?: number            Initial velocity (default: 0)
      restThreshold?: number       Threshold for considering the spring at rest (default: 0.001)

  interpolate(config: InterpolationConfig): number | string
    Maps the current value through input/output ranges. Returns the interpolated result.

    InterpolationConfig:
      inputRange: number[]           Input values to map from (ascending)
      outputRange: number[] | string[]  Output values to map to (same length as inputRange)
      extrapolate?: 'clamp' | 'extend'  Behavior outside inputRange (default: 'extend')

    When outputRange contains strings (e.g., color values in "rgba(...)" format), color
    interpolation is performed automatically.

Animation interface:
  start(callback?: (result: { finished: boolean }) => void): void
    Starts the animation. Optional callback fires when animation completes.
    result.finished is true if animation completed naturally, false if stopped.

  stop(): void
    Stops the animation at its current value.

---------
EXAMPLES
---------

EXAMPLE: Basic timing animation
--------
import { useEffect } from 'react';
import { useAnimation, Box, Easing } from '@reactjit/core';

function SlideIn() {
  const [xAnim, xValue] = useAnimation(-200);

  useEffect(() => {
    xAnim.timing({
      toValue: 0,
      duration: 600,
      easing: Easing.easeOut,
    }).start();
  }, [xAnim]);

  return (
    <Box style={{
      width: 100,
      height: 100,
      backgroundColor: '#89b4fa',
      transform: { translateX: xValue },
    }} />
  );
}
--------
Platforms: all

EXAMPLE: Spring animation
--------
import { useEffect, useState } from 'react';
import { useAnimation, Box } from '@reactjit/core';

function BounceBox() {
  const [scaleAnim, scaleValue] = useAnimation(1);
  const [pressed, setPressed] = useState(false);

  useEffect(() => {
    scaleAnim.spring({
      toValue: pressed ? 0.9 : 1.0,
      stiffness: 300,
      damping: 15,
    }).start();
  }, [pressed, scaleAnim]);

  return (
    <Box
      style={{
        width: 100,
        height: 100,
        backgroundColor: '#f38ba8',
        transform: { scaleX: scaleValue, scaleY: scaleValue },
      }}
      onClick={() => setPressed(!pressed)}
    />
  );
}
--------
Platforms: all

EXAMPLE: Interpolation
--------
import { useAnimation, Box, Text } from '@reactjit/core';

function ProgressLabel() {
  const [progress, progressValue] = useAnimation(0);

  // Interpolate 0-1 to color
  const color = progress.interpolate({
    inputRange: [0, 0.5, 1],
    outputRange: ['rgba(243, 139, 168, 1)', 'rgba(249, 226, 175, 1)', 'rgba(166, 227, 161, 1)'],
  });

  // Interpolate 0-1 to percentage text width
  const barWidth = progress.interpolate({
    inputRange: [0, 1],
    outputRange: [0, 300],
    extrapolate: 'clamp',
  });

  return (
    <Box style={{ width: 300, height: 30 }}>
      <Box style={{
        width: barWidth as number,
        height: '100%',
        backgroundColor: color as string,
        borderRadius: 4,
      }} />
    </Box>
  );
}
--------
Platforms: all

EXAMPLE: Chained animations with callback
--------
import { useEffect } from 'react';
import { useAnimation, Box } from '@reactjit/core';

function ChainedAnimation() {
  const [xAnim, xValue] = useAnimation(0);
  const [yAnim, yValue] = useAnimation(0);

  useEffect(() => {
    xAnim.timing({ toValue: 200, duration: 500 }).start(({ finished }) => {
      if (finished) {
        yAnim.timing({ toValue: 200, duration: 500 }).start();
      }
    });
  }, [xAnim, yAnim]);

  return (
    <Box style={{
      width: 50,
      height: 50,
      backgroundColor: '#cba6f7',
      transform: { translateX: xValue, translateY: yValue },
    }} />
  );
}
--------
Platforms: all

---------
CRITICAL RULES
---------

* setValue() stops any active animation before setting the value
* Starting a new animation via start() automatically stops any previously running animation on the same AnimatedValue
* The start() callback receives { finished: false } when stop() is called
* InterpolationConfig inputRange must be in ascending order
* inputRange and outputRange must have the same length (minimum 2 elements)
* Color interpolation only works with "rgba(...)" format strings
* AnimatedValue is mutable -- it does not trigger React re-renders on its own. Use useAnimation hook for React integration.

---------
SEE ALSO
---------

- useanimation.txt -- React hook that wraps AnimatedValue
- easing.txt -- Easing functions for timing animations
- composite.txt -- Combining multiple animations
- usespring.txt -- Convenience hook for spring transitions


========================================
Composite Animations
========================================

Category: Animation | Difficulty: intermediate | Platforms: love2d, web, terminal, cc, nvim, hs, awesome

Combining animations with parallel, sequence, stagger, and loop.

---------
OVERVIEW
---------

ReactJIT provides four functions for composing multiple animations into coordinated sequences: parallel (run simultaneously), sequence (run one after another), stagger (start each with a delay offset), and loop (repeat an animation). All return an Animation object with start() and stop() methods, so they can themselves be composed into larger animations.

These functions operate on Animation objects (returned by AnimatedValue.timing() and AnimatedValue.spring()), not on AnimatedValues directly. This means you create individual animations first, then compose them.

---------
API / SYNTAX
---------

All functions are imported from '@reactjit/core':

  import { parallel, sequence, stagger, loop } from '@reactjit/core';

parallel(animations: Animation[]): Animation
  Starts all animations simultaneously. Completes when ALL animations finish.
  The callback receives { finished: true } only if every animation finished naturally.
  If any animation is stopped, finished is false.
  Passing an empty array completes immediately with { finished: true }.

sequence(animations: Animation[]): Animation
  Runs animations one after another in order. Each animation starts when the previous
  one finishes. If any animation in the sequence does not finish (stopped), the sequence
  halts and reports { finished: false }.

stagger(delay: number, animations: Animation[]): Animation
  Starts each animation with an incremental delay. The first starts immediately, the
  second after `delay` ms, the third after `2 * delay` ms, etc. Completes when all
  animations finish.

  delay: number    Milliseconds between each animation start.
  animations: Animation[]    The animations to stagger.

loop(animation: Animation, config?: { iterations?: number }): Animation
  Repeats a single animation.

  animation: Animation    The animation to repeat.
  config.iterations?: number
    Number of iterations. Default: -1 (infinite).
    -1 = infinite loop (must be stopped manually).
    Positive number = run that many times, then complete.

All return Animation:
  {
    start(callback?: (result: { finished: boolean }) => void): void
    stop(): void
  }

---------
EXAMPLES
---------

EXAMPLE: Parallel fade + slide
--------
import { useEffect } from 'react';
import { useAnimation, parallel, Box, Text } from '@reactjit/core';

function FadeSlideIn() {
  const [opacityAnim, opacity] = useAnimation(0);
  const [yAnim, y] = useAnimation(50);

  useEffect(() => {
    const anim = parallel([
      opacityAnim.timing({ toValue: 1, duration: 500 }),
      yAnim.timing({ toValue: 0, duration: 500 }),
    ]);
    anim.start();
    return () => anim.stop();
  }, [opacityAnim, yAnim]);

  return (
    <Box style={{
      width: 200,
      height: 80,
      opacity,
      backgroundColor: '#89b4fa',
      borderRadius: 8,
      transform: { translateY: y },
    }}>
      <Text style={{ fontSize: 18, color: '#1e1e2e' }}>Hello</Text>
    </Box>
  );
}
--------
Platforms: all

EXAMPLE: Sequential animation chain
--------
import { useEffect } from 'react';
import { useAnimation, sequence, Easing, Box } from '@reactjit/core';

function SequenceDemo() {
  const [xAnim, x] = useAnimation(0);
  const [yAnim, y] = useAnimation(0);
  const [scaleAnim, scale] = useAnimation(1);

  useEffect(() => {
    const anim = sequence([
      xAnim.timing({ toValue: 200, duration: 400, easing: Easing.easeOut }),
      yAnim.timing({ toValue: 200, duration: 400, easing: Easing.easeOut }),
      scaleAnim.spring({ toValue: 1.5, stiffness: 200, damping: 12 }),
    ]);
    anim.start();
    return () => anim.stop();
  }, [xAnim, yAnim, scaleAnim]);

  return (
    <Box style={{
      width: 50,
      height: 50,
      backgroundColor: '#f38ba8',
      borderRadius: 25,
      transform: { translateX: x, translateY: y, scaleX: scale, scaleY: scale },
    }} />
  );
}
--------
Platforms: all

EXAMPLE: Staggered list appearance
--------
import { useEffect } from 'react';
import { useAnimation, stagger, Box, Text } from '@reactjit/core';

function StaggeredList() {
  const [a1, v1] = useAnimation(0);
  const [a2, v2] = useAnimation(0);
  const [a3, v3] = useAnimation(0);
  const [a4, v4] = useAnimation(0);

  useEffect(() => {
    const anim = stagger(100, [
      a1.timing({ toValue: 1, duration: 300 }),
      a2.timing({ toValue: 1, duration: 300 }),
      a3.timing({ toValue: 1, duration: 300 }),
      a4.timing({ toValue: 1, duration: 300 }),
    ]);
    anim.start();
    return () => anim.stop();
  }, [a1, a2, a3, a4]);

  const items = [v1, v2, v3, v4];
  const labels = ['Item A', 'Item B', 'Item C', 'Item D'];
  const colors = ['#f38ba8', '#a6e3a1', '#89b4fa', '#f9e2af'];

  return (
    <Box style={{ width: '100%', height: '100%', padding: 20, gap: 8 }}>
      {items.map((opacity, i) => (
        <Box key={i} style={{
          width: '100%',
          height: 50,
          opacity,
          backgroundColor: colors[i],
          borderRadius: 8,
          padding: 12,
          transform: { translateX: (1 - opacity) * 30 },
        }}>
          <Text style={{ fontSize: 16, color: '#1e1e2e' }}>{labels[i]}</Text>
        </Box>
      ))}
    </Box>
  );
}
--------
Platforms: all

EXAMPLE: Infinite pulsing loop
--------
import { useEffect } from 'react';
import { useAnimation, loop, sequence, Box } from '@reactjit/core';

function PulsingDot() {
  const [scaleAnim, scale] = useAnimation(1);

  useEffect(() => {
    const pulse = loop(
      sequence([
        scaleAnim.timing({ toValue: 1.3, duration: 600 }),
        scaleAnim.timing({ toValue: 1.0, duration: 600 }),
      ]),
      { iterations: -1 }
    );
    pulse.start();
    return () => pulse.stop();
  }, [scaleAnim]);

  return (
    <Box style={{
      width: 40,
      height: 40,
      backgroundColor: '#a6e3a1',
      borderRadius: 20,
      transform: { scaleX: scale, scaleY: scale },
    }} />
  );
}
--------
Platforms: all

EXAMPLE: Loop with finite iterations
--------
import { useEffect } from 'react';
import { useAnimation, loop, Box } from '@reactjit/core';

function ShakeBox() {
  const [xAnim, x] = useAnimation(0);

  useEffect(() => {
    const shake = loop(
      sequence([
        xAnim.timing({ toValue: 10, duration: 50 }),
        xAnim.timing({ toValue: -10, duration: 50 }),
        xAnim.timing({ toValue: 0, duration: 50 }),
      ]),
      { iterations: 3 }
    );
    shake.start();
    return () => shake.stop();
  }, [xAnim]);

  return (
    <Box style={{
      width: 100,
      height: 100,
      backgroundColor: '#f38ba8',
      borderRadius: 8,
      transform: { translateX: x },
    }} />
  );
}
--------
Platforms: all

---------
CRITICAL RULES
---------

* All functions return Animation objects -- you must call .start() to begin
* Always call .stop() in effect cleanup to prevent memory leaks and runaway animations
* parallel completes when ALL animations finish -- one slow animation holds up the group
* sequence halts if any animation is stopped (reports finished: false)
* stagger delay is in milliseconds, applied cumulatively (0, delay, 2*delay, 3*delay, ...)
* loop with iterations: -1 runs forever -- you MUST stop it manually
* Composite animations can be nested: loop(sequence([parallel([...]), ...])) is valid
* Empty animation arrays complete immediately with { finished: true }

---------
SEE ALSO
---------

- animatedvalue.txt -- Creating individual Animation objects
- useanimation.txt -- React hook for animated values
- easing.txt -- Easing functions used with timing animations


========================================
Easing Functions
========================================

Category: Animation | Difficulty: intermediate | Platforms: love2d, web, terminal, cc, nvim, hs, awesome

Library of easing functions for timing-based animations.

---------
OVERVIEW
---------

ReactJIT provides a set of easing functions that control the rate of change during timing-based animations. Easing functions take a progress value t (0 to 1) and return a transformed value that determines the animation's velocity curve. They are used with AnimatedValue.timing() and useTransition().

The library includes standard easing curves (linear, easeIn, easeOut, easeInOut), a cubic bezier function for custom curves, a bounce effect, and an elastic overshoot effect. All easing functions conform to the EasingFunction type: (t: number) => number.

---------
API / SYNTAX
---------

Type:
  type EasingFunction = (t: number) => number

  Input: t is the linear progress from 0 (start) to 1 (end).
  Output: the eased progress value (may overshoot 0-1 range for elastic/bounce effects).

Static easing functions:

  Easing.linear
    (t) => t
    Constant velocity. No acceleration or deceleration.

  Easing.easeIn
    (t) => t * t
    Quadratic ease-in. Starts slow, accelerates.

  Easing.easeOut
    (t) => t * (2 - t)
    Quadratic ease-out. Starts fast, decelerates.

  Easing.easeInOut
    (t) => t < 0.5 ? 2 * t * t : -1 + (4 - 2 * t) * t
    Quadratic ease-in-out. Starts slow, accelerates, then decelerates.
    This is the default easing for AnimatedValue.timing().

  Easing.bounce
    Standard bounce curve. Simulates a ball bouncing and settling.
    Uses four quadratic segments with decreasing amplitude.

Factory easing functions (return an EasingFunction):

  Easing.bezier(x1: number, y1: number, x2: number, y2: number): EasingFunction
    Creates a cubic bezier easing function. The four parameters define two control points
    (P1 and P2) of a cubic bezier curve from (0,0) to (1,1). Uses Newton-Raphson iteration
    (8 iterations) to solve the cubic for the given t value.

    Common presets:
      Easing.bezier(0.25, 0.1, 0.25, 1)     // CSS ease
      Easing.bezier(0.42, 0, 1, 1)           // CSS ease-in
      Easing.bezier(0, 0, 0.58, 1)           // CSS ease-out
      Easing.bezier(0.42, 0, 0.58, 1)        // CSS ease-in-out
      Easing.bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1)         // Expo ease-out
      Easing.bezier(0.34, 1.56, 0.64, 1)     // Back ease-out (overshoot)

  Easing.elastic(bounciness?: number): EasingFunction
    Creates an elastic easing that overshoots the target and oscillates before settling.
    Default bounciness: 1. Higher values = more oscillation. Lower values = less overshoot.
    Uses exponential decay with sinusoidal oscillation.

---------
EXAMPLES
---------

EXAMPLE: Different easing curves
--------
import { useEffect } from 'react';
import { useAnimation, Easing, Box } from '@reactjit/core';

function EasingDemo() {
  const [linearAnim, linearX] = useAnimation(0);
  const [easeInAnim, easeInX] = useAnimation(0);
  const [easeOutAnim, easeOutX] = useAnimation(0);
  const [bounceAnim, bounceX] = useAnimation(0);

  useEffect(() => {
    linearAnim.timing({ toValue: 300, duration: 1000, easing: Easing.linear }).start();
    easeInAnim.timing({ toValue: 300, duration: 1000, easing: Easing.easeIn }).start();
    easeOutAnim.timing({ toValue: 300, duration: 1000, easing: Easing.easeOut }).start();
    bounceAnim.timing({ toValue: 300, duration: 1000, easing: Easing.bounce }).start();
  }, [linearAnim, easeInAnim, easeOutAnim, bounceAnim]);

  return (
    <Box style={{ width: '100%', height: '100%', padding: 20, gap: 16 }}>
      <Box style={{ width: 40, height: 40, backgroundColor: '#89b4fa', transform: { translateX: linearX } }} />
      <Box style={{ width: 40, height: 40, backgroundColor: '#a6e3a1', transform: { translateX: easeInX } }} />
      <Box style={{ width: 40, height: 40, backgroundColor: '#f38ba8', transform: { translateX: easeOutX } }} />
      <Box style={{ width: 40, height: 40, backgroundColor: '#f9e2af', transform: { translateX: bounceX } }} />
    </Box>
  );
}
--------
Platforms: all

EXAMPLE: Custom bezier curve
--------
import { useEffect } from 'react';
import { useAnimation, Easing, Box } from '@reactjit/core';

function CustomEase() {
  const [anim, value] = useAnimation(0);

  useEffect(() => {
    anim.timing({
      toValue: 1,
      duration: 800,
      easing: Easing.bezier(0.34, 1.56, 0.64, 1), // overshoot then settle
    }).start();
  }, [anim]);

  return (
    <Box style={{
      width: 100,
      height: 100,
      backgroundColor: '#cba6f7',
      transform: { scaleX: value, scaleY: value },
    }} />
  );
}
--------
Platforms: all

EXAMPLE: Elastic animation
--------
import { useEffect } from 'react';
import { useAnimation, Easing, Box } from '@reactjit/core';

function ElasticBox() {
  const [anim, value] = useAnimation(0);

  useEffect(() => {
    anim.timing({
      toValue: 200,
      duration: 1200,
      easing: Easing.elastic(1.5),
    }).start();
  }, [anim]);

  return (
    <Box style={{
      width: 60,
      height: 60,
      backgroundColor: '#f38ba8',
      borderRadius: 30,
      transform: { translateX: value },
    }} />
  );
}
--------
Platforms: all

---------
CRITICAL RULES
---------

* Easing functions must accept 0 and return 0, and accept 1 and return 1
* Easing.easeInOut is the default when no easing is specified in timing()
* Easing.bezier uses Newton-Raphson iteration (8 iterations) -- extremely fast but not exact for extreme curves
* Easing.elastic and Easing.bounce may produce values outside the 0-1 range (overshoot)
* Easing.elastic(0) will produce extreme oscillation -- use values >= 0.001
* All easing functions are stateless and can be shared across animations

---------
SEE ALSO
---------

- animatedvalue.txt -- Using easing with AnimatedValue.timing()
- usetransition.txt -- Using easing with useTransition
- composite.txt -- Combining eased animations


========================================
useAnimation
========================================

Category: Animation | Difficulty: intermediate | Platforms: love2d, web, terminal, cc, nvim, hs, awesome

Primary React hook for consuming animated values with automatic re-rendering.

---------
OVERVIEW
---------

useAnimation is the primary hook for using animated values in React components. It creates an AnimatedValue instance (once, on first render), subscribes to its value changes, and triggers component re-renders when the value updates. Value updates are batched to animation frames so the component re-renders at most once per frame, even if the animated value changes multiple times.

The hook returns a tuple of [AnimatedValue, currentValue]. Use the AnimatedValue to create and start animations (.timing(), .spring()). Use currentValue in your JSX to render the animated style properties.

---------
API / SYNTAX
---------

Signature:
  function useAnimation(initialValue: number): [AnimatedValue, number]

Parameters:
  initialValue: number
    The initial numeric value of the animated value.

Returns:
  [animatedValue, currentValue]

  animatedValue: AnimatedValue
    The AnimatedValue instance. Stable reference across re-renders.
    Use .timing(config) or .spring(config) to create animations, then call .start().
    Use .setValue(n) to set immediately.
    Use .interpolate(config) to map to other ranges.

  currentValue: number
    The current numeric value, updated each animation frame. Use this in your JSX
    for style props like opacity, width, translateX, etc.

Lifecycle:
  - AnimatedValue is created once (stored in a ref)
  - A listener is registered on mount to batch value changes into React state updates
  - On unmount, the listener is removed and any active animation is stopped

---------
EXAMPLES
---------

EXAMPLE: Opacity fade-in
--------
import { useEffect } from 'react';
import { useAnimation, Box, Text } from '@reactjit/core';

function FadeIn() {
  const [opacityAnim, opacity] = useAnimation(0);

  useEffect(() => {
    opacityAnim.timing({ toValue: 1, duration: 800 }).start();
  }, [opacityAnim]);

  return (
    <Box style={{ width: '100%', height: '100%', opacity }}>
      <Text style={{ fontSize: 24, color: '#cdd6f4' }}>Faded in!</Text>
    </Box>
  );
}
--------
Platforms: all

EXAMPLE: Spring press effect
--------
import { useState, useEffect } from 'react';
import { useAnimation, Box } from '@reactjit/core';

function PressableBox() {
  const [scaleAnim, scale] = useAnimation(1);
  const [isPressed, setIsPressed] = useState(false);

  useEffect(() => {
    scaleAnim.spring({
      toValue: isPressed ? 0.95 : 1.0,
      stiffness: 300,
      damping: 20,
    }).start();
  }, [isPressed, scaleAnim]);

  return (
    <Box
      style={{
        width: 200,
        height: 80,
        backgroundColor: '#a6e3a1',
        borderRadius: 12,
        transform: { scaleX: scale, scaleY: scale },
      }}
      onClick={() => setIsPressed(!isPressed)}
    >
      <Text style={{ fontSize: 18, color: '#1e1e2e' }}>Press me</Text>
    </Box>
  );
}
--------
Platforms: all

EXAMPLE: Continuous rotation
--------
import { useEffect } from 'react';
import { useAnimation, loop, Easing, Box } from '@reactjit/core';

function Spinner() {
  const [rotAnim, rotation] = useAnimation(0);

  useEffect(() => {
    const spin = loop(
      rotAnim.timing({ toValue: 360, duration: 1000, easing: Easing.linear }),
      { iterations: -1 }
    );
    spin.start();
    return () => spin.stop();
  }, [rotAnim]);

  return (
    <Box style={{
      width: 50,
      height: 50,
      backgroundColor: '#89b4fa',
      borderRadius: 25,
      transform: { rotate: rotation },
    }} />
  );
}
--------
Platforms: all

---------
CRITICAL RULES
---------

* The AnimatedValue instance is created once and stored in a ref -- it is a stable reference
* The currentValue triggers a React re-render when it changes (batched to animation frames)
* On unmount, active animations are stopped and the listener is removed
* Do not create AnimatedValue instances manually for React components -- use this hook
* The hook does not clean up animations started in effects -- you must stop them in effect cleanup

---------
SEE ALSO
---------

- animatedvalue.txt -- AnimatedValue class details
- usespring.txt -- Convenience hook for automatic spring transitions
- usetransition.txt -- Convenience hook for automatic timing transitions
- composite.txt -- Combining animations (parallel, sequence, etc.)


========================================
useSpring
========================================

Category: Animation | Difficulty: beginner | Platforms: love2d, web, terminal, cc, nvim, hs, awesome

Convenience hook that automatically springs to a target value when it changes.

---------
OVERVIEW
---------

useSpring is a convenience hook that automatically starts a spring animation whenever its target value changes. It is the simplest way to add spring-based motion to a component -- you provide the target value and optional spring configuration, and the hook returns the current interpolated value that updates each frame.

The hook skips animation on the first render (the value starts at the target immediately). On subsequent renders where the target changes, a spring animation is started to transition from the current value to the new target.

---------
API / SYNTAX
---------

Signature:
  function useSpring(targetValue: number, config?: { stiffness?: number; damping?: number; mass?: number }): number

Parameters:
  targetValue: number
    The value to spring towards. When this changes, a new spring animation starts.

  config?: object (optional)
    stiffness?: number   Spring stiffness. Higher = snappier. Default: 100
    damping?: number     Damping coefficient. Higher = less oscillation. Default: 10
    mass?: number        Mass of the object. Higher = slower/heavier. Default: 1

Returns:
  number
    The current interpolated value. Updates each animation frame, triggering re-render.

Spring behavior:
  - stiffness controls how strongly the spring pulls toward the target
  - damping controls how quickly oscillation settles
  - mass controls how heavy the animated object feels
  - Low damping + high stiffness = bouncy
  - High damping + low stiffness = sluggish
  - The animation uses Verlet integration with clamped dt (max 64ms) to avoid instability

---------
EXAMPLES
---------

EXAMPLE: Toggle width
--------
import { useState } from 'react';
import { useSpring, Box, Text } from '@reactjit/core';

function ExpandableBox() {
  const [expanded, setExpanded] = useState(false);
  const width = useSpring(expanded ? 300 : 100);

  return (
