marginLeft?: number | string
  Left outer space. Overrides margin for the left side.

marginRight?: number | string
  Right outer space. Overrides margin for the right side.

marginTop?: number | string
  Top outer space. Overrides margin for the top side.

marginBottom?: number | string
  Bottom outer space. Overrides margin for the bottom side.

--- VISUAL ---

backgroundColor?: Color
  Fill color for Box/View nodes. Hex string, rgba string, or [r,g,b,a] array.
  Default: transparent

borderRadius?: number
  Uniform corner radius in pixels. Applied to all four corners.
  Default: 0

borderTopLeftRadius?: number
  Per-corner override. Takes precedence over borderRadius.

borderTopRightRadius?: number
  Per-corner override. Takes precedence over borderRadius.

borderBottomLeftRadius?: number
  Per-corner override. Takes precedence over borderRadius.

borderBottomRightRadius?: number
  Per-corner override. Takes precedence over borderRadius.

borderWidth?: number
  Uniform border stroke width on all sides.
  Default: 0

borderTopWidth?: number
  Per-side border width. Overrides borderWidth for the top side.

borderRightWidth?: number
  Per-side border width. Overrides borderWidth for the right side.

borderBottomWidth?: number
  Per-side border width. Overrides borderWidth for the bottom side.

borderLeftWidth?: number
  Per-side border width. Overrides borderWidth for the left side.

borderColor?: Color
  Uniform border color on all sides.
  Default: gray (0.5, 0.5, 0.5, 1)

borderTopColor?: Color
  Per-side border color. Overrides borderColor for the top side.

borderRightColor?: Color
  Per-side border color. Overrides borderColor for the right side.

borderBottomColor?: Color
  Per-side border color. Overrides borderColor for the bottom side.

borderLeftColor?: Color
  Per-side border color. Overrides borderColor for the left side.

overflow?: 'visible' | 'hidden' | 'scroll'
  'visible': Content can extend beyond bounds (default).
  'hidden': Content clipped to bounds (stencil for rounded, scissor for rectangular).
  'scroll': Scrollable container with clipping and scroll indicators.
  Default: 'visible'

opacity?: number
  Node opacity (0-1). Multiplies with inherited opacity from parent chain.
  0 = fully transparent. 1 = fully opaque.
  Default: 1

zIndex?: number
  Paint order within the parent's children. Higher values paint on top.
  Does NOT affect layout, only paint order.
  Default: 0

scrollX?: number
  Horizontal scroll position for scroll containers (controlled mode).

scrollY?: number
  Vertical scroll position for scroll containers (controlled mode).

--- SHADOW ---

shadowColor?: Color
  Box shadow color. Shadow only renders when shadowBlur > 0.

shadowOffsetX?: number
  Horizontal shadow offset in pixels.
  Default: 0

shadowOffsetY?: number
  Vertical shadow offset in pixels.
  Default: 0

shadowBlur?: number
  Shadow blur radius. Simulated by rendering multiple fading rectangles.
  Capped at 10 blur steps for performance.
  Default: 0

--- GRADIENT ---

backgroundGradient?: { direction, colors }
  Linear gradient background. Takes precedence over backgroundColor.
  direction: 'horizontal' | 'vertical' | 'diagonal'
  colors: [Color, Color] -- start and end colors
  Rendered via Love2D mesh API on Love2D, CSS linear-gradient on web.

--- TRANSFORM ---

transform?: { translateX?, translateY?, rotate?, scaleX?, scaleY?, skewX?, skewY?, originX?, originY? }
  Visual transform. Does NOT affect layout or hit testing.
  translateX/Y: Pixel offset.
  rotate: Degrees (not radians).
  scaleX/Y: Scale factor (1 = no scale).
  skewX/Y: Skew angle in degrees.
  originX/Y: Transform origin as 0-1 fraction (0.5 = center, default).

--- TEXT ---

color?: Color
  Text color. Applied to Text and __TEXT__ nodes.
  Inherited by __TEXT__ children from parent Text nodes.
  Default: white (1, 1, 1, 1)

fontSize?: number
  Font size in pixels. REQUIRED on all Text nodes (enforced by linter).
  Inherited by __TEXT__ children from parent Text nodes.
  Default: 14 (but the linter requires explicit values)

fontFamily?: string
  Path to a .ttf or .otf font file. Relative to the Love2D filesystem root.
  Inherited by __TEXT__ children from parent Text nodes.
  Default: Love2D default font

fontWeight?: 'normal' | 'bold' | number
  Font weight. 'bold' triggers faux-bold rendering (double draw with offset).
  Default: 'normal'

textAlign?: 'left' | 'center' | 'right'
  Horizontal text alignment within the node's computed width.
  Default: 'left'

textOverflow?: 'clip' | 'ellipsis'
  How to handle text that overflows the node's width.
  'ellipsis': Truncate with "..." at the end.
  Default: 'clip'

textDecorationLine?: 'none' | 'underline' | 'line-through'
  Text decoration line style.
  Default: 'none'

lineHeight?: number
  Line height in pixels. Overrides the font's natural line height.
  When set, text is rendered line-by-line with this spacing.
  Default: Font's natural line height

letterSpacing?: number
  Extra space between characters in pixels.
  WARNING: Activates character-by-character rendering. Expensive on large text.
  Default: 0

--- TEXT SHADOW ---

textShadowColor?: Color
  Text shadow color. Shadow only renders when this is set.

textShadowOffsetX?: number
  Horizontal text shadow offset.
  Default: 0

textShadowOffsetY?: number
  Vertical text shadow offset.
  Default: 0

--- IMAGE ---

objectFit?: 'fill' | 'contain' | 'cover' | 'none'
  How the image scales within its bounds.
  'fill': Stretch to fill (default). 'contain': Fit inside, letterbox.
  'cover': Fill, crop overflow. 'none': No scaling, center.
  Default: 'fill'

--- POSITIONING ---

position?: 'relative' | 'absolute'
  'relative': Normal flow (default).
  'absolute': Positioned relative to the nearest positioned ancestor.
  Default: 'relative'

top?: number | string
  Offset from the top edge (absolute positioning).

bottom?: number | string
  Offset from the bottom edge (absolute positioning).

left?: number | string
  Offset from the left edge (absolute positioning).

right?: number | string
  Offset from the right edge (absolute positioning).

--- VISIBILITY ---

visibility?: 'visible' | 'hidden'
  'visible': Normal rendering (default).
  'hidden': Node is not painted, but still occupies layout space.
  Unlike display:'none', children of a hidden node still paint.
  Default: 'visible'

--- OUTLINE ---

outlineColor?: Color
  Outline color. Drawn outside the border box.
  Default: white (1, 1, 1, 1) or currentColor on web

outlineWidth?: number
  Outline stroke width.
  Default: 0

outlineOffset?: number
  Gap between the border box and the outline.
  Default: 0

--- TRANSITION ---

transition?: { [property: string]: { duration?, easing?, delay? } }
  CSS-style transitions. Lua-side: JS declares transition targets, Lua interpolates.
  Web-side: Mapped directly to CSS transition property.

  Per-property: transition: { backgroundColor: { duration: 300, easing: 'easeInOut' } }
  All properties: transition: { all: { duration: 300 } }

  duration: number in milliseconds (default: 300)
  easing: 'linear' | 'easeIn' | 'easeOut' | 'easeInOut' | 'bounce' | 'elastic' (default: 'easeInOut')
  delay: number in milliseconds (default: 0)

--- ANIMATION ---

animation?: { keyframes, duration?, easing?, iterations?, direction?, fillMode?, delay?, playState? }
  CSS-style keyframe animations. Runs on the Lua side.

  keyframes: { [percentage: number]: Partial<Style> }
    Percentage keys from 0 to 100. Each value is a partial Style object.
    Example: { 0: { opacity: 0 }, 100: { opacity: 1 } }

  duration: number in milliseconds (default: 300)
  easing: string easing function name (default: 'linear')
  iterations: number, -1 = infinite (default: 1)
  direction: 'normal' | 'reverse' | 'alternate' | 'alternate-reverse' (default: 'normal')
  fillMode: 'none' | 'forwards' | 'backwards' | 'both' (default: 'none')
  delay: number in milliseconds (default: 0)
  playState: 'running' | 'paused' (default: 'running')

---------
SEE ALSO
---------

- types.txt - TypeScript type definitions
- 04-layout-system/flexbox.txt - Flexbox user guide
- 04-layout-system/sizing.txt - Sizing model guide
- 04-layout-system/spacing.txt - Spacing (padding/margin) guide
- 11-troubleshooting/common-errors.txt - Style-related errors


========================================
Telegram Bot API
========================================

Category: API Reference | Difficulty: beginner | Platforms: love2d, web, terminal

Hooks for fetching bot identity, polling updates, and sending messages or photos through a Telegram bot.

---------
OVERVIEW
---------

The Telegram hooks wrap the official Telegram Bot API. They cover three concerns: identifying the bot (`useTelegramBot`), polling for incoming messages and callback queries (`useTelegramUpdates`), and sending messages or photos imperatively (`useTelegramSend`).

Auth: Create a bot via @BotFather in Telegram. BotFather gives you a token string of the form `1234567890:ABCDEFGHIJabcdefghij`. Pass it as `botToken` to every hook. The token is embedded directly in the API URL path — treat it as a secret.

---------
API / SYNTAX
---------

--- useTelegramBot ---

Fetch the identity of the bot associated with the given token. Use this to confirm the token is valid and to get the bot's username and capability flags.

  import { useTelegramBot } from '@reactjit/apis/telegram';

  const result: APIResult<{ ok: boolean; result: TelegramBotInfo }> = useTelegramBot(
    botToken: string | null,
  );

Parameters:
  botToken  string | null   Telegram bot token from BotFather. Pass null to disable.

Returns: APIResult<{ ok: boolean; result: TelegramBotInfo }>
  data.ok      boolean
  data.result  TelegramBotInfo

TelegramBotInfo fields:
  id                            number
  is_bot                        boolean    Always true for bots
  first_name                    string     Bot display name
  username                      string     Bot username (without @)
  can_join_groups               boolean
  can_read_all_group_messages   boolean
  supports_inline_queries       boolean

--- useTelegramUpdates ---

Poll for incoming updates (messages, edited messages, callback queries) using Telegram's long-polling `getUpdates` method. Refetches on a configurable interval.

  import { useTelegramUpdates } from '@reactjit/apis/telegram';

  const result: APIResult<{ ok: boolean; result: TelegramUpdate[] }> = useTelegramUpdates(
    botToken: string | null,
    opts?:    { offset?: number; limit?: number; interval?: number },
  );

Parameters:
  botToken       string | null   Telegram bot token. Pass null to disable.
  opts.offset    number          Return only updates with update_id >= offset. Pass the last update_id + 1 to acknowledge processed updates.
  opts.limit     number          Maximum number of updates to return per poll. Defaults to 20. Max 100.
  opts.interval  number          Polling interval in milliseconds. Defaults to 5000 (5 seconds).

Returns: APIResult<{ ok: boolean; result: TelegramUpdate[] }>
  data.ok      boolean
  data.result  TelegramUpdate[]

TelegramUpdate fields:
  update_id        number                      Globally unique update ID. Use for offset tracking.
  message          TelegramMessage | undefined  A new incoming message
  edited_message   TelegramMessage | undefined  An edited message
  callback_query   object | undefined           A callback from an inline keyboard button

TelegramMessage fields:
  message_id           number
  from                 TelegramUser | undefined
  chat                 TelegramChat
  date                 number                   Unix timestamp
  text                 string | undefined
  photo                Array<{ file_id, width, height }> | undefined
  document             { file_id, file_name?, mime_type? } | undefined
  reply_to_message     TelegramMessage | undefined

TelegramChat fields:
  id          number
  type        'private' | 'group' | 'supergroup' | 'channel'
  title       string | undefined
  username    string | undefined
  first_name  string | undefined
  last_name   string | undefined

TelegramUser fields:
  id              number
  is_bot          boolean
  first_name      string
  last_name       string | undefined
  username        string | undefined
  language_code   string | undefined

--- useTelegramSend ---

Imperative mutation hook for sending messages and photos to any chat your bot has access to.

  import { useTelegramSend } from '@reactjit/apis/telegram';

  const sender = useTelegramSend(botToken: string | null);

Parameters:
  botToken  string | null   Telegram bot token. Pass null to disable (all methods reject with an error).

Returns: object
  sendMessage   (chatId, text, opts?) => Promise<any>
  sendPhoto     (chatId, photoUrl, opts?) => Promise<any>
  loading       boolean   True while any mutation request is in flight
  error         Error | null

sendMessage signature:
  sendMessage(
    chatId:            number | string,
    text:              string,
    opts?: {
      parseMode?:         'HTML' | 'Markdown' | 'MarkdownV2';
      replyToMessageId?:  number;
    },
  ): Promise<any>

  chatId              number | string   Telegram chat ID or @username
  text                string            Message text
  opts.parseMode      string            Enable HTML or Markdown formatting in text
  opts.replyToMessageId  number         Reply to a specific message by ID

sendPhoto signature:
  sendPhoto(
    chatId:   number | string,
    photoUrl: string,
    opts?: {
      caption?: string;
    },
  ): Promise<any>

  chatId      number | string   Target chat ID
  photoUrl    string            Public URL of the image to send
  opts.caption  string          Optional caption text shown below the photo

---------
SEE ALSO
---------

- hue — another token-in-URL-path API with similar auth pattern
- useAPIMutation — low-level imperative mutation hook used internally by useTelegramSend


========================================
TMDB API Hooks
========================================

Category: API Reference | Difficulty: beginner | Platforms: love2d, web, terminal

Hooks for The Movie Database API — trending, search, movie details, series details, and popular titles.

---------
OVERVIEW
---------

The TMDB hooks wrap The Movie Database (TMDB) REST API v3. Authentication uses an API key passed as a query parameter — get one at https://www.themoviedb.org/settings/api. Pass the key as the first argument to every hook. A null key skips the request.

All hooks return APIResult<T> with data, loading, error, and refetch. Paginated endpoints return TMDBPaginated<T> which includes the current page, total pages, and total results.

The package also exports a tmdbImage() utility for constructing full image URLs from the partial paths the API returns.

---------
API / SYNTAX
---------

--- useTMDBTrending ---

Fetch trending movies or TV shows for today or this week.

  import { useTMDBTrending } from '@reactjit/apis/tmdb';

  const result: APIResult<TMDBPaginated<TMDBMovie>> = useTMDBTrending(apiKey, opts?);

Parameters:
  apiKey   string | null
  opts     object (optional)
    mediaType   'movie' | 'tv' | 'all'    Default: 'movie'
    timeWindow  'day' | 'week'            Default: 'week'

Returns: APIResult<TMDBPaginated<TMDBMovie>>
  data.page           number
  data.results        TMDBMovie[]
  data.total_pages    number
  data.total_results  number

--- useTMDBSearch ---

Search the TMDB catalog by text query. Supports movies, TV shows, or a combined multi-search.

  import { useTMDBSearch } from '@reactjit/apis/tmdb';

  const result: APIResult<TMDBPaginated<TMDBMovie & TMDBSeries>> = useTMDBSearch(apiKey, query, opts?);

Parameters:
  apiKey   string | null
  query    string | null   Pass null to skip (e.g. while the user is typing).
  opts     object (optional)
    type   'movie' | 'tv' | 'multi'   Default: 'multi'
    page   number                      Default: none (API default: 1)

Returns: APIResult<TMDBPaginated<TMDBMovie & TMDBSeries>>

--- useTMDBMovie ---

Fetch full details for a single movie, including runtime, budget, genres, and production companies.

  import { useTMDBMovie } from '@reactjit/apis/tmdb';

  const result: APIResult<TMDBMovieDetails> = useTMDBMovie(apiKey, movieId);

Parameters:
  apiKey    string | null
  movieId   number | null   TMDB movie ID. Pass null to skip.

Returns: APIResult<TMDBMovieDetails>

TMDBMovieDetails extends TMDBMovie and adds:
  runtime               number
  budget                number
  revenue               number
  tagline               string
  genres                Array<{ id: number; name: string }>
  production_companies  Array<{ id: number; name: string; logo_path: string | null }>
  imdb_id               string | null

--- useTMDBSeries ---

Fetch full details for a single TV series, including season count, episode count, and networks.

  import { useTMDBSeries } from '@reactjit/apis/tmdb';

  const result: APIResult<TMDBSeriesDetails> = useTMDBSeries(apiKey, seriesId);

Parameters:
  apiKey     string | null
  seriesId   number | null   TMDB series ID. Pass null to skip.

Returns: APIResult<TMDBSeriesDetails>

TMDBSeriesDetails extends TMDBSeries and adds:
  number_of_seasons    number
  number_of_episodes   number
  episode_run_time     number[]
  genres               Array<{ id: number; name: string }>
  status               string
  networks             Array<{ id: number; name: string; logo_path: string | null }>

--- useTMDBPopular ---

Fetch the current popular movies or TV shows.

  import { useTMDBPopular } from '@reactjit/apis/tmdb';

  const result: APIResult<TMDBPaginated<TMDBMovie>> = useTMDBPopular(apiKey, opts?);

Parameters:
  apiKey   string | null
  opts     object (optional)
    type   'movie' | 'tv'   Default: 'movie'
    page   number           Default: none (API default: 1)

Returns: APIResult<TMDBPaginated<TMDBMovie>>

--- tmdbImage ---

Construct a full image URL from the partial path returned by TMDB.

  import { tmdbImage } from '@reactjit/apis/tmdb';

  const url: string | null = tmdbImage(path, size?);

Parameters:
  path   string | null   The poster_path or backdrop_path value from any TMDB type.
  size   string          TMDB image size code. Default: 'w500'. Common: 'w92', 'w185', 'w342', 'w500', 'w780', 'original'.

Returns: string | null — null if path is null or empty.

---------
SEE ALSO
---------

- trakt — alternative watch history and media tracking (see packages/apis/src/trakt.ts)


========================================
Todoist Hooks
========================================

Category: API Reference | Difficulty: beginner | Platforms: love2d, web, terminal

Hooks for reading tasks, projects, sections, and labels from Todoist, plus imperative mutations for creating, completing, and deleting tasks.

---------
OVERVIEW
---------

The Todoist hooks in @reactjit/apis use the Todoist REST API v2. Authentication is a single Bearer token found under Settings > Integrations > Developer in the Todoist web app.

Read hooks (useTodoistTasks, useTodoistProjects, etc.) are reactive — they fetch on mount and expose a refetch() method. The useTodoistMutation hook returns imperative functions for write operations: creating tasks, marking them complete, reopening, updating, and deleting.

All read hooks accept null as the token argument to suppress fetching, which is the standard pattern for conditional data loading in ReactJIT.

---------
API / SYNTAX
---------

--- useTodoistTasks ---

Fetch tasks with optional filtering by project, label, or Todoist filter expression.

  import { useTodoistTasks } from '@reactjit/apis';

  const result: APIResult<TodoistTask[]> = useTodoistTasks(
    token: string | null,
    opts?: { projectId?: string; label?: string; filter?: string },
  );

Parameters:
  token  string | null   Todoist API token
  opts   object | undefined
    projectId  string    Filter to a specific project ID
    label      string    Filter to a specific label name
    filter     string    Todoist filter query string (e.g. 'today | overdue')

Returns: APIResult<TodoistTask[]>

TodoistTask fields:
  id             string
  content        string              Task title
  description    string              Additional notes
  is_completed   boolean
  priority       1 | 2 | 3 | 4      1 = normal, 4 = urgent
  due            object | null
    date         string              Due date in YYYY-MM-DD format
    string       string              Human-readable due string
    datetime     string | undefined  ISO 8601 if time is set
    recurring    boolean
  labels         string[]            Label names
  project_id     string
  section_id     string | undefined
  parent_id      string | null       ID of parent task if nested
  order          number              Sort order within project
  created_at     string              ISO 8601
  url            string              Direct link to the task

--- useTodoistProjects ---

Fetch all projects in the account.

  import { useTodoistProjects } from '@reactjit/apis';

  const result: APIResult<TodoistProject[]> = useTodoistProjects(
    token: string | null,
  );

Parameters:
  token  string | null  Todoist API token

Returns: APIResult<TodoistProject[]>

TodoistProject fields:
  id                string
  name              string
  color             string   Color name (e.g. 'berry_red', 'sky_blue')
  order             number
  is_favorite       boolean
  is_inbox_project  boolean
  comment_count     number
  url               string

--- useTodoistSections ---

Fetch sections within a project.

  import { useTodoistSections } from '@reactjit/apis';

  const result: APIResult<TodoistSection[]> = useTodoistSections(
    token: string | null,
    projectId: string | null,
  );

Parameters:
  token      string | null  Todoist API token
  projectId  string | null  Project ID to fetch sections for

Returns: APIResult<TodoistSection[]>

TodoistSection fields:
  id          string
  name        string
  project_id  string
  order       number

--- useTodoistLabels ---

Fetch all personal labels in the account.

  import { useTodoistLabels } from '@reactjit/apis';

  const result: APIResult<TodoistLabel[]> = useTodoistLabels(
    token: string | null,
  );

Parameters:
  token  string | null  Todoist API token

Returns: APIResult<TodoistLabel[]>

TodoistLabel fields:
  id           string
  name         string
  color        string
  order        number
  is_favorite  boolean

--- useTodoistComments ---

Fetch comments for a task or project.

  import { useTodoistComments } from '@reactjit/apis';

  const result: APIResult<TodoistComment[]> = useTodoistComments(
    token: string | null,
    opts: { taskId?: string; projectId?: string },
  );

Parameters:
  token  string | null   Todoist API token
  opts   object          Exactly one of taskId or projectId must be provided
    taskId     string    Fetch comments for this task
    projectId  string    Fetch comments for this project

Returns: APIResult<TodoistComment[]>

TodoistComment fields:
  id          string
  content     string
  posted_at   string              ISO 8601
  task_id     string | undefined
  project_id  string | undefined

--- useTodoistMutation ---

Get imperative operations for creating and managing tasks.

  import { useTodoistMutation } from '@reactjit/apis';

  const mutation = useTodoistMutation(token: string | null);

Parameters:
  token  string | null  Todoist API token

Returns: object
  createTask    (content: string, opts?) => Promise<any>
                Create a new task. opts fields:
                  projectId   string    Target project ID (defaults to Inbox)
                  priority    number    1–4 where 4 is urgent
                  dueString   string    Natural language due date (e.g. 'tomorrow at 9am')
                  labels      string[]  Label names to apply
  completeTask  (taskId: string) => Promise<any>
                Mark a task complete (close it).
  reopenTask    (taskId: string) => Promise<any>
                Reopen a previously completed task.
  deleteTask    (taskId: string) => Promise<any>
                Permanently delete a task.
  updateTask    (taskId: string, updates: Partial<Pick<TodoistTask, 'content' | 'description' | 'priority' | 'labels'>>) => Promise<any>
                Update task fields. Only provided keys are changed.
  loading       boolean        True while any mutation is in flight
  error         Error | null   Last error, or null

---------
SEE ALSO
---------

- useNotionDatabase — read and write rows in Notion databases
- useGoogleCalendarEvents — upcoming calendar events from Google Calendar


========================================
Trakt Hooks
========================================

Category: API Reference | Difficulty: beginner | Platforms: love2d, web, terminal

Hooks for watch history, watchlists, live now-watching status, trending content, and user statistics from Trakt.tv.

---------
OVERVIEW
---------

The Trakt hooks in @reactjit/apis connect to the Trakt.tv REST API. Most endpoints require a Client ID (public) and a user OAuth token (private). The now-watching hook is especially useful for building media dashboards because it reflects the current scrobble state in near real-time.

Credentials: create an application at https://trakt.tv/oauth/applications to get a Client ID. User tokens are obtained through OAuth — store them in your environment or config file.

All hooks return null data until credentials are provided. Passing null for clientId or token suppresses the fetch and is the recommended way to conditionally disable a hook without breaking the component tree.

---------
API / SYNTAX
---------

--- useTraktWatching ---

Fetch what a user is currently watching. Returns null data when nothing is playing. Supports polling.

  import { useTraktWatching } from '@reactjit/apis';

  const result: APIResult<TraktWatching | null> = useTraktWatching(
    clientId: string | null,
    token: string | null,
    username?: string,
    opts?: { interval?: number },
  );

Parameters:
  clientId  string | null   Trakt application Client ID
  token     string | null   OAuth Bearer token for the user
  username  string          Trakt username (default: 'me' — the authenticated user)
  opts      object | undefined
    interval  number        Polling interval in milliseconds (default: 30000)

Returns: APIResult<TraktWatching | null>
  data is null when nothing is currently playing.

TraktWatching fields:
  expires_at   string              ISO 8601 datetime when the scrobble expires
  started_at   string              ISO 8601 datetime when playback started
  action       string              Typically 'scrobble'
  type         'movie' | 'episode'
  movie        TraktMovie | undefined   Present when type is 'movie'
  show         TraktShow | undefined    Present when type is 'episode'
  episode      TraktEpisode | undefined Present when type is 'episode'

--- useTraktHistory ---

Fetch a user's watch history, optionally filtered by media type.

  import { useTraktHistory } from '@reactjit/apis';

  const result: APIResult<TraktHistoryItem[]> = useTraktHistory(
    clientId: string | null,
    token: string | null,
    opts?: { username?: string; type?: 'movies' | 'shows' | 'episodes'; limit?: number },
  );

Parameters:
  clientId  string | null   Trakt Client ID
  token     string | null   OAuth Bearer token
  opts      object | undefined
    username  string        Trakt username (default: 'me')
    type      string        Filter to 'movies', 'shows', or 'episodes'
    limit     number        Max items to return (default: 20)

Returns: APIResult<TraktHistoryItem[]>

TraktHistoryItem fields:
  id          number
  watched_at  string              ISO 8601 datetime
  action      string              'watch' or 'scrobble'
  type        'movie' | 'episode'
  movie       TraktMovie | undefined
  show        TraktShow | undefined
  episode     TraktEpisode | undefined

--- useTraktWatchlist ---

Fetch a user's watchlist, optionally filtered to movies or shows only.

  import { useTraktWatchlist } from '@reactjit/apis';

  const result: APIResult<TraktWatchlistItem[]> = useTraktWatchlist(
    clientId: string | null,
    token: string | null,
    opts?: { username?: string; type?: 'movies' | 'shows' },
  );

Parameters:
  clientId  string | null   Trakt Client ID
  token     string | null   OAuth Bearer token
  opts      object | undefined
    username  string        Trakt username (default: 'me')
    type      string        'movies' or 'shows' — omit for both

Returns: APIResult<TraktWatchlistItem[]>

TraktWatchlistItem fields:
  listed_at  string            ISO 8601 datetime added to list
  type       'movie' | 'show'
  movie      TraktMovie | undefined
  show       TraktShow | undefined

--- useTraktTrending ---

Fetch currently trending movies or shows across all Trakt users. Does not require a user token.

  import { useTraktTrending } from '@reactjit/apis';

  const result: APIResult<Array<{ watchers: number; movie?: TraktMovie; show?: TraktShow }>> = useTraktTrending(
    clientId: string | null,
    type?: 'movies' | 'shows',
    opts?: { limit?: number },
  );

Parameters:
  clientId  string | null   Trakt Client ID
  type      string          'movies' or 'shows' (default: 'movies')
  opts      object | undefined
    limit   number          Max items to return (default: 10)

Returns: APIResult<Array<{ watchers: number; movie?: TraktMovie; show?: TraktShow }>>
  watchers — number of Trakt users watching this item right now

--- useTraktStats ---

Fetch aggregate statistics for a user: total plays, time watched, shows and episodes seen.

  import { useTraktStats } from '@reactjit/apis';

  const result: APIResult<TraktStats> = useTraktStats(
    clientId: string | null,
    token: string | null,
    username?: string,
  );

Parameters:
  clientId  string | null   Trakt Client ID
  token     string | null   OAuth Bearer token
  username  string          Trakt username (default: 'me')

Returns: APIResult<TraktStats>

TraktStats fields:
  movies    { plays: number; watched: number; minutes: number }
  shows     { watched: number; collected: number }
  episodes  { plays: number; watched: number; minutes: number }

--- Shared Types ---

TraktMovie fields:
  title  string
  year   number
  ids    { trakt: number; slug: string; imdb?: string; tmdb?: number }

TraktShow fields:
  title  string
  year   number
  ids    { trakt: number; slug: string; imdb?: string; tmdb?: number; tvdb?: number }

TraktEpisode fields:
  season  number
  number  number
  title   string
  ids     { trakt: number; tvdb?: number; imdb?: string; tmdb?: number }

---------
SEE ALSO
---------

- usePlexSessions — active Plex playback sessions
- useTMDB — movie and show metadata, posters, and ratings


========================================
TypeScript Types Reference
========================================

Category: API Reference | Difficulty: intermediate | Platforms: love2d, web, terminal, cc, nvim, hs, awesome

Complete TypeScript type definitions for all ReactJIT interfaces

---------
OVERVIEW
---------

This reference lists all TypeScript types exported from @reactjit/core (packages/shared). Types are organized by category: core types, primitive component props, higher-level component props, animation types, and utility types. All types are importable from '@reactjit/core'.

---------
API / SYNTAX
---------

--- CORE TYPES ---

Color = string | [number, number, number, number?]
  CSS string ("#ff0000", "rgba(255,0,0,0.5)") or Love2D array [r, g, b, a] (0-1 range).
  The alpha channel is optional and defaults to 1.

Style
  Full style interface. See style-properties.txt for every property.
  All properties are optional.

RendererMode = 'web' | 'native'
  Which rendering path the primitives use.
  'web': DOM elements (div/span/img).
  'native': Host elements for react-reconciler (View/Text/Image).

--- EVENT TYPE ---

LoveEvent
  type: string
  targetId?: number
  x?: number
  y?: number
  button?: number
  key?: string
  scancode?: string
  isRepeat?: boolean
  text?: string
  deltaX?: number
  deltaY?: number
  touchId?: number | string
  dx?: number
  dy?: number
  pressure?: number
  gamepadButton?: string
  axis?: string
  axisValue?: number
  joystickId?: number
  startX?: number
  startY?: number
  totalDeltaX?: number
  totalDeltaY?: number
  bubblePath?: number[]
  currentTarget?: number
  stopPropagation?: () => void

--- PRIMITIVE COMPONENT PROPS ---

  style?: Style
  hoverStyle?: Style                          -- Lua-side hover overlay (0-frame latency)
  activeStyle?: Style                         -- Lua-side active/pressed overlay
  onClick?: (event: LoveEvent) => void
  onRelease?: (event: LoveEvent) => void
  onPointerEnter?: (event: LoveEvent) => void
  onPointerLeave?: (event: LoveEvent) => void
  onKeyDown?: (event: LoveEvent) => void
  onKeyUp?: (event: LoveEvent) => void
  onTextInput?: (event: LoveEvent) => void
  onWheel?: (event: LoveEvent) => void
  onTouchStart?: (event: LoveEvent) => void
  onTouchEnd?: (event: LoveEvent) => void
  onTouchMove?: (event: LoveEvent) => void
  onGamepadPress?: (event: LoveEvent) => void
  onGamepadRelease?: (event: LoveEvent) => void
  onGamepadAxis?: (event: LoveEvent) => void
  onDragStart?: (event: LoveEvent) => void
  onDrag?: (event: LoveEvent) => void
  onDragEnd?: (event: LoveEvent) => void
  children?: React.ReactNode
  key?: string | number

TextProps

  numberOfLines?: number                      -- Max visible lines (truncation)
  onKeyDown?: (event: LoveEvent) => void
  onKeyUp?: (event: LoveEvent) => void
